name: Cucumber Agua Fresca
title: CUCUMBER AGUA FRESCA
description: A deeply refreshing, cooling elixir that harnesses the watery essence of cucumber and the bright, volatile acidity of lime to soothe the spirit. Perfect for intense summer heat, this beverage restores thermodynamic equilibrium through its cooling properties.
ingredients: 6 English cucumbers with skin (12 ounces each), seeded and cut into 1-inch pieces
instructions: Combine cucumbers, lime juice, mint, water, and agave in blender and puree until smooth.
Strain puree through sieve and serve in glasses with slice of lime.

name: Pomegranate, Blueberry, and Ginger Elixir
title: POMEGRANATE, BLUEBERRY, AND GINGER ELIXIR
description: A vibrant, antioxidant-rich tonic balancing the earthy sweetness of dark berries with the fiery, kinetic bite of fresh ginger. This intensely restorative elixir promotes energetic circulation and grounds the body while stimulating the senses.
instructions: Combine pomegranate juice, blueberries, ginger juice, and agave in VitaMix and puree. Do not strain. Serve over ice.

name: Hibiscus-Mint Cooler
title: HIBISCUS-MINT COOLER
description: A visually striking, ruby-red infusion uniting the tart, fiery nature of hibiscus flowers with the crisp, ethereal cooling effect of fresh mint. This harmonizing cooler acts as a powerful botanical reset during transitional seasons.
instructions: Combine water and hibiscus tea in stockpot. Bring to boil, turn off tea, and steep for 20 minutes. Chill tea in ice bath.
Combine agave and mint leaves in suribachi with 2 cups of cooled tea. Muddle (crush) mint leaves with agave and tea using surikogi (pestle) until well combined.
Add mint mixture to remaining tea and strain.
Serve over ice and garnish with mint sprig.

name: Fresh Herb Dressing
title: FRESH HERB DRESSING
description: An aromatic, emulsified suspension that merges the earthy groundedness of mustard and shallots with the bright, elevating airiness of fresh basil, tarragon, and dill. This versatile dressing transforms raw vegetables by aligning their elemental profiles.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons chopped fresh basil, tarragon, or dill
instructions: In small sauce pan, reduce stock to 6 tablespoons and set aside to cool.
Combine stock with shallots, olive oil, mustard, orange juice, rice syrup and herbs in blender and puree until emulsified.

title: Mushroom Consommé
ingredients: 1/2 pound onion (1/2 large), peeled and chopped
1/2 pound carrot (1 large), peeled and chopped
instructions: Combine shiitakes, porcinis, and kombu with water in 2-gallon pot. Simmer stock 20 minutes, strain and reserve stock, discarding mushrooms and kombu.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Toss onion, carrot and fennel with oil. Roast vegetables until well caramelized, approximately 20 to 30 minutes. Add tomatoes and garlic for last 10 minutes.
In 12-inch sauté pan, sauté button mushrooms in oil until well caramelized, stirring often. Deglaze pan over high heat with some reserved stock.

Transfer sautéed mushrooms, their deglazing liquid, and roasted vegetables to 1-gallon pot. Add bouquet garni and remaining stock. Simmer 45 minutes, skimming surface. Strain and reserve broth, discarding bouquet garni and vegetables.
In 1-gallon pot, reduce broth to about 8 cups. Chill broth in ice bath to room temperature.

Add clarification to pot, mixing well. Heat broth to simmer, stirring occasionally. When simmer is reached, cease stirring. Clarification will rise to top, forming “raft.” Simmer uncovered another 20 minutes. Strain resulting “consommé” (clarified broth) through chinois lined with many layers of cheesecloth.
Degrease surface of consommé with paper towels, if necessary. Season with salt and pepper. Ladle soup in bowls and garnish with chives.

title: Red Lentil and Toasted Sunflower Burger
ingredients: 1/2 cup short grain brown rice, rinsed and drained
2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided
3/4 cup sunflower seeds toasted and ground in food processor
2 broccoli stalks (12 ounces), peeled, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 cup red lentils, sorted and rinsed
1/2 cup fresh chopped parsley leaves (3 ounces)
Extra virgin olive oil for brushing
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
In pressure cooker, dry roast rice until it has nutty aroma. Add water and pinch of salt. Bring rice to pressure for 30 minutes. Bring pressure down, and drain rice in colander.

While rice cooks, heat 2 tablespoons of oil in 10-inch sauté pan on medium-low heat. Add onions and garlic; sweat for 2-3 minutes. Add sunflower seeds, chili powder, cumin, and salt. Add broccoli stalks, carrots, and celery and sauté for 3 minutes more.
Combine vegetables with rice in food processor. Pulse until mixture becomes slightly sticky and binding. Set rice-vegetable mixture aside in bowl.

Combine lentils in 2-quart pot with 4 cups water and pinch of salt. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer, and cook for 15 minutes or until lentils are soft. Drain lentils in colander for 5 minutes and transfer to bowl with rice-vegetable mixture.
When mixture is cool, add parsley and kuzu. Mix all ingredients well with gloved hands. Form patties using 1/2 cup measure.
Lightly spray or brush 10-inch non-stick pan with olive oil. Brown patties on each side over medium heat and return to parchment-lined sheet pan. Heat patties in oven for 15 more minutes.
Serve with Fresh Ketchup (recipe following).

title: Fresh Ketchup (for Red Lentil and Toasted Sunflower Burger)
instructions: Combine onion and vegetable stock in 2-quart sauce pot. Bring mixture to boil, reduce to simmer, and cook for 15 minutes or until onion mixture is soft and reduced to 1/3 cup.
Add remaining ingredients to pot and simmer for 10 minutes more. Refrigerate.

title: Vegetable-Polenta Napoleons
ingredients: 2/3 cup cannellini beans, soaked and drained
8 sun-dried tomatoes, soaked until soft and coarsely chopped
instructions: In small saucepan, simmer beans in water with rosemary until beans are soft. Drain beans, discarding rosemary. In small bowl, combine beans, roasted garlic paste, oil and salt to taste. Using fork, mash bean mixture to chunky-smooth consistency and set aside.

In small saucepan, combine water, polenta, and salt, stirring until smooth. Simmer for 15 minutes. Stir oil, sun-dried tomatoes and basil into polenta and spread polenta onto lightly oiled sheet tray to 1/2-inch thickness. Chill polenta until firmly set. Using 3 1/2-inch ring mold, cut out 4 circles.
In small bowl, whisk together oil and vinegar. Using pastry brush, brush tops of mushrooms with oil-vinegar mixture and sprinkle with salt.

Coat non-stick pan with thin film of oil. Sear mushrooms covered (top side down), over low heat for 5-6 minutes. Flip mushrooms, brush “gill” side with remaining oil/vinegar, sprinkle with salt, and cook until tender, about 5 minutes. Cool mushrooms and cut out 4 circles using 3 1/2-inch ring mold.
Steam spinach in covered pot until wilted, 1 to 2 minutes. Drain and wring well, chop coarsely and season to taste with salt.
Stack Napoleon elements in layers on sizzle platter and reheat in oven. Serve atop Tomato Cream Sauce garnished with basil.

title: VEGETABLE AND TEMPEH WRAPS WITH AVOCADO-CILANTRO CREAM
yield_amount: Eight to ten 1-cup (3 ounce) wraps/1 cup avocado cream
ingredients: 8-ounce package tempeh, grated to texture of ground beef
1 red onion (6 ounces), 1/8-inch salad slice, soaked in water for 10 minutes, drained
1 red pepper (8 ounces), seeded, pith removed, and cut into 1/8-inch strips
1 zucchini (7 ounces), seeded, cut into 3-inch julienne
1 English cucumber (12 ounces), peel, seed, cut into 3-inch julienne
1 carrot cut (6-8 ounces), cut into 3-inch julienne, blanched for 1 1/2 minutes and shocked
1 ounce cilantro leaves, picked, washed, and spun dry
instructions: In non-stick pan, heat oil and lightly brown tempeh for 4-5 minutes. Add cumin and coriander, cooking 3 minutes more. Set mixture aside in bowl to cool.
Drain onion and combine with red pepper, zucchini, cucumber, carrot, and tempeh mixture. Mix well.
Rehydrate each rice paper wrap in bowl of warm water until soft. Transfer wrap to clean dry towel.

Spoon 1 cup of vegetable-tempeh filling on lower part of wrap in sausage shape. Fold bottom of wrap over mixture, then left and right sides to meet in middle. Finish rolling wrap forward to form shape of spring roll. Repeat with remaining rolls.
In bowl of food processor puree avocado, cilantro, olive oil, brown rice vinegar, pepper, and salt until smooth.
Slice each wrap in half on diagonal and top with dollop of avocado cream.

title: MEDITERRANEAN ROASTED BLACK COD WITH MUHAMMARA
ingredients: Extra virgin olive oil for brushing
2 large red peppers (1 pound), roasted, skinned and seeded
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F.
In small sauté pan over low heat lightly toast fennel and cumin. Remove from heat.
When spices are cool, powder them in coffee grinder and add allspice and sumac.
Divide black cod into six portions and sprinkle spice mixture on both sides of fish. Season with salt and pepper.
Transfer fish to parchment-lined half sheet pan. Lightly brush fish with oil.
Roast fish for about 7-8 minutes or until inside of fish is opaque. Remove from oven and set aside. Serve with Muhammara (recipe below).
Combine peppers, garlic, pomegranate molasses, lemon juice, and walnuts in bowl of food processor and puree.
Add breadcrumbs, black pepper, olive oil, vegetable stock, and sherry vinegar. Puree until smooth. Serve with Mediterranean Roasted Black Cod.

title: SEAFOOD SAUSAGE
ingredients: 1/3 large leek (4 ounces), white portion small-diced, green portion discarded
4 ounces shrimp, shelled, deveined, and small-diced
Extra virgin olive oil for brushing
instructions: In small sauté pan, heat oil, and add shallots and leeks. Sauté for 2 minutes or until translucent. Transfer to food processor.
Add sole to shallots and leeks in food processor and puree 30 seconds. Add egg whites, and process 15 seconds more. Scrape down bowl and process 15 seconds more.
Transfer mixture to bowl. Fold in chives, salt, and pepper scallops and shrimp. Divide mixture into eight 1/3-cup portions.

Form sausages 4 inches long and 1 1/2 inch thick. Roll each tightly first in parchment (8-inch x 6-inch sheets) and then in foil (8-inch x 6-inch sheets), twisting ends, and poach in simmering water for 9-10 minutes.
Unwrap sausage and cool slightly. Slice each sausage once on bias in middle.

Lightly brush non-stick pan with olive oil. Arrange sausages in pan so as not to crowd them. Brown them lightly on all sides. Serve with dollop of Honey-Mustard Yogurt and side of Baby Bok Choy and Red Cabbage Slaw (recipes following).
In small bowl, whisk all ingredients together.

title: BABY BOK CHOY AND RED CABBAGE SLAW (FOR SEAFOOD SAUSAGE)
ingredients: 2 small heads baby bok choy (8 ounces), leaves separated from stalks
1 carrot (6 ounces), cut into 4-inch long, thin matchsticks
6 scallions, trimmed and cut into thin, 1-inch long diagonals
1/4 cup of extra virgin olive oil
instructions: Blanch bok choy stalks in small pot of lightly salted, boiling water for 30 seconds. Drain and shock in ice water. Cut into 1/2-inch diagonals. Blanch leaves for 10 seconds. Drain and shock in ice water. Wring out excess water from leaves and finely slice.
Combine bok choy stems in bowl with cabbage, carrots, and scallions.
Whisk dressing ingredients together and combine with slaw.
Arrange 1 cup slaw mixture in middle of large plate.
Top with 2 pieces of Seafood Sausage and dollop of Honey-Mustard Yogurt.

title: Broiled Arctic Char Over Black Quinoa, Rapini, and Capers
ingredients: Four 3-ounce, skinless Arctic char filets (12 ounces), butterflied and pounded to 1/4 inch between parchment
Black Quinoa with Capers and Rapini (see following recipe)
instructions: Heat salamander and set rack on highest setting.
Combine salt and pepper, and sprinkle over char.
Lightly coat sizzle platter with oil and arrange fish on top.
Sprinkle remaining oil and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice atop each portion of fish.
Broil char under salamander for approximately 3 minutes.
Serve over Black Quinoa (recipe below).

title: Black Quinoa with Capers and Rapini (for Broiled Arctic Char)
ingredients: 1/2 cup black quinoa, rinsed and dry-roasted in pan
1 bunch rapini (1 pound), washed and trimmed (2 inches of stem removed)
1 tablespoon and 2 teaspoons extra virgin olive oil
1/4 cup basil leaves, stemmed and tightly packed
instructions: Combine quinoa with vegetable stock and salt in small saucepan. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer, and cook covered approximately 15 to 20 minutes or until stock is absorbed. Remove from heat, uncover pot and allow quinoa to rest 10 minutes.
In separate pot, bring 2 quarts of salted water to boil. Prepare ice bath. Blanch rapini for 3 minutes, shock in ice water, and drain. Squeeze out excess liquid.
Roughly chop rapini and set aside.
Heat oil in small sauté pan over low flame, add garlic, cooking briefly until just golden. Do not let garlic brown.
Transfer garlic and one tablespoon oil from pan to food processor. Add rapini. Pulse mixture until very finely chopped. Add basil and pulse again.
Transfer rapini mixture to bowl and thoroughly mix with quinoa, capers, black pepper, and lemon zest.
Divide quinoa-rapini mixture among four plates, placing Arctic Char filet atop each portion.

title: Apple Phyllo Roll
ingredients: 4 tablespoons coconut sugar (buzzed to powder in coffee grinder)
1/3 cup walnuts, oven-roasted and ground in food processor to breadcrumb texture
instructions: Preheat conventional oven to 400°F (convection oven will dry phyllo too much).
Prepare large bowl of water and add lemon juice. Peel, core, and thinly slice apples to 1/8-inch thickness and transfer them to lemon water to prevent browning.

Drain apples in colander. In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil. Add apples, coconut sugar, and cinnamon. Sauté apples on medium heat until they release most of their moisture and become tender, stirring frequently. Transfer apples to bowl to cool.

Unroll phyllo on work surface and cover with damp cloth. To assemble, lay one sheet of phyllo on parchment paper horizontally. Brush sheet with oil and sprinkle lightly with small amount of walnuts. Repeat process, stacking and brushing another 3 sheets.

Spoon apple mixture horizontally along bottom of phyllo, leaving 1-inch border on bottom and 2 inches on left and right side. Fold in left and right sides over filling, then roll phyllo from bottom to top to form log. Repeat steps 5-7 for the second phyllo roll.
Score both rolls halfway through on bias into 8 equal portions. Brush roll with remaining oil.
Transfer rolls to parchment-lined sheet tray and bake for 30-35 minutes or until golden.
Cool ten minutes, then slice all the way through pre-scored cuts. Serve with Lemon and Maple-Flavored Yogurt (recipe below).

title: Lemon and Maple-Flavored Yogurt
instructions: Whisk together all ingredients in small bowl.

title: Berry Sorbet
instructions: In blender, puree berries with grape juice, vodka, and lime juice.
Strain puree through fine sieve to remove seeds. Add maple syrup to taste.
Transfer berry mixture to ice cream machine and process until frozen.

title: Skewered Fruit with Chocolate Fondue
ingredients: 3 kiwi, peeled and cut into 12 (1/3-inch thick) rounds
1 1/2 oranges, peeled and cut into 3 (1-inch thick) rounds, then cut in half again
2 bananas, peeled and cut into 12 (1/2-inch thick) diagonal slices
instructions: Skewer fruit as follows: banana-kiwi-pineapple-strawberry-orange-strawberry-pineapple-kiwi-banana. Serve with Chocolate Fondue (recipe below).

title: Chocolate Fondue
instructions: In small saucepan, combine cocoa, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and orange extract, stirring until well combined.
Cover pot and gently bring to simmer. Lower heat and cook for 1-2 minutes, being careful not to burn chocolate.
Remove from heat and stir in soy milk until sauce has nappé.

title: Hemp Seed Milk
instructions: Put all ingredients into high-speed blender and blend until smooth.
Serve milk chilled or warmed.

title: Watermelon Juice
ingredients: 1 (2 pound) organic, seedless watermelon, scrubbed and peeled up to white pith
instructions: Cut watermelon into approximately 2 inch x 4 inch pieces.
In a high-speed blender puree watermelon pieces in batches.
Using a mesh strainer set over a large bowl strain watermelon juice. Discard solids.
Stir in lemon juice and refrigerate until ready to serve.

title: Beet and Apple Juice
instructions: Cut produce to fit juicer feed tube.
Alternate juicing beets and apples, finishing with beets. Refrigerate juice 5-10 minutes for foam to settle.
Scrape off any excess foam and serve immediately.

title: CARROT-CELERY-GINGER JUICE
ingredients: 1 1/4 bunches parsley (4 1/2 ounces), washed and spun dry
1 bunch celery (15 stalks or 2 pounds), cleaned
instructions: Cut produce to fit juicer feed tube. Juice produce alternating ingredients (parsley, celery, lemon, ginger, carrots, parsley, etc.).
Skim foam and serve immediately.

title: GREEN JUICE
ingredients: 1 1/2 large cucumbers (2 1/4 pounds), peeled, ends discarded
1-inch piece of ginger root, scrubbed, unpeeled
6 large kale or collard leaves (1/2 pound), washed, trimmed, stems intact
1/4 bunch parsley (1 ounce), washed and spun dry, 1/4-inch stems trimmed and discarded
instructions: Cut produce to fit juicer feed tube. Juice produce in order of ingredient listing, ending with celery stalks.
Serve immediately.

title: AMARANTH PORRIDGE
ingredients: 3 cup amaranth, soaked 6 hours in water to cover with 2 tablespoons of lemon juice
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F. Place 1 cup of the almonds on a half-sheet pan. Toast. Cool, roughly chop and set aside.
Bring 4 cups of water to a boil. Pour in remaining 2 cups almonds, cover and set aside for 15 minutes.

Using a high-speed blender, process almonds and their water in batches. Strain almond mixture through chinois and set milky liquid aside covered in the refrigerator until needed. Discard solids or save for another use.
Drain amaranth and combine with sea salt and 3 cups of water in 4-quart pot.

Bring amaranth to boil, reduce to simmer, and cover pot. Stir amaranth frequently, adding water in 1-2 cup increments until grains are cooked and no starchy aftertaste remains. Grain should still be chewy.
Remove pot from heat and stir in freshly ground flax seeds. Add additional salt, if desired.
Serve alongside toppings including fresh nut milk, toasted almonds, sweeteners, cinnamon, butter, and/or Apricot-Papaya Puree to taste.

title: APRICOT-PAPAYA PUREE
ingredients: 5 dried unsulphured apricots, soaked in boiling water until soft (about 5 minutes)
1 ripe papaya (1/2 pound), peeled, seeded and chopped
instructions: In high-speed blender, blend apricots with papaya, adding a bit of soaking water if necessary to form smooth puree.

title: VEGETABLE DETOX SOUP
ingredients: 8 cups vegetable stock or filtered water
2 cup green beans, washed, trimmed, cut into 1-inch lengths
2 medium zucchini, thinly sliced in 1/4 moons
2 scallions, cleaned and sliced on the bias to 1/8 inch
instructions: Bring stock or water and tomato paste to boil. Blend with wire whisk and skim.
Reduce heat, add garlic, beans, celery, and zucchini, and salt. Simmer until they are just tender and still green, about 10 minutes.
Stir in parsley, dill and scallions.
Serve in individual bowls with 1 teaspoon of Horseradish-Lemon condiment each.

title: HORSERADISH AND LEMON CONDIMENT
instructions: Grate horseradish on box grater using fine holes.
Combine horseradish with lemon juice and set aside for ten minutes at room temperature.
Serve cold or room temperature mixed into water, stock or soup.

title: Master Cleanse
description: Popularized by Stanley Burroughs (From Elson M. Haas, MD, The Detox Diet)
ingredients: 1-2 tablespoons organic maple syrup (preferably Grade B)
instructions: Mix and drink 8-12 glasses throughout day.
Eat or drink nothing else except water, laxative herb tea, and peppermint or chamomile teas.
Keep master cleanse in glass jar, not in plastic.
Rinse your mouth with water after each glass to prevent lemon juice from damaging enamel of your teeth.

title: Lemon-Cayenne Tea
instructions: Mix all ingredients together and drink in morning 1/2 hour before breakfast.

title: Flax Seed Drink
description: This drink contains phytoestrogens, omega-3 fatty acids, and is high in soluble fiber.
instructions: Grind flax seeds in spice grinder and mix with water. Drink immediately.

title: Warm Pinto Bean Salad with Shiitake
ingredients: 15 fresh shiitake mushrooms, stemmed and sliced thinly
instructions: Bring beans, kombu, and water to cover beans (plus 3 inches above) to full pressure in pressure cooker. Lower flame, cook 15 minutes.
In 10-inch sauté pan, lightly sauté fresh shiitake mushrooms in oil. Set aside.
Combine shiitakes, onion and radishes in medium mixing bowl.
In small bowl, combine shoyu, mustard, rice syrup, and vinegars. Slowly whisk in olive oil until emulsified. Pour dressing over vegetables.
Drain beans, add to vegetables, and toss until well mixed.
Gently mix in chives and watercress right before serving.

title: HIZIKI WITH FRESH LEMON
ingredients: 1 cup dried hiziki (rinsed and soaked 15-20 minutes)
1 small to medium onion, small dice
instructions: Drain off any remaining soaking water from hiziki and discard.
In 10-inch pan, sauté onion in oil for 3 to 5 minutes. Add hiziki, mixing well.
Add enough water to just barely cover hiziki. Add shoyu. Bring to boil. Cover, lower flame, and cook until most of liquid has evaporated – about 30 minutes.
Gently stir in scallions and lemon juice until well mixed. Serve.

title: CRUCIFEROUS SALAD
ingredients: 2 heads broccoli, bite-size florets (and stems trimmed and cut to bite-size pieces)
1/2 cup cashews, soaked overnight and drained
instructions: Bring 1-gallon water to boil with 1 tablespoon salt. Prepare an ice bath.
Blanch broccoli, broccoli stems, and cauliflower separately – broccoli for 30 seconds, cauliflower for 1 minute. Shock blanched vegetables in ice water.
Drain and transfer broccoli, broccoli stems, and cauliflower to bowl and add Brussels sprouts.
In Vitamix, combine dressing ingredients. Process until creamy and smooth, and more water if needed.
Toss vegetables in dressing and serve garnished with sesame seeds.

title: ROASTED ROOT VEGETABLES WITH TOASTED HAZELNUTS, CILANTRO, AND GARLIC
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Toast hazelnuts until fragrant, approximately 6-7 minutes. Set hazelnuts aside to cool.
Toss carrots, sweet potatoes, parsnips, and shallots in large bowl with olive oil and salt.
Transfer vegetables to sheet tray lined with parchment. Roast vegetables in oven until evenly caramelized, approximately 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Skin hazelnuts by wrapping in towel and vigorously rubbing them. Roughly chop hazelnuts and transfer them to large bowl.
Add vegetables to bowl and toss with parsley, cilantro, and garlic just before serving.

title: APRICOT PARFAIT
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, combine juice, agar flakes and vanilla extract. Let sit for 10 minutes to help agar dissolve.
Bring mixture to boil, and reduce to simmer, covered, until agar is completely dissolved, stirring and scraping bottom and sides of pot with wooden spoon to avoid sticking.
Pour juice mixture into hotel pan to cool quickly. Once steam has stopped rising, place in refrigerator to set up and harden into kanten.
Blend 3/4 kanten briefly in processor. Pour in serving bowls.
Blend remaining 1/4 kanten briefly with cashew butter. Serve as topping. Garnish with almonds and mint leaf.

title: SWEET BREW WITH CITRUS FRUIT
ingredients: 6 kukicha tea bags (1/2 cup loose kukicha)
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, bring water to boil.
Turn off flame. Steep tea bags and fruit in water, covered, for 5 minutes.
Add apple juice to pot, stir and serve.

title: GOLDEN TURMERIC MILK
ingredients: 1-2 tablespoons maple syrup, or to taste
instructions: In 2 batches, puree almonds, turmeric, and ginger with water in Vitamix until smooth. Strain milk through chinois.
Transfer milk to 1-gallon pot. Bring milk mixture to simmer over medium flame (to infuse flavors). Turn off heat and add pepper, cinnamon, and maple syrup to taste.

title: HERBED DINNER ROLLS
ingredients: 2 cups whole wheat pastry flour, divided
2 teaspoons chopped fresh thyme OR other herbs
1/4 cup sesame OR poppy seeds for top
olive oil OR rice syrup diluted with water for brushing
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. In large bowl, combine water, yeast, maple syrup, white high-gluten flour, and 1 cup whole wheat pastry flour. Stir until thick batter forms. Cover sponge with plastic wrap and set aside in warm place for 30-45 minutes or until foamy and doubled in size.
Add oil, salt, and thyme to sponge and stir to combine. Add remaining whole wheat pastry flour and just enough all-purpose flour to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.

Knead dough with remaining flour for about 10-15 minutes or until smooth dough has been created. Transfer dough to clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
Fold and turn dough. Rise again. Divide dough into 1-ounce balls.
Form dough into dinner roll shapes (see diagrams following) and transfer rolls to parchment-lined sheet trays. Cover with damp towel and set aside to rise for 10-15 minutes more.
Lightly brush tops of rolls with olive oil or egg wash. Sprinkle sesame seeds or poppy seeds on top.
Bake 15-20 minutes or until lightly golden and firm to touch.
Remove from sheet trays and cool on racks before serving.

title: BULGUR RAISIN BREAD
ingredients: 1/2 cup bulgur wheat, soaked in water until soft and drained
1 cup raisins, soaked in hot water until soft and drained
instructions: Preheat oven to 375° F. Prepare two 5 x 9 loaf pans with parchment. In medium bowl, combine water, maple crystals, and yeast. Proof for 10 minutes or until mixture is foamy.

Add salt, oil, cinnamon, allspice, vanilla, bulgur wheat, raisins, white high-gluten flour, and all-purpose flour. Then add just enough whole wheat pastry flour to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough adding remaining pastry flour as needed for about 10-15 minutes or until smooth dough has been created.
Transfer dough to large, clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
While dough is proofing, prepare bread pans or sheet pans with parchment.
Fold/turn dough. Rise again.

Divide dough into two equally sized balls. Roll each dough to a long rectangle, about 1/2-inch thick. Spread maple crystals and cinnamon over surface of dough. Roll dough lengthwise, jelly-roll style and transfer to loaf pans, seam side down.
Cover dough with wet towels and set aside in warm place. Rise until dough has doubled in size.
Bake approximately 30 minutes or until dough is golden and firm to touch. Internal dough temperature should be approximately 190°-205° F.
Cool bread on racks (out of pan) before serving.

title: BAGELS
ingredients: Toppings: poppy seeds, garlic, sesame seeds, Kosher salt, minced onion
instructions: In large bowl, combine water, rice syrup, and yeast. Proof for 10 minutes or until mixture is foamy.
Add olive oil and salt to yeast mixture. Stir together bread and pastry flour. Add enough flour to yeast mixture to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough using remaining flour as necessary, about 10-15 minutes, or until dough is smooth.
Transfer dough to clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
While dough is proofing, prepare 2 sheet pans lined with oiled parchment. Bring 1 gallon water to boil and preheat oven to 400° F.
Fold/turn dough. Rise again.
Divide dough into 16 equal-sized balls (3 ounces each by weight).
Form dough into bagel shapes by rolling to 8-inch strips and overlapping and rolling ends or poking hole through center and spreading dough apart.

Place toppings in bowls. Boil 3-4 bagels at a time for 30 seconds, flipping once or twice. Remove from pot with spider and turn bagel face side down into toppings. Gently lift bagels out of topping and transfer to prepared baking sheets. Repeat process until all bagels have been boiled.
Transfer bagels to oven and bake approximately 20-25 minutes or until dough is golden and firm to touch.

title: WHOLE WHEAT POPPYSEED BREAD
ingredients: 4-5 cups whole wheat pastry flour, divided
instructions: Preheat oven to 375° F. In large bowl, combine yeast, water, rice syrup, whole wheat bread flour, and 1 cup whole wheat pastry flour and stir until thick like batter. Cover sponge with plastic wrap and set aside in warm place for 30-45 minutes.
Add oil, salt, poppy seeds, and just enough of remaining whole wheat pastry flour to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough on table for about 10-15 minutes or until smooth dough has been created.
Transfer dough to large, clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
While dough is proofing, prepare bread pans or sheet pans with parchment.
When dough doubles in size, gently fold and turn dough. Cover with plastic and rise again.
Divide dough into two. Form dough into loaves and transfer to prepared loaf pans or on sheet trays. Cover dough with wet towel and rise one final time (approximately 15-30 minutes).
Bake approximately 30-45 minutes or until dough is golden and firm to touch.

title: WHOLE WHEAT BREAD
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°. In large bowl, combine yeast, water, whole wheat bread flour, 1 cup whole wheat pastry flour, and honey or maple syrup. Stir until thick batter is achieved. Cover sponge with plastic and set aside in warm place for 30-45 minutes.
Add olive oil, salt, and just enough of remaining pastry flour to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough about 10-15 minutes, adding remaining pastry flour as necessary, until smooth dough is formed.
Transfer dough to clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled.
While dough is proofing, prepare bread pans or sheet pans by lining with parchment paper.
Fold/turn dough. Rise one more time.

Divide dough into two, form into loaves, and transfer to loaf pans or on sheet tray for final rise. Cover dough with damp towel and set aside in warm place. Rise until dough has doubled in size, about 10-15 minutes.
Transfer bread to oven and bake approximately 30-45 minutes or until golden and firm to touch. Internal dough temperature should register between 190° and 205° F.

title: APPLE RING
ingredients: 6 apples, peeled, cored, and cut into medium dice
1/2 cup dried apricots, cut in 1/4 inch slivers
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. In large bowl, combine yeast, water, apple juice, rice syrup, and 2 cups all-purpose flour; stir until smooth. Cover sponge and set aside in warm place for 30-45 minutes.
Add melted oil, salt, and maple crystals. Mix together all-purpose and whole wheat pastry flours. Add flour, cup-by-cup until dough is formed that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough on table about 10-15 minutes, using remaining flour until smooth dough forms.

Transfer dough to large, clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled in size. While dough is proofing, prepare two half-sheet trays with parchment paper.
Turn/fold dough. Rise one more time.

Divide into two equal portions. Roll out first dough into rectangle about 1/4” thick. Pull edges of dough to create a rectangular shape. Spread half of apple filling across near end of dough horizontally. Fold dough over filling and roll log forward until seam is on bottom. Attach ends of dough to create ring. Move ring to prepared baking sheet. Repeat with remaining dough and filling.

Loosely cover rings with damp towel and set aside in warm place. Rise until dough has doubled in size, about 15-20 minutes. Cut 5-6 slits (steam vents) in top of each ring. Glaze each ring with maple syrup.
Transfer bread to oven and bake approximately 30-45 minutes or until dough is thoroughly browned. Cool rings completely before serving.

title: CHALLAH
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. In large bowl, combine water, honey, and yeast. Proof about 10 minutes until mixture is foamy. Add eggs, butter, salt, and just enough flour to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough about 10 minutes, adding extra flour as necessary, or until smooth dough has been created.
Transfer dough to large, clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
While dough is proofing, prepare 2 half sheet pans by lining with parchment paper.
Turn/fold dough. Rise one more time.

Divide dough into two equal portions. Divide each portion of dough into balls equaling number of braids you’ve chosen to make. Roll each ball of dough with your hands on table to form logs of equal width and length (about 1” wide and 10-12” long). Pinch logs of dough together at top and braid dough according to method in attached diagrams.
Loosely cover challahs with damp towels and set aside in warm place. Rise until dough has doubled in size, 15 to 20 minutes.

Bake bread approximately 30-40 minutes or until dough is thoroughly golden and firm to touch. Internal dough temperature should register between 190° and 205°F. Brush with egg wash few minutes before bread is finished baking.

title: SEMOLINA BREAD
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. In medium bowl, combine water, rice syrup, and yeast. Proof for 10 minutes or until mixture is foamy.
Add oil, rosemary, garlic, and salt. Add whole wheat bread flour, then semolina flour, and finally all-purpose flour in sufficient quantity to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough about 10-15 minutes, adding additional all-purpose flour as needed, until smooth dough has been created.
Transfer dough to large, clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
While dough is proofing, prepare bread pans or sheet pans with parchment.
Turn/fold dough. Rise one more time.
Divide dough into two equal-sized balls. Form dough into loaves and place in prepared loaf pans or sheet pans for final rise.
Loosely cover dough with damp towel and set aside in warm place. Rise for about 30 minutes or until dough has doubled again in size.
Bake bread approximately 30 minutes or until dough is golden and firm to touch. Internal dough temperature should register approximately 190° to 205°F.

title: PITA
instructions: Preheat convection oven to 400°F with a pizza stone inside. In bowl, combine water, maple syrup, and yeast. Proof for about 10 minutes or until mixture is foamy.
Add oil, salt, all-purpose flour, bread flour, and enough whole wheat pastry flour to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough about 10-15 minutes, using remaining pastry flour, until smooth dough has been created.
Transfer dough to clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for approximately 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
While dough is proofing, prepare 6 pizza peels dusted with cornmeal or semolina.
Fold/turn dough. Rise dough again.
Divide dough into 16 equal-sized balls. Using rolling pin, roll each ball of dough into 1/8-inch thick, 6-inch circle and transfer to pizza peels.
Loosely cover dough with damp towels and set aside in warm place. Rise for about 15 minutes.
Bake pita for approximately 3-4 minutes or until puffed. Don’t let pita brown, as that makes them too crisp.

title: Olive Bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 475°F. In small bowl, combine water, maple syrup, and yeast. Allow mixture to sit about 10 minutes until is foamy.
Add oil, olives, salt, bread flour, and just enough pastry flour to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough on table about 10-15 minutes, using remaining pastry flour, until smooth dough has been created.
Place dough in large, clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in a warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled.
While dough is proofing, prepare bread pans or sheet pans with parchment.

Punch down dough. If time permits, place dough back in bowl, rise, and punch down 1 to 2 times more. Divide dough into two equal portions. Form dough into loaves and place in prepared loaf pans or on sheet trays for final rise.
Loosely cover dough with damp towel and set aside in warm place. Allow to rise for about 40 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
Bake bread approximately 30 minutes or until dough is golden and firm to touch. Internal dough temperature should register approximately 190° - 205°.

title: Spelt Bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. In small bowl, combine water, maple syrup, and yeast. Allow mixture to sit about 10 minutes until mixture is foamy.
Add oil, salt, and just enough flour to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough on table about 10-15 minutes using remaining flour as needed until smooth dough has been created.
Place dough in large, clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in a warm place for 45-60 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
While dough is proofing, prepare 2 bread pans or sheet trays with parchment.

Punch down dough. If time permits, place dough back in bowl, rise, and punch down 1 to 2 times more. Cut dough into two equal-sized portions. Form dough into loaves and place in prepared loaf pans or on sheet trays.
Loosely cover dough with damp towel and set aside in warm place. Allow to rise for about 40 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
Bake bread approximately 40-50 minutes or until dough is golden and firm to touch. Internal dough temperature should register between 190° and 205°.

title: Sticky Buns
ingredients: 1 cup rice syrup OR 3/4 cups honey
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Whisk together syrup ingredients, combine raisin-walnut filling; set both aside.
In large bowl, combine warm water, maple syrup, and yeast. Proof for 5-10 minutes, or until yeast is activated (foamy). Add melted coconut oil.
In separate large bowl, whisk together flours and salt.
Add enough flour mixture to yeast mixture to create dough that pulls away from sides.
Knead dough about 10 minutes to form soft, smooth dough, using flour as necessary.
Transfer dough to clean, large bowl. Cover with plastic wrap, and rise until doubled in size (about 30 minutes to 1 hour).
Fold/turn dough. Rise one more time.

Cut dough in half and roll each half to 1/4-inch thick rectangle. Spread 1/3 syrup mixture over dough. Sprinkle 1/2 raisins and walnuts on top. Roll dough into cylinder. Repeat process with second dough.
Cut rolled dough into 2-inch rounds and arrange nestled in two oiled 9-inch cake pans lined with parchment.
Rise dough until doubled in size, approximately 15-20 minutes.
Bake for 25 minutes. Brush remaining 1/3 syrup mixture on top, rotate pans, and bake for 10-15 minutes more or until firm to touch and golden. Cool and unmold.

title: CHICKPEA CRÊPES
ingredients: 2 tablespoons olive oil OR coconut oil (or infused oil), plus more for pan
instructions: In bowl, whisk together flours with salt and pepper.
Add water, herbs and oil to flour mixture and beat with whisk until smooth. (May also be processed in blender.)
Let batter rest at least 30 minutes.

Prepare crêpe pan by oiling lightly. Pour 1/4 cup of batter into pan, tipping pan so batter thins to about 6-inch circle. Cook 30 seconds, until crêpe is golden brown on bottom. Flip, cook another 30 seconds.
If necessary, thin batter with more water to maintain consistency of light cream.

title: GLUTEN-FREE CRÊPES
ingredients: 3 tablespoons butter, melted (plus more for pan)
instructions: Emulsify all ingredients in blender until smooth.

title: GLUTEN-FREE FLOUR BLEND
instructions: Note: Use only 2 cups of this flour for crepe batter.

title: WILD RICE PANCAKES
ingredients: 1 cup cooked wild rice (from 1/3 cup raw rice)
3 tablespoons canola oil OR melted butter + more for frying
instructions: In large bowl, whisk together flour, maple crystals, baking powder, soda and salt.
Roughly chop cooked rice (needs to be dry, fluffy before adding to batter) and mix with dry ingredients.
In separate bowl, whisk together milk and lemon juice. Let stand 15 minutes. Add 2 tablespoons of oil or butter.
Gradually add wet ingredients to dry ingredients and stir to combine. Whisk egg whites to stiff peaks and fold into combined ingredients.
Heat griddle or large non-stick pan over medium heat. Brush little of remaining tablespoon of oil over surface of pan.
Using 1-ounce ladle, pour batter onto griddle. Cook cakes until bubbles in batter break on surface; flip and cook until browned.

title: BUCKWHEAT PANCAKES
instructions: In medium bowl, combine flours, baking powder, salt, baking soda, and maple crystals. Set aside.
In separate bowl, combine apple cider vinegar, and almond milk. Let sit for 5-10 minutes. Add banana.
Add dry ingredients to wet ingredients. Beat batter only until blended.
Using 1-ounce ladle, pour batter onto griddle. Cook cakes until bubbles in batter break on t surface; flip and cook until browned.

title: CORNMEAL PANCAKES (JOHNNY CAKES)
instructions: In large bowl, whisk cornmeal, salt and maple crystals. Slowly add boiling water. Cover and let stand for 10 minutes.
In separate bowl, beat together egg, milk and melted butter. Add to cornmeal mixture.
In separate bowl, whisk together flour and baking powder and stir into batter with few swift strokes. Add lemon zest and blueberries, if using.
Using 1-ounce ladle, pour batter onto griddle. Cook cakes until bubbles in batter break on surface; flip and cook until browned.

title: RICE FLOUR GRIDDLE CAKES
ingredients: 1 tablespoon flax seed, ground to fine meal
instructions: Whisk together flour, baking powder, maple crystals, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt and ground flax seed.
In blender combine almond milk, and butter or oil.
Combine liquid ingredients and dry ingredients.
Using 1-ounce ladle, pour batter onto griddle. Cook cakes until bubbles in batter break on surface; flip and cook until browned.

title: REAL BUTTERMILK WAFFLES
instructions: Heat waffle iron.
In large bowl, mix together flour, baking powder, salt, and maple crystals.
In separate bowl whisk egg yolks, butter, and buttermilk.
Mix wet ingredients with dry until batter is smooth. Set aside.
Beat eggs whites until soft peaks form. Fold egg whites into mixed batter.
Using 2 or 3-ounce ladle, pour batter onto waffle iron and cook 5-6 minutes, or until crispy.

title: SWEET POTATO WAFFLES
instructions: Heat waffle iron.
In large bowl, whisk together sweet potatoes, egg yolks, milk and butter.
In separate bowl, whisk together all-purpose flour, cornmeal, salt, baking powder, and maple crystals.
Mix wet ingredients into dry until batter is smooth. Set aside.
Whisk egg whites until soft peaks form. Fold egg whites into mixed batter.
Using 2 or 3-ounce ladle, pour batter onto waffle iron and cook 5-6 minutes, or until crispy.

title: BUTTERMILK-STYLE WAFFLES
ingredients: Aquafaba strained from 2 cans of chickpeas, beaten to stiff peaks
instructions: Heat waffle iron. In bowl, whisk together almond milk and vinegar. Set aside and let stand 15 minutes.
In medium bowl, whisk together to combine flour, baking soda and cream of tartar.
In separate bowl, whisk yolk with milk mixture and oil.
Beat egg white to stiff peak.

Add milk mixture to dry ingredients in thin, steady stream, while gently mixing with rubber spatula; be careful not to add liquid faster than it can be incorporated. Toward end of mixing, use folding motion to incorporate aqua faba.
Spread appropriate amount of batter onto waffle iron. Following manufacturer's instructions, cook waffle until golden brown, at least 5 minutes.

title: Blueberry Sauce
instructions: Combine blueberries, apple juice, and maple syrup in pot. Bring mixture to simmer.
Simmer sauce uncovered until blueberries begin to pop open and sauce begins to thicken, approximately 15 minutes.
Stir in lemon zest and vanilla. Simmer another minute.

title: Caramelized Apples
instructions: Melt butter or oil in large 12-inch pan over medium heat. Add apples to the pan with cinnamon, ginger, and salt.
Sauté apples until caramelized, stirring only occasionally, approximately 15 minutes.
Add maple syrup to pan. Sauté apples another 5-10 minutes until mixture thickens.

title: Fresh Strawberries and Bananas
instructions: In bowl, toss strawberries with maple crystals. Set aside to macerate for 30 minutes.
In separate bowl, combine bananas with lemon juice. When strawberries are done macerating, add bananas to strawberries.

title: Chocolate Sauce
instructions: In double boiler (bowl set over simmering water), melt chocolate.
In separate pot, bring almond milk to simmer, and turn off heat.
Slowly whisk just enough almond milk into melted chocolate to create a smooth and pourable sauce.

title: Whipped Cream
instructions: Combine all ingredients in stand mixer fitted with chilled bowl and whip until stiff peaks form.

title: Vegan Cashew Cream
instructions: Combine cashews, vanilla, brown rice vinegar, salt, and maple syrup in Vitamix with wand attachment. Start blending on low speed, and slowly raise speed while stirring with wand.
When mixture is starting to smooth, add water, and continue processing. When mixture is almost smooth, turn Vitamix up to highest setting. Finished cream should be thick and pipe-able.

title: White Bean with Garlic, Rosemary, and Tomatoes
ingredients: 1 cup white beans, soaked overnight and drained
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°F. Toss grape tomatoes in bowl with 2 tablespoons olive oil. Spread tomatoes on parchment-lined sheet tray and roast until well caramelized, an hour or more. Set aside.

While tomatoes are roasting, heat remaining 3 tablespoons olive oil over medium flame in pressure cooker. Add garlic. The moment garlic sizzles, add rosemary and oregano and stir. Add white beans, salt, and water.
Secure lid to pressure cooker, bring cooker up to pressure, lower heat, and cook beans for 25 minutes.
Bring down pressure by running cold water on lid of cooker. Drain beans, transfer to bowl and add roasted tomatoes. Season with additional salt and pepper, as needed.

title: SAUTÉED MUSHROOMS WITH HERBS
ingredients: Additional salt and pepper to taste
instructions: In large pan, heat oil over medium flame. Add shallots and garlic, lower heat, and sauté for 1-2 minutes. Add mushrooms with salt to pan and sauté until well caramelized, stirring only occasionally, 10-15 minutes.
Add wine to pan, turn up heat, and simmer until wine has evaporated.
All flour to pan. Stir until incorporated. Add stock and simmer until sauce is thickened, about 10 minutes.
Season with additional salt and pepper, if needed.
Add cheese to taste, if using.

title: ARUGULA, OLIVE OIL, BALSAMIC REDUCTION
ingredients: 1/4 pound Reggiano parmesan, grated or shaved (optional)
instructions: Combine balsamic vinegar and vanilla in medium pan. Bring mixture to simmer, and cook down to approximately 1/2 cup or until mixture thickens enough to coat back of spoon. Set mixture aside to cool.
Toss arugula, oil, and salt together in bowl. Drizzle balsamic reduction to taste over greens. Add Reggiano parmesan, if using.

title: CRANBERRY WALNUT SCONES
ingredients: 3/4 cup almond milk soured with 1 tablespoon lemon juice
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Line half-sheet pan with parchment.
In medium bowl, stir together flours, maple crystals, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
With pastry cutter, cut butter into dry ingredients until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add cranberries, nuts, and zest.
Make well in center and gradually stir in milk, ginger juice, and maple syrup just to combine. Do not over mix; dough should be sticky and difficult to work with.
On lightly floured surface, pat or roll dough to 1/2 inch thickness. Cut into shapes and place on sheet pan. Brush tops with milk and sprinkle with maple crystals.
Bake 12-15 minutes or until golden brown and firm to touch. Remove from pan to cool.

title: VEGAN SCONES
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F and line one half-sheet tray with parchment paper.
Sift flour, sea salt, baking soda, baking powder, and cinnamon together in medium bowl. Whisk to combine and set aside.
In small bowl, combine apple juice, currants, lemon zest, lemon juice, almond milk, and maple syrup. Whisk to emulsify and set aside.
Cut chilled coconut oil into flour mixture until fat is crumbly and coated in flour. Crumbles should be about size of small peas or smaller.
Pour wet ingredients into dry and gently mix with rubber spatula just until combined, being careful to not over-mix.
Transfer dough to lightly floured table and gently roll to about 3/4 inch thick. Using cookie cutter, cut shapes and place on parchment-lined baking sheet.

Bake for about 6 minutes and rotate pan in oven to ensure even browning. Glaze with maple syrup at this point, if desired. Continue to bake another 6-8 minutes or until golden on top and somewhat firm to touch.
Transfer scones to cooling rack. Serve warm with jam or cool and store in tightly sealed container for up to four days.

title: Flaky Biscuits
ingredients: 8 tablespoons chilled unsalted butter OR 7 tablespoons chilled coconut oil
3/4 cup organic milk OR almond milk
2 tablespoons melted butter for brushing on top (optional, for non-vegan biscuits)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375° F and line half-sheet tray with parchment paper.
Sift flours, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon (if using) in medium sized bowl. Whisk to combine.
Cut fat into flour mixture until fat is crumbly and coated in flour. Crumbles should be about size of small peas or smaller.
Pour in milk or almond milk and gently mix with rubber spatula just until combined, being careful to not over-mix.

Transfer dough to lightly floured table and gently roll out to shape of rectangle, about 1/4 inch thick. With long edge facing you, fold in both short ends to meet in middle. Fold dough forward in half and very gently roll together to 3/4 inch or no thinner than 1/2-inch square. This folding technique will provide flaky layers if not over-worked and too flat.
Cut biscuits with lightly floured cookie cutter or sharp knife and transfer to sheet tray. If desired, brush tops with melted butter.
Bake for about 6 minutes and rotate pan in oven to ensure even browning. Continue baking for about another 10 minutes or until golden brown on top and slightly firm to touch.
Transfer to rack and cool.

title: Sour Cream Coffee Cake
ingredients: 1/4 pound cold butter (1 stick), cut into 8 chunks
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Grease a deep 9-inch Bundt mold with butter and dust with flour. Set aside.
In bowl, stir together the filling mixture until well mixed and set aside.
In stand mixer, cream butter and maple crystals. Add eggs one at a time, and beat until well incorporated. Add sour cream and mix until batter is smooth. Add vanilla.
Sift together flour, salt, and baking soda. Add dry ingredients to wet in stand mixer, and mix until batter is smooth.
Pour batter in prepared cake pan. Sprinkle crumble mixture over batter. Pour remainder of batter atop filling.
Bake cake for 35-40 minutes, or until a toothpick or skewer inserted in cake comes out smooth.
Let cake sit in pan for approximately 15 minutes before unmolding onto cooling rack. Cook cake completely before slicing.

title: Morning Glory Muffins
ingredients: 1/2 cup raisins, soaked for 5 minutes in hot water and drained
1 Granny Smith apple, peeled and grated
1 tablespoon flax seed, ground, mixed with 3 tablespoons water
2 tablespoons ground kuzu, mixed with 3 tablespoons water
instructions: Heat oven to 350° F (325° F in convection). Prepare muffin tins with paper liners.
Sift flour, sugar, baking soda, and cinnamon into bowl. Whisk ingredients together and set aside.
Combine carrot, raisins, pecans, coconut, and apple in bowl. Mix into the dry ingredients.
Whisk together the flax mixture, kuzu mixture, oil, and vanilla.
Add the liquid ingredients to batter. Stir to combine. Let batter sit for 2-3 minutes to allow moisture from the fruit to loosen batter, stir again.
Line muffin tin with cupcake liners. Portion batter with 1/4 cup ice cream scoop (yellow handle).
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until skewer or toothpick inserted in center of muffin comes out clean. Cool muffins completely on cooling rack before serving.

title: Zucchini Bread
ingredients: 1 tablespoon flax seed, ground, mixed with 3 tablespoons water
1/2 cup + 3 tablespoons whole wheat pastry flour
instructions: Heat convection oven to 350° F. Line a 4 1/2 x 9-inch loaf pan with parchment.
Whisk together flax mixture, canola oil, vanilla, and rice vinegar in bowl. Stir in zucchini and pecans. Set aside.
In separate bowl, sift together coconut sugar, cinnamon, salt, baking soda, baking powder, all-purpose flour and whole wheat pastry flour.
Whisk wet ingredients into dry. Pour batter into loaf pan and bake for 55 minutes.
Let bread cool completely before slicing and serving.

title: Vegan and Gluten-Free Spiced Fruit Muffins
ingredients: 1/4 cup flax seed, ground to powder in a coffee grinder
1/4 cup red quinoa, ground to powder in a coffee grinder
1 cup diced pear (or may use diced Granny Smith apples)
1/2 cup frozen or fresh cranberries (could use blueberries or cherries, instead)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Line muffin tins with muffin papers or use silicon baking molds.
Whisk together almond meal, flax meal, ground quinoa, oats, baking powder, salt, pepper, spices, and orange zest in bowl; set aside.
Whisk together applesauce, oil, and maple syrup in separate bowl.
Make well in dry ingredients and pour in wet mixture; stir to combine. Mix in pear and cranberries (or apples and blueberries).
Fill each muffin chamber 2/3 full. Bake 20-25 minutes or until skewer inserted in center of a muffin comes out clean.
Leave muffins in chambers for 10 minutes before inverting onto a cooling rack. Allow muffins to cool completely before serving.

title: Vegan and Gluten-Free Chocolate Brownies
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (convection oven to 300°F). Oil and flour 9-inch square pan and line with parchment.
In bowl, whisk together almond flour, sorghum flour, tapioca flour, cocoa powder, salt, and baking powder. Process to combine. Transfer dry ingredients to bowl.

In separate bowl set over simmering water, melt 1 cup of chocolate and coconut oil. Remove from heat and whisk chocolate and coconut oil together. Stir in kuzu-water mixture, coconut sugar, coconut milk, and vanilla. Whisk wet ingredients to emulsify.
Pour wet mixture into dry and stir well. The batter should be stiff. Fold in walnuts and remaining 1/4 cup chocolate.
Transfer batter to pan, place parchment atop brownie. Smooth surface with hands until even.
Bake 30 minutes. Cool in pan for about 1 hour (otherwise brownie will crack when cut). Cut edges of brownie away from sides of pan before removing from pan.

title: Gluten-Free Flour Blend
instructions: Whisk together to combine.

title: Gluten-Free Feta-Dill Quick Bread
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups Gluten-Free Flour Blend (see previous recipe)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Mix together flour, baking powder, and salt in large bowl.
In separate bowl, combine olive oil, milk, egg, and feta cheese. Add wet mixture to dry, and mix dough to combine. Add dill.
Pour batter into muffin molds and immediately transfer back to oven to bake.
Bake for at least 30-40 minutes. Bread is ready when toothpick inserted in bread comes out clean.

title: GLUTEN-FREE PEAR-CARDAMOM QUICK BREAD
ingredients: 1 cup Gluten-Free Flour Blend (see previous recipe)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F.
In large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and cardamom.
In separate bowl, whisk together egg, maple crystals, canola oil, and vanilla extract. Add wet ingredients to dry and stir to combine. Add pears and walnuts and divide batter between muffin molds.
Bake for 25-30 minutes or until golden brown and toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.
Let bread cool in pan for 10 minutes before removing to wire rack to finish cooling.

title: GLUTEN-FREE IRISH SODA BREAD
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups Gluten-Free Flour Blend (see previous recipe)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375° F.
In large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and maple crystals.
In separate bowl, combine butter, eggs, and buttermilk and beat to combine well. Add raisins.
Create well in center of dry ingredients. Add buttermilk and egg mixture and mix gently to combine. Transfer dough to muffin molds.

Bake bread about 30-40 minutes, or until golden in color and toothpick inserted in quick bread comes out clean. Remove from oven, and allow bread to cool in muffin forms for 10 minutes before inverting finished bread onto cooling rack.

title: GLUTEN-FREE BANANA BREAD
ingredients: 1 cup Gluten-Free Flour Blend (see previous recipe)
1 cup mashed banana (from about 2 medium bananas)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F.
In large bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
In separate medium bowl, whisk together butter, maple crystals, egg, mashed bananas, vanilla extract, and buttermilk.
Whisk wet ingredients into dry. Fold together, making sure to scrape bottom of bowl to mix well. Pour dough into prepared muffin molds.
In small saucepan, melt butter for crumble. Combine butter, maple crystals, and rolled oats in bowl. Transfer crumble to top of bread.
Bake for 30-45 minutes, or until toothpick inserted into center of loaf comes out clean. Remove from oven and allow to rest in pan for 15 minutes, before inverting onto wire rack to cool completely.

title: GLUTEN-FREE GREEN OLIVE-PARMESAN BREAD
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups Gluten-Free Flour Blend (see previous recipe)
instructions: Preheat oven to 325° F.
Whisk together flour, baking powder, salt, and baking soda in one bowl.
In separate bowl, whisk together egg, buttermilk and melted butter.
Mix wet into dry until just combined. Add olives and parmesan.
Pour batter into muffin molds.
Bake for 30-45 minutes, or until toothpick inserted in bread comes out clean.

title: Pizza Dough #2
ingredients: 1 tablespoon rice syrup OR honey OR maple syrup
instructions: In medium bowl, mix sponge ingredients together with whisk.
Let sponge sit 20-30 minutes covered with plastic wrap in warm place.
Add salt, oil and water to sponge, then add flours in 1/2 cup increments until incorporated.
Knead about ten minutes. Let pizza dough sit 40 minutes covered with plastic wrap in warm place.
Preheat oven to 475°F.
Place pizza stone in oven to heat up at least 30-40 minutes before baking.
Transfer pizza to peel before adding sauce and topping.
Bake on stone 8-10 minutes, turning once at half-way point.

title: Spelt Pizza Dough
ingredients: 1 tablespoon rice syrup or honey or maple syrup
instructions: In medium bowl, mix sponge ingredients with whisk.
Cover with plastic wrap and let rise for 20 to 30 minutes in warm place.
Add water, salt, and oil to sponge, and mix together with wooden spoon. Add flour in 1/2 cup increments.
Remove dough from bowl, form dough into ball and knead on floured work surface. Dough should be soft and a little sticky. Knead for about 10 minutes.
Place dough in lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic and place in warm spot until doubled in size, about 40 minutes. Punch down, shape dough and bake.
Preheat oven to 475°F. Place pizza stone in oven to heat up at least 30-40 minutes before baking.
Transfer pizza to peel before adding sauce and toppings.
Bake on baking stone 8-10 minutes, turning once at half-way point.

title: Gluten-Free, Egg-Free Mini Pizzas
ingredients: 2 tablespoons olive oil for greasing sheet trays
3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided
instructions: Line 2 half-sheet trays with Silpats or parchment generously brushed with olive oil.
In medium bowl, dissolve maple crystals in water and add yeast. Set aside.
In another medium bowl, sift and whisk together rice flour, tapioca flour, and xanthan gum. Add fennel seeds and salt, and mix together. Set aside.

In small pot, add flax seed to boiling water and simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until water reaches consistency of egg white. Strain mixture through fine-meshed sieve, and retrieve 2 tablespoons of seedless extract. Set aside.
In stand mixer, blend flaxseed extract, 1 tablespoon olive oil and yeast-maple crystal water. Add flour mixture and beat on high for 2 minutes.
Using tablespoon, dollop batter onto baking sheets, making 6-8 mini pizzas per sheet tray. With circular motion, spread each circle out with spoon to about 2 inches in diameter.
Cover with inverted baking sheet, and let rise 40-45 minutes. Preheat oven to 375°F.
Bake each mini pizza 10 minutes without toppings.
Remove trays from oven, and garnish pizzas with toppings of choice. Dribble remaining oil over each mini pizza. Bake another 10 minutes to cook fillings and melt cheese, if using.

title: Whole Wheat Pizza
ingredients: 1 tablespoon rice syrup or honey or maple syrup
instructions: In medium bowl, mix sponge ingredients with whisk.
Cover with plastic wrap and rise for 20-30 minutes in warm place.
Add water, salt and oil to sponge. Mix together with wooden spoon. Add flour in 1/2 cup increments.
Remove dough from bowl, form into ball and knead on floured work surface. Dough should be soft and pliable. Knead for about 8-10 minutes.
Place dough in lightly-oiled bowl, cover with plastic and place in warm spot until doubled in size about 40 minutes. Punch down, shape dough and bake.
Preheat oven to 475°F. Place pizza stone in oven to heat up at least 30-40 minutes before baking.
Transfer pizza to peel before adding sauce and toppings.
Bake on pizza stone from 8-10 minutes, turning once at half-way point.

title: Sun-Dried Tomato and Pesto
ingredients: 1 large bunch fresh basil, washed and stems removed
instructions: Combine all ingredients in food processor until puréed. Taste and adjust seasoning.

title: Roasted Red Pepper and Mushrooms
ingredients: 1 pound red pepper, cut into large dice
1/2 pound mushrooms, washed, stemmed, cut into quarters
3 cloves garlic, peeled and cut in half
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F-400°F.
Combine vegetables, garlic, and olive oil in large roasting pan.
Roast for 30 minutes. Drain and cool.

title: Tomato Sauce
ingredients: 1 28-ounce can peeled tomatoes, chopped (pulsed in food processor)
sea salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat oil in 1-gallon pot over low heat. Add onion, garlic and salt. Sweat onion and garlic for 5-10 minutes, covered, stirring occasionally.
Add rosemary, thyme, basil, tomatoes, and wine. Bring sauce to simmer.
Simmer sauce until flavors are well developed, approximately 40 minutes to 1 hour.
Add salt and pepper.

title: Tofu Ricotta
ingredients: 1 tablespoon white miso, or more to taste
instructions: In small sauté pan, combine olive oil and garlic. Bring oil to heat over medium flame. When garlic starts to sizzle, remove pan from heat. Let garlic and oil cool.
Combine oil, garlic, tofu, rice vinegar, lemon juice, and white miso in food processor. Pulse mixture until it resembles texture of ricotta. Season to taste with salt, as needed.

title: Vegan Mozzarella
instructions: Place cashews, cashew milk, tahini, lemon juice, coconut oil, salt, garlic powder, dried basil and 1/4 cup water in a Vitamix and blend until smooth. Blend until very smooth and creamy.
Place agar in 1 1/2 cups water and bring to simmer. Simmer until agar dissolves. Add agar mixture to blender and blend until smooth.
Pour mixture into silicone molds and refrigerate until set.

title: Cashew Milk
instructions: Puree cashews and water in Vitamix until cashews are completely pulverized. Strain milk through chinois.

title: Spinach Pasta
ingredients: 1 large bunch spinach (3/4 pound), washed, stemmed, cooked until tender, drained, and finely chopped
2 1/2 cups unbleached all-purpose flour OR half all-purpose flour/half whole wheat bread flour
instructions: Press spinach with your hand to squeeze out any excess liquid. Mix spinach with eggs and set aside.

Pulse flour in food processor to evenly distribute and aerate. Add spinach/egg mixture; process until dough forms rough ball, about 30 seconds. If dough resembles small pebbles, add water, 1/2 teaspoon at a time; if dough sticks to side of bowl, add flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, and process until dough forms rough ball.
Turn dough ball onto work surface; knead until dough is smooth, 1 to 2 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside for at least 15 minutes and up to 2 hours to relax.

title: Fresh Herb Pasta
ingredients: 2 tablespoons minced fresh parsley, basil, mint, cilantro, sage, thyme, oregano, or marjoram
instructions: Pulse flour in food processor to evenly distribute and aerate. Add eggs and herbs, process until dough forms rough ball, about 30 seconds. If dough resembles small pebbles, add water, 1/2 teaspoon at a time; if dough sticks to sides of bowl, add flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, and process until dough forms rough ball.
Turn dough onto work surface; knead until dough is smooth, 1 to 2 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside for at least 15 minutes and up to 2 hours to relax.
Sift flour onto work surface (or bowl) in mound and make hollow in middle. Break eggs into hollow and add minced herbs. With fork, start to mix in flour from edge.

Gradually incorporate most of flour until viscous paste begins to form. Put fork to one side and, using both hands, heap remaining flour from outside over paste in middle. Work flour into paste. If paste does not absorb all flour, and if ingredients cannot be easily worked, add a little water.

Work in water with both thumbs, then press dough into ball and work in rest of flour. Knead dough by pushing out dough with heels of hands, then form it into ball again. Repeat this kneading action until dough has firm but slightly elastic consistency and no longer changes shape when you remove your hands. Cover with plastic wrap and rest minimum of fifteen minutes.

title: WHOLE WHEAT PASTA
instructions: Pulse flours in food processor to evenly distribute and aerate. Add eggs, process until dough forms rough ball, about 30 seconds. If dough resembles small pebbles, add water, 1/2 teaspoon at a time; if dough sticks to sides of bowl, add flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, and process until dough forms rough ball.
Turn dough onto work surface; knead until dough is smooth, 1 to 2 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside for at least 15 minutes and up to 2 hours to relax.
Sift flours onto work surface (or bowl) in mound and make hollow in middle. Break eggs into hollow. With fork, start to mix in flour from edge.

Gradually incorporate most of flour until viscous paste begins to form. Put fork to one side and, using both hands, heap remaining flour from outside over paste in middle. Work flour into paste. If paste does not absorb all flour, and if ingredients cannot be easily worked, add a little water.

Work in water with both thumbs, then press dough into ball and work in rest of flour. Knead dough by pushing out dough with heels of hands, then form it into ball again. Repeat this kneading action until dough has firm but slightly elastic consistency and no longer changes shape when you remove your hands. Cover with plastic wrap and rest minimum of fifteen minutes.

title: BUCKWHEAT PASTA
instructions: Pulse flours in food processor to evenly distribute and aerate. Add eggs, process until dough forms rough ball, about 30 seconds. If dough resembles small pebbles, add water, 1/2 teaspoon at a time; if dough sticks to sides of bowl, add flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, and process until dough forms rough ball.
Turn dough onto work surface; knead until dough is smooth, 1 to 2 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside for at least 15 minutes and up to 2 hours to relax.
Sift flours onto work surface (or bowl) in mound and make hollow in middle. Break eggs into hollow. With fork, start to mix in flour from edge.

Gradually incorporate most of flour until viscous paste begins to form. Put fork to one side and, using both hands, heap remaining flour from outside over paste in middle. Work flour into paste. If paste does not absorb all flour, and if ingredients cannot be easily worked, add a little water.

Work in water with both thumbs, then press dough into ball and work in rest of flour. Knead dough by pushing out dough with heels of hands, then form it into ball again. Repeat this kneading action until dough has firm but slightly elastic consistency and no longer changes shape when you remove your hands. Cover with plastic wrap and rest minimum of fifteen minutes.

title: CORN PASTA
instructions: Pulse flour and cornmeal in food processor to evenly distribute and aerate. Add eggs, process until dough forms rough ball, about 30 seconds. If dough resembles small pebbles, add water, 1/2 teaspoon at a time; if dough sticks to sides of bowl, add flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, and process until dough forms rough ball.
Turn dough onto work surface; knead until dough is smooth, 1 to 2 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside for at least 15 minutes and up to 2 hours to relax.
Sift flour and cornmeal onto work surface (or bowl) in mound and make hollow in middle. Break eggs into hollow. With fork, start to mix in flour from edge.

Gradually incorporate most of flour until viscous paste begins to form. Put fork to one side and, using both hands, heap remaining flour from outside over paste in middle. Work flour into paste. If paste does not absorb all flour, and if ingredients cannot be easily worked, add a little water.

Work in water with both thumbs, then press dough into ball and work in rest of flour. Knead dough by pushing out dough with heels of hands, then form it into ball again. Repeat this kneading action until dough has firm but slightly elastic consistency and no longer changes shape when you remove your hands. Cover with plastic wrap and rest minimum of fifteen minutes.

title: SAFFRON PASTA
instructions: Crumble saffron into eggs and stir to dissolve.
Follow one of the below methods.

Pulse flour in food processor to evenly distribute and aerate. Add saffron-egg mixture, process until dough forms rough ball, about 30 seconds. If dough resembles small pebbles, add water, 1/2 teaspoon at a time; if dough sticks to sides of bowl, add flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, and process until dough forms rough ball.
Turn dough onto work surface; knead until dough is smooth, 1 to 2 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside for at least 15 minutes and up to 2 hours to relax.
Sift flour onto work surface (or bowl) in mound and make hollow in middle. Pour saffron-egg mixture into hollow. With fork, start to mix in flour from edge.

Gradually incorporate most of flour until viscous paste begins to form. Put fork to one side and, using both hands, heap remaining flour from outside over paste in middle. Work flour into paste. If paste does not absorb all flour, and if ingredients cannot be easily worked, add a little water.

Work in water with both thumbs, then press dough into ball and work in rest of flour. Knead dough by pushing out dough with heels of hands, then form it into ball again. Repeat this kneading action until dough has firm but slightly elastic consistency and no longer changes shape when you remove your hands. Cover with plastic wrap and rest minimum of fifteen minutes.

title: TOMATO PASTA
instructions: Stir tomato paste into eggs.
Follow one of the below methods.
Method - Food Processor:

Pulse flour in food processor to evenly distribute and aerate. Add tomato-egg mixture, process until dough forms rough ball, about 30 seconds. If dough resembles small pebbles, add water, 1/2 teaspoon at a time; if dough sticks to sides of bowl, add flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, and process until dough forms rough ball.
Turn dough onto work surface; knead until dough is smooth, 1 to 2 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside for at least 15 minutes and up to 2 hours to relax.
Method - By Hand:
Sift flour onto work surface (or bowl) in mound and make hollow in middle. Pour tomato-egg mixture into hollow. With fork, start to mix in flour from edge.

Gradually incorporate most of flour until viscous paste begins to form. Put fork to one side and, using both hands, heap remaining flour from outside over paste in middle. Work flour into paste. If paste does not absorb all flour, and if ingredients cannot be easily worked, add a little water.

Work in water with both thumbs, then press dough into ball and work in rest of flour. Knead dough by pushing out dough with heels of hands, then form it into ball again. Repeat this kneading action until dough has firm but slightly elastic consistency and no longer changes shape when you remove your hands. Cover with plastic wrap and rest minimum of fifteen minutes.

title: BLACK PEPPER PASTA
instructions: Method - Food Processor:

Pulse flour and black pepper in food processor to evenly distribute and aerate. Add eggs, process until dough forms rough ball, about 30 seconds. If dough resembles small pebbles, add water, 1/2 teaspoon at a time; if dough sticks to sides of bowl, add flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, and process until dough forms rough ball.
Turn dough onto work surface; knead until dough is smooth, 1 to 2 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside for at least 15 minutes and up to 2 hours to relax.
Method - By Hand:
Sift flour and pepper onto work surface (or bowl) in mound and make hollow in middle. Break eggs into hollow. With fork, start to mix in flour from edge.

Gradually incorporate most of flour until viscous paste begins to form. Put fork to one side and, using both hands, heap remaining flour from outside over paste in middle. Work flour into paste. If paste does not absorb all flour, and if ingredients cannot be easily worked, add a little water.

Work in water with both thumbs, then press dough into ball and work in rest of flour. Knead dough by pushing out dough with heels of hands, then form it into ball again. Repeat this kneading action until dough has firm but slightly elastic consistency and no longer changes shape when you remove your hands. Cover with plastic wrap and rest minimum of fifteen minutes.

title: BEET PASTA
ingredients: 1 small beet, all but last inch of stem trimmed
instructions: Place beet in medium saucepan and cover with water. Boil until tender, about 1 hour. Drain and cool beet.
Slip skin off beet, puree, put beet through food mill or potato ricer. (Do not put into food processor or it will be too wet.)
Transfer beet puree to fine sieve over bowl or sink, pressing down occasionally with back of large spoon, until most of water has drained off.
Reserve 1 tablespoon of puree for pasta; discard remaining puree or save for another use.
Follow either method below.
Method - Food Processor:

Pulse flour in food processor to evenly distribute and aerate. Add eggs and beet puree, process until dough forms rough ball, about 30 seconds. If dough resembles small pebbles, add water, 1/2 teaspoon at a time; if dough sticks to sides of bowl, add flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, and process until dough forms rough ball.
Turn dough onto work surface; knead until dough is smooth, 1 to 2 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside for at least 15 minutes and up to 2 hours to relax.
Method - By Hand:
Sift flour onto work surface (or bowl) in mound and make hollow in middle. Break eggs into hollow and add beet puree. With fork, start to mix in flour from edge.

Gradually incorporate most of flour until viscous paste begins to form. Put fork to one side and, using both hands, heap remaining flour from outside over paste in middle. Work flour into paste. If paste does not absorb all flour, and if ingredients cannot be easily worked, add a little water.

Work in water with both thumbs, then press dough into ball and work in rest of flour. Knead dough by pushing out dough with heels of hands, then form it into ball again. Repeat this kneading action until dough has firm but slightly elastic consistency and no longer changes shape when you remove your hands. Cover with plastic wrap and rest minimum of fifteen minutes.

title: VEGAN PASTA DOUGH
ingredients: 2 cups unbleached all-purpose flour OR 1 cup unbleached all-purpose flour and 1 cup whole wheat bread flour
instructions: Blend tofu, olive oil, water, and salt in blender under smooth.

In food processor, pulse flour for few seconds to aerate it. Add tofu mixture and process 30 seconds. Squeeze dough into ball. Remove dough to lightly floured work surface and knead for 1 minute, or until smooth. Wrap in plastic wrap and let sit at least 15 minutes.

title: Vegan Dough with Water
instructions: Combine flours and salt in food processor. With machine running, add just enough hot water to make stiff dough that forms a ball. Make sure to add water slowly (over at least 30 seconds) to ensure you don’t add too much water. Dough should not be tacky or sticky.

Remove dough from food processor and knead on lightly floured work surface until smooth and elastic, about 1 minute. Wrap in plastic wrap and let sit at least 15 minutes. (The dough can be tightly wrapped in plastic wrap for several hours.)

title: Ravioli with Beet Stuffing
ingredients: 1/2 teaspoon salt, or more to taste
instructions: Cover beets with water and simmer until tender. Peel and puree in food processor.
Toast fennel seeds in sauté pan. Remove to spice grinder or mortar and pestle and grind.

Heat butter in 8-inch sauté pan, and sauté shallots until translucent. Add breadcrumbs and sauté until golden, stirring constantly. Add shallot-breadcrumb mixture to beet puree and stir in fennel, ginger, salt and pepper. Stir in egg yolk.

Form ravioli: Roll out pasta dough thinly and cut out disks about 2 1/4 inches in diameter. Put a little stuffing in middle of each dish and brush edge with egg white or water. Fold each disc over into half-moon shape and press edges firmly together to seal. Cook ravioli in boiling salted water until they rise to the surface.

title: Herb Ravioli with Porcini Mushroom Pesto and Tofu "Ricotta"
ingredients: 1/2 teaspoon sea salt, plus more to taste
instructions: In food processor, combine tofu, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, garlic and miso. Process, stopping few times to scrape down sides, until tofu is smooth with slightly granular texture. Add chopped rosemary and pulse to combine. Remove from processor and set aside. Wash out food processor bowl.
Cover mushrooms in bowl with boiling water, and soak for 20 minutes. Drain mushrooms and clean thoroughly to remove all sand attached to stems. Discard soaking liquid.
Combine mushrooms, garlic, pine nuts, olive oil, salt and black pepper in food processor and process until smooth. Add chives and pulse to combine. Taste for seasoning.

Roll out dough and fill ravioli according to directions (see procedures in Pasta Lecture/Demo), spooning in 1/2 teaspoon of tofu cheese first, then adding heaping 1/2 teaspoon of mushroom pesto on top. Cover with second sheet of dough to encase filling. Ravioli can be frozen, if desired, at this point.
