Cook ravioli in 12-inch sauté pan filled with 2-inches of simmering water for 3 to 5 minutes, depending on dryness of your pasta and whether or not they were frozen.
Use slotted spoon to gently lift ravioli out of water. Drain briefly for a moment, then serve immediately.

title: PUMPKIN, SAGE, AND PECAN RAVIOLI
ingredients: 1 winter squash (2 to 2 1/2 pounds), e.g., kabocha or butternut (about 2 cups cooked)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.

Cut squash in half from stem to bottom and place cut side down on parchment-covered sheet pan. Bake about 45 minutes, until fork tender. Let cool a few minutes, then remove and discard seeds. Scoop flesh into bowl and mash with fork. Measure out 2 cups of mashed squash, reserving any extra.

Warm oil in 10-inch sauté pan. Add onions and sauté until caramelized, about 10 minutes. Add pecans and sauté few minutes more. Add squash, mixing until heated through. Stir in sage leaves, and season with salt and pepper to taste. Remove from heat.
Roll out dough; fill and form raviolis.
Bring 12-inch sauté pan filled with 2 inches of water to simmer. Add raviolis and cook 3 to 5 minutes, depending on dryness of pasta and whether or not they were frozen.
Use slotted spoon to gently lift raviolis out of water. Drain briefly for a moment, then serve immediately.

title: WHITE BEAN AND GARLIC SAUCE
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups navy beans OR Great Northern beans, soaked OR 2 (15-ounce) can white beans
instructions: Drain soaked beans. Cover with 6 cups water and pressure cook about 8 minutes for navy beans, or 10 minutes for Great Northern beans. Season with salt to taste. Let beans sit in cooking liquid a few minutes to absorb salt. Drain beans, reserving cooking liquid. Alternately, on stove top, simmer partially covered about an hour and a half, until beans are soft. Season with salt to taste. Let beans sit in cooking liquid a few minutes to absorb salt. Drain beans, reserving cooking liquid.

Warm olive oil in 12-inch sauté pan. Add onions and cook over medium-low heat for about 7 minutes, until onions soften and start to brown. Add garlic and cook a few more minutes until fragrant. Add cooked beans and 2 1/2 cups of bean cooking liquid. (If using canned beans, add 2 1/2 cups water.)

Tie herbs together with string and add to pot along with bay leaf. Cover, bring to boil over medium heat, reduce heat and simmer, partially covered, for 15 minutes to allow flavors to marry. Add lemon juice and salt and pepper to taste. Remove bay leaf and tied herbs.

title: OLIVE TAPENADE
ingredients: 1 cup pitted black olives, preferably Kalamata
instructions: Combine all ingredients in food processor and blend until smooth. Stop now and again to scrape down sides with spoon or spatula.

title: FRESH TOMATO SAUCE
ingredients: 1 28-ounce can whole tomatoes, drained OR 2 pounds fresh tomatoes, concassé, 1/2-inch dice
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan combine oil, garlic, basil and tomatoes.
Cook over medium heat 25 to 35 minutes until tomatoes have released their juices and water has cooked out.
Add salt and black pepper to taste. You can puree sauce in blender, or serve it as is.

title: BASIL-WALNUT PESTO
ingredients: 3/4 cup walnuts for pesto plus 1/2 cup for garnish
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Toast walnuts on sheet tray for 10 minutes. Remove from oven and transfer to strainer. Rub walnuts against strainer over bowl to loosen some of skins. Whatever readily comes off is enough.
Transfer walnuts from strainer and add 3/4 cup to food processor. Add remaining ingredients.
Process everything until smooth. Chop by hand remaining 1/2 cup of walnuts into pieces. Reserve for garnish.

title: SAUTÉED SWISS CHARD
ingredients: sea salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
instructions: Remove stems from chard and discard or save for another dish. Roughly chop leaves and wash. In 1-gallon pot, cook chard over medium heat, stirring frequently until wilted but still bright green. You don’t have to add water to pot; water clinging to leaves from washing is enough to cook them.
Remove chard from pot and squeeze out any excess liquid. Finely chop.
Warm oil with garlic in 10-inch sauté pan. When garlic just begins to color, add chard and stir to warm through. Season to taste with salt and black pepper. Remove from heat and stir in rosemary.

title: BUTTER-POPPYSEED SAUCE
instructions: Heat butter in 8-inch sauté pan and stir in ground poppy seeds.
As soon as ravioli are cooked, remove them with slotted spoon to pre-warmed plates. Pour poppy seed butter over them and sprinkle with grated parmesan.

title: CLASSIC BUTTER AND SAGE SAUCE
instructions: Heat butter until it browns slightly and add sage leaves.
As soon as ravioli are cooked, remove them with slotted spoon, arrange on plates, pour sage butter over.

title: ALMOND “CREAM” SAUCE
ingredients: 1-2 tablespoons mellow white or chickpea miso
instructions: In blender, soak almonds in 1 cup hot water for at least 15 minutes. Process until smooth, gradually adding remaining cup of water. Strain through fine mesh sieve and return almond milk to blender.
In 2 1/2 quart sauce pan, sweat onion in oil with salt until completely soft. Add garlic and cook a few minutes longer, being careful not to brown vegetables.
Add sweated vegetables and miso to almond milk. Process until very smooth, adding salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer sauce back to sauce pan and heat slowly, whisking to break up lumps. Stir in lemon juice and serve immediately.

title: SPICE LOAF RING
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups (3 sticks) softened butter, + 1-2 teaspoons butter for greasing pan
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour 9 or 10-inch non-stick Bundt pan.
In food processor, combine butter and maple crystals. Process 2-3 minutes. Add eggs one at a time. Add almond extract. Continue to blend 2-3 minutes longer. Transfer to medium bowl.
In separate medium bowl, sift together flours, baking powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice, and salt.
Fold flour mixture into butter egg mixture (batter will be slightly thick).
Pour batter into Bundt pan (should be about 2/3 full).
Bake for 25-30 minutes. Cool in pan about 10 minutes. Run knife around edge to loosen cake from pan. Cool completely on rack.

title: GLUTEN-FREE FLOUR BLEND
instructions: Use only 3/4 cup flour for cake recipe.

title: TOASTED WALNUT CRUST (FOR LIGHT LEMON TART)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Oil and parchment 8 or 9-inch tart pan. Alternatively, use 5-6 four-inch, non-stick paper tart pans.
Combine nuts, flour, maple crystals, and salt in food processor. Grind to fine meal.
Add syrup and oil to processor. Pulse to form dough.
Place dough in middle of tart pan. Using your fingertips, press dough out so that bottom and sides are evenly covered. Trim crust and discard any excess. Blot excess oil from crust with paper towel.
Transfer tart pan(s) to refrigerator for 10-15 minutes to chill.
Transfer tart pan(s) to sheet tray and bake about 20 minutes. Cool completely before filling.

title: LIGHT LEMON TART
ingredients: 2 cups apple juice or white grape juice
1/4 cup kuzu mixed with 1/2 cup water
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, simmer agar in apple juice until agar is completely dissolved.
Add rice syrup, maple syrup, turmeric and pinch of salt.
Combine kuzu slurry with vanilla; add to agar-juice mixture. Cook until kuzu begins to thicken and goes from cloudy to clear.
Add lemon juice and zest.
Pour mixture into shallow bowl or pan and cool completely. Whisk frequently so that it does not 'set up.'
When mixture is cooled, pour into pre-baked and cooled tart crust.
Refrigerate tart to set filling.

title: GLUTEN-FREE CHOCOLATE MACAROONS
ingredients: 1/2 cup + 2 tablespoons maple crystals, ground to powder
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line half sheet tray with parchment paper.
Melt chocolate in double boiler, and set aside to cool.
In medium bowl, add salt to egg whites and beat with hand mixer or whisk to soft peaks. Slowly add maple crystals, spoonful by spoonful, to form until incorporated.
Using spatula, fold in vanilla and coconut.
Fold in cocoa powder and cooled melted chocolate. Gently fold to incorporate all ingredients.
Using 1-ounce ice cream scoop, transfer cookie dough to sheet tray, leaving space between for slight spread.
Bake 15-20 minutes. Cool and serve.

title: ALMOND-OAT CRUST (FOR ALMOND-FRUIT TART)
ingredients: 1 tablespoon melted coconut oil or melted butter
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Oil 9-inch tart pan and line with parchment paper or use 5-6 four-inch, non-stick paper tart pans.
In food processor, grind almonds, oats, and oat flour together until pulverized to fine meal.
In small bowl, combine maple syrup, salt, and oil or butter. Whisk until emulsified.
Pour maple-oil mixture into food processor, pulsing to form cohesive dough. If dough is too moist to work with, transfer to fridge for 5-10 minutes.
Using fingers, press dough evenly into prepared pan. Blind bake with pie weights to prevent crust from puffing up. Bake 15-20 minutes or until edges of crust are browned. Set aside to cool.

title: RICH ALMOND MILK (FOR ALMOND-FRUIT TART)
instructions: Combine almonds and water in blender or Vitamix. Process for a few minutes until well-homogenized.
Rinse piece of cheesecloth (double thickness) with cold water and wring thoroughly.

Pour small amount of almond mixture through sieve or chinois lined with prepared cheesecloth, wringing and squeezing cheesecloth to extract as much liquid from pulp as possible. Discard each batch of almond pulp when no more liquid can be extracted. Rinse cheesecloth well in cold water and proceed with next batch, repeating this process until all of almond mixture has been 'milked.'
Strain almond milk through sieve. Add vanilla.

title: ALMOND-FRUIT TART WITH ALMOND CRUST
ingredients: 6 tablespoons agar flakes, processed in coffee grinder until fine
2 tablespoons kuzu dissolved in 2 tablespoons water
1 ripe banana, sliced and sprinkled with lemon juice
instructions: For pastry cream: Combine agar, water and maple syrup in 2 1/2 quart saucepan. Soak agar 10 minutes.
Bring agar mixture to boil, lower heat and simmer with lid slightly ajar until agar is dissolved, about 15 minutes. Whisk or stir often, scraping agar from sides and bottom of saucepan.
Stir almond milk, salt, and extracts into agar mixture. Bring to boil, whisking often.
Add kuzu slurry, whisking vigorously, until mixture comes back to boil. Cook few minutes longer.
Pour mixture into shallow pan and chill in freezer or refrigerator until set.
In food processor, whip chilled mixture until very smooth and creamy. Halfway through processing, stop machine and scrape sides of work bowl with spatula.
For tart assembly: Fill tart crusts with Almond Pastry Cream. Arrange fruit on top of cream.

title: AMARETTI COOKIES
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 325°F. Line 2 half-sheet pans with Silpats.
Beat egg whites to soft peaks; add maple crystals one tablespoon at a time and continue whipping until incorporated.
Add extracts and salt; whip 10 seconds more.
Using rubber spatula, fold almond flour into egg white mixture. Mixture will be soft and 'meringue-like' in consistency.
Scoop dough onto sheet tray with 1-ounce ice cream scoop. Top each cookie with 8-10 pignoli nuts.
Bake 18-20 minutes or until cookies are golden. Cool cookies before carefully removing from pan with metal offset spatula.

title: FRESH BERRY TART WITH NUT CRUST
ingredients: 2 tablespoons kuzu dissolved in 1/4 cup juice
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Oil 9-inch tart pan or use 5-6 four-inch, non-stick paper tart pans.
In food processor, grind pecans to fine meal with flour, maple crystals, baking powder, and salt.
In separate small bowl, whisk together oil and maple syrup.
Add oil and syrup to dry ingredients in processor. Pulse lightly until dough comes together.
Press crust mixture into tart pan(s). Refrigerate 15-20 minutes, then bake 20-25 minutes. Let cool completely.
In small pot, simmer agar flakes in apple juice until agar completely dissolves. Add kuzu/juice slurry. Whisk until mixture thickens. Add maple syrup, pinch of salt, and berries.
Cook 6-8 minutes. Remove from heat and let mixture cool partially. Pour filling into cooled crust.
Let set completely. Garnish with mint before serving. There may be filling left over. Puree in food processor to make pudding.

title: BLUEBERRY CORN MUFFINS
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 400°F. Grease muffin pan with butter.
In medium bowl, combine dry ingredients.
In separate small bowl, combine wet ingredients: butter, milk, maple syrup, and eggs.
Mix wet ingredients into dry ingredients. Add blueberries. Mix carefully to incorporate.
Using standard ice cream scoop, fill muffin tins 3/4 full.
Bake 15-18 minutes. Best served warm.

title: LEMON WAFER COOKIES
ingredients: 3/4 cup blanched almonds, toasted and cooled
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 350°F. Line 2 sheet trays with parchment paper.
In food processor, grind almonds to fine meal with flours, baking soda, and salt.
In separate smaller bowl, whisk together wet ingredients.
Add wet ingredients to dry ingredients in processor (mixture will be loose). Chill dough in refrigerator for 10-15 minutes.
Using 1-ounce ice cream scoop, scoop batter onto sheet tray, leaving room between each cookie as batter spreads.
Sprinkle each cookie with few almond slices.
Cook 15 minutes or until cookies are slightly golden. Cool before removing from parchment paper.

title: CURRANT SCONES
ingredients: 1 cup flour (rice or Gluten-Free Flour Blend)
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 350° F.
Sift and whisk together flour, baking powder, salt, and maple crystals.
Cut butter into dry mix to form coarse crumbs.
In another bowl, beat egg, heavy cream, zest and currants together.
Add wet to dry until just combined.
Fold out onto table and form semi-flattened log.
Cut log into triangles and bake on parchment for 10 minutes.
Serve warm.

title: GLUTEN-FREE FLOUR BLEND
instructions: Note: For this recipe, use only 1 cup.

title: SPINACH-SHIITAKE SALAD
ingredients: 1 1/4 pounds shiitake mushrooms, sliced thinly (6 cups packed)
instructions: Heat oil over medium flame in 12-inch sauté pan. Add mushrooms and salt, and sauté until mushrooms are crispy and golden brown, stirring often to prevent sticking. There should be approximately 1 1/2 cups of cooked mushrooms.
Wash and dry spinach thoroughly. Transfer to large bowl and toss with mushrooms, pine nuts and onion.
Mix salad with dressing and top with 'Cheese Medallions.' Serve immediately.

title: SHERRY VINAIGRETTE
ingredients: 1 tablespoon + 1 1/2 teaspoons Grade A maple syrup
4 tablespoons + 1 1/2 teaspoons extra virgin olive oil
instructions: In medium mixing bowl, combine garlic, mustard, vinegar, salt, and maple syrup. Gradually whisk in olive oil.

title: "CHEESE" MEDALLIONS (FOR SPINACH-SHIITAKE SALAD)
ingredients: 1 cup cashews, soaked overnight and drained
instructions: Combine cashews, lemon juice, miso, salt, coconut oil, and boiling water in food processor. Process for 1 minute, or until smooth, scraping down sides occasionally. Add pectin powder and pulse until combined. Stream in calcium water as engine is running.
Transfer mixture to bowl and refrigerate for 10 to 20 minutes, or until slightly firm. Scoop into medallions using retractable ice cream scoop. Roll sides in crushed pistachios.

title: GOAT CHEESE, BEET, AND WALNUT SALAD
ingredients: 20 small beets, roasted, peeled and cut into bite size pieces
2 baguettes, cut into 1/2" slices and toasted
1 recipe Creamy Fresh Horseradish Dressing (recipe below)
instructions: In large bowl, toss all greens together and put on individual plates.
Assemble each plate with endive, onion, beets, walnuts, goat cheese, and baguettes.
Dress each salad immediately prior to service.

title: CREAMY FRESH HORSERADISH DRESSING
ingredients: 1/4 pound silken tofu, blanched 2-3 minutes and drained
instructions: In blender, combine horseradish, tofu, oil, miso, soymilk, garlic, lemon, vinegar, and mustard. Process until creamy and smooth. Add pepper and Tabasco to taste, blending until incorporated.

title: Tempeh Reuben Sandwich
instructions: Assemble the sandwich by spreading avocado spread or Nayonaise on sliced Spelt Bread.
Layer with Fried Tempeh, sliced tomato, mustard, ketchup, and sauerkraut.
Garnish with pickles and serve immediately.

title: Ketchup (for Tempeh Reuben Sandwich)
ingredients: 1/4 cup golden raisins (soaked in 1/4 cup water, 10-15 minutes)
instructions: In blender, combine raisins (with soaking liquid), tomato puree, garlic, water, paprika, cayenne, cinnamon, and salt. Blend until all ingredients are combined.
In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, on high heat, heat oil. Slowly add tomato mixture. Lower heat and simmer 40 minutes. Add Worcestershire sauce and lemon juice. Simmer 5 minutes more. Adjust seasonings.

title: Fried Tempeh (for Tempeh Reuben Sandwich)
ingredients: 8 oz. tempeh, sliced on diagonal into 6-8 pieces
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, combine marinade ingredients. Cover pan, bring to boil.
Add tempeh slices, lower heat, and simmer uncovered 30-40 minutes.
Drain tempeh and pat dry.
In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil. Brown tempeh slices on both sides. Drain on paper towel.

title: Spelt Bread (for Tempeh Reuben Sandwich)
instructions: In small bowl, combine water and maple syrup then gently sprinkle yeast on top. Allow to sit until foamy.
Stir in oil, some of the flour, and salt until well combined. Gradually add more flour while stirring until dough is firm and workable.

Turn out dough onto lightly floured surface and knead to form sturdy and pliable dough. Transfer to large, lightly oiled bowl and cover tightly with plastic wrap. Let rise in warm place until doubled in size (about 1 hour).

Fold and turn dough, and rise again (about 30 minutes). Divide into two balls. Shape dough and transfer to lightly oiled loaf pans, score if desired. Cover with damp towels and rise again in warm place (about 20 minutes).
While bread is rising, pre-heat oven to 375° F. Bake bread until well-browned and finished (inner temp of 200° F). Remove from pan and cool on racks before slicing.

title: Tofu Scramble
ingredients: 1 pound firm tofu, pressed and then mashed
1 large ripe tomato (peeled, seeded and chopped)
1 1/2 teaspoons sea salt or to taste
instructions: In 12-inch sauté pan over medium heat, warm oil. Add onion and sauté for 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in garlic, pine nuts, cumin, red pepper flakes and turmeric; sauté 2 minutes more.
Add mashed tofu, tomato, salt and lime juice to pan. Raise heat and simmer, stirring, for 5 minutes to heat through.

title: Basic Waffles (with Butter and Milk)
ingredients: 1 3/4 cups whole wheat pastry flour, sifted
instructions: In large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and maple crystals.
In separate bowl, beat egg yolks well; add butter, milk, and salt.
Make well in center of dry ingredients. Pour in liquid ingredients and combine with a few swift strokes. Batter should have pebbled look, similar to muffin batter.
Beat egg whites until stiff, but not dry, fold into batter with rubber spatula just to combine.
Heat waffle iron. Ladle in 3/4 cups batter.
Cook each waffle 4 minutes or until indicator light on machine goes on.

title: Blueberry Syrup
instructions: Combine all ingredients in 2 1/2 quart pot. Bring syrup to boil, reduce heat, and simmer mixture uncovered for 15 minutes.
Strain syrup through chinois, pressing any remaining juice from blueberries. Discard blueberries, and transfer syrup to syrup containers.

title: KASHA VARNISHKES (KASHA WITH BOWTIES)
ingredients: 2 cups sliced mushrooms (button, or Portobello, or combination)
sea salt and black pepper, freshly ground
2 cups mushroom OR chicken stock, heated
instructions: Melt butter or oil in 10-inch sauté pan. Over medium-high heat, cook onions and mushrooms until caramelized, adding garlic toward end of cooking. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Transfer to large bowl.
In large pot of boiling salted water, cook pasta until al dente. Drain pasta and toss with onion mixture.
In small bowl, beat egg and add kasha. Stir until grains are well coated.
Wipe out sauté pan and heat again over high heat. Transfer kasha mixture to pan and cook, stirring, until grains are toasted and separate, 2 to 3 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and add stock. Stir, cover, and simmer until stock is absorbed and kasha is tender but not mushy, 7 to 8 minutes.
Stir in pasta mixture. Taste and adjust seasonings. Garnish with parsley and salmon.

title: CHICKPEA CRÊPES WITH CURRIED CHICKPEAS, VEGETABLES & MANGO SAUCE
instructions: Place a warm Chickpea Crêpe flat on a clean surface or plate.
Spoon a generous portion of the Curried Chickpea and vegetable filling down the center of the crêpe.
Fold or roll the crêpe to neatly enclose the filling.
Garnish with Mango Sauce drizzle and fresh herbs. Serve warm.

title: CHICKPEA CRÊPE
ingredients: 2 tablespoons olive OR coconut oil, plus more for pan
instructions: In large bowl, sift flours, salt, and pepper.
Add water, herbs and oil to flour mixture and blend with whisk until smooth. (May also be processed in blender.)
Let batter rest at room temperature at least 30 minutes. If necessary, thin batter with more water to maintain consistency of light cream.

Prepare crêpe pan by oiling lightly. Pour approximately 1/4 cup of batter into pan, tipping pan so batter thins covers pan. Cook 30 seconds, until crêpe is golden brown on bottom. Flip, cook another 30 seconds.

title: MANGO SAUCE (FOR CHICKPEA CRÊPE)
ingredients: 1 ripe mango, peeled, pitted and coarsely chopped
2 ripe plum tomatoes, concassé (peeled, seeded, and chopped), reserve juices
instructions: In blender, combine mango, tomatoes, tomato juices, shallot, canola oil, lime juice, and salt. Blend until creamy.
Adjust seasonings. Garnish sauce with jalapeño and cilantro.

title: CURRIED CHICKPEA CRÊPE FILLING
ingredients: 2 medium carrots (about 12 ounces), cut into 1/4-inch wide quarter moon slices
1 medium potato (about 1/2 pound), peeled and cut into small dice
1/2 cup vegetable stock OR bean liquid from cooked chickpeas
2 bunches spinach (about 1 1/2 pounds), stemmed, washed, and chopped
instructions: In pressure cooker, combine chickpeas, water, cumin, salt, and bay leaves.
Bring pot to pressure, lower heat, and cook 30 minutes. Drain beans, set aside, and reserve bean liquid if using in place of stock.
In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil. Add onions. Lower heat and cook until onions begin to soften and become translucent. Add curry. Cook 2-3 minutes more.
Add carrots and potatoes, stirring often to prevent sticking. Cook 5-6 minutes. Add garlic and salt. Cook 2-3 minutes more. Add stock or bean liquid reserved from chickpeas.

Add coconut milk and chickpeas. Simmer 3-4 minutes more. Add spinach. Cook until spinach becomes tender (about 1-2 minutes). Adjust seasonings. If mixture looks too thick, add bit more stock or bean liquid.

title: Blueberry Muffins
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 350°F. Line muffin pans with paper liners and set aside.
In medium bowl, sift flours, baking powder, baking soda, and spices together and whisk to combine.
In separate bowl, whisk together oat milk, kuzu, melted coconut oil, maple syrup, vinegar, and salt. Pour wet mixture into dry ingredients bowl. Whisk to combine. Gently stir in blueberries.

With 1-ounce scoop, pour batter into muffin papers to fill almost to top (about 3/4 full). Transfer muffin tins to half sheet pans. Bake 5-6 minutes, rotate pans, and continue to bake (about 6-8 minutes more) until top of muffins bounces back when touched and toothpick comes out clean.
Remove muffins from pans immediately and cool completely before serving.

title: Corn Muffins
ingredients: 1 tablespoon + 1 1/2 teaspoons chopped jalapeno pepper, minced
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 350°F. Line mini muffin pans with muffin papers and set aside.
In 8-inch sauté pan, heat oil. Add onions and garlic. Cook, on medium-low heat, to caramelize (about 10-15 minutes). Remove from heat and stir in red pepper, jalapeno pepper, and cilantro. Set aside.
In medium bowl, sift cornmeal, flour, maple crystals, baking powder, and baking soda. Whisk to combine.
In separate medium bowl, whisk together oat milk, kuzu, salt, vinegar, and olive oil.
Pour wet mixture into flour mixture and gently stir with spatula just to combine then add onion/pepper mixture. Mix just enough to incorporate ingredients.

With 1-ounce scoop, pour batter into liner-filled muffin tins to almost the top (about 3/4 full). Transfer muffin tins to half sheet pans. Bake 5-6 minutes, rotate pans, and continue to bake (about 6-8 minutes more) until top of muffin bounces back when touched and toothpick comes out clean.
Remove muffins from pans immediately and cool completely before serving.

title: Basic Home-Fried Potatoes
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 350°F.
Wash potatoes and pat dry. Prick with fork.
Combine potatoes on sheet pan and bake for one hour (or until fork tender).
When potatoes are cool, peel them and cut them into large dice.

In 12-inch sauté pan, heat 2-3 tablespoons oil. Add onions in batches, cooking over medium-high heat, until they are wilted and lightly browned (about 10-15 minutes), adding more oil as necessary. Transfer each batch of onions to bowl, and continue until all onions are caramelized. Add baked potatoes to onions and stir to combine.
Add potato-onion mixture, in batches, back to pan with more oil, as necessary, turning potatoes with spatula until they are crisp and brown.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.

title: Challah
instructions: Combine water and honey. Sprinkle yeast on top of mixture. Set aside until foamy.
Add eggs, butter, and salt. Add flour, one cup at a time, stirring until dough comes together.
Turn dough out onto lightly floured surface and knead to form smooth, pliable dough (about 10 minutes), adding flour as necessary.
Transfer dough to large, clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover tightly with plastic wrap. Let rise in warm area until doubled in size (about 1 hour).
Turn and fold dough. Cover and rise again (about 1/2 hour).
Divide dough into two large balls. Divide each ball evenly into equal portions for braiding. Roll divided dough into long strips of equal length.

To braid, pinch strips together at top. Braid both breads, transferring each to separate parchment-lined sheet tray. Cover breads with damp towels, and rise one more time (15-20 minutes), until doubled in size.
While bread is rising, pre-heat oven to 350°F.
Glaze bread with egg wash and bake until golden brown and fully cooked (inner temp about 190°F).
Transfer breads to cooling racks and fully cool before slicing.

title: ORANGE, KIWI, AND RED GRAPE COMPOTE
ingredients: 4 large oranges, supremed (about 2 cups of slices)
3 kiwis, peeled and sliced (about 1 cup of slices)
1 cup seedless red grapes, sliced in half
instructions: In small saucepan, combine water, rice syrup, maple syrup, crushed cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and allspice. Whisk together and bring to boil. Reduce until mixture becomes thick and syrupy, about 3-4 minutes.
Add vanilla extract and vodka (if using) and continue to cook another 2 minutes. Remove from heat and remove cardamom pods and cinnamon stick.
In medium bowl, combine oranges, kiwis, and grapes. Combine syrup mixture with fruit and stir gently to combine. Taste and adjust seasonings, adding lime juice as needed.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour and up to 36 hours to allow flavors to combine. When ready to serve, sprinkle with toasted almonds.

title: CHOCOLATE PUDDING
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, over medium heat, simmer together milk, cocoa, agar, and sea salt for about 20-25 minutes, whisking to fully dissolve agar.
In small bowl, dissolve kuzu in water. Pour into saucepan with cocoa mixture, stirring until liquid returns to boil. Continue to cook one minute more until mixture is thicker.
Add maple syrup and vanilla to taste. Cook 2 to 3 minutes more.
Remove pot from heat and mix some of cocoa mixture with cashew butter until cashew butter is thinner, smooth, and creamy. Whisk tempered cashew butter into cocoa mixture.
Pour cocoa mixture into shallow pan and cool until firm. In food processor, blend mixture until smooth and creamy. Spoon pudding into ramekins and chill until set (about 30 minutes).

title: CRÊPES SUZETTE
ingredients: 1 pint heavy cream, whipped, for garnish (optional)
instructions: In 12-inch sauté pan, over medium heat, bring butter, orange juice, maple crystals, lemon juice, and zest to boil. Cook until thickened, about 3-4 minutes and stir in Grand Marnier and brandy. Bring again to boil for 30 seconds. Transfer syrup to bowl and set aside.

In separate 10-inch sauté pan, over medium-low heat, pour in small ladle full of syrup and heat to simmer. Place one crêpe (browned side down) in pan. Coat crêpe in warmed syrup then flip crêpe over with tongs to coat other side. Fold crêpe in quarters (fold in half then half again) to form triangle.
Pour 1 tablespoon of Grand Marnier per crêpe over top of folded crepe. Ignite by tilting pan toward burner. Allow alcohol to burn off before removing to plate and serving. Repeat to order.

title: PLAIN CRÊPES (CLASSIC)
ingredients: 1/4 cup clarified butter, for crepe pan
instructions: In mixing bowl, sift flour with salt. Make well in center of dry ingredients. Break eggs into well and whisk until thoroughly incorporated. Immediately add 2 cups of milk, melted butter and brandy or rum (if using). Whisk together until incorporated.
Strain mixture through fine chinois to remove any lumps. Add more milk, if necessary, to achieve consistency of thin cream.
Refrigerate mixture for 30 minutes.

In 7-inch crêpe pan, fry crêpes. If storing, layer with parchment paper and wrap tightly so they do not dry out. If using immediately, pile cooked crêpes on plate, one on top of the other to keep them moist.

title: PITA
instructions: Preheat convection oven to 400° F with a pizza stone inside. In bowl, combine water, maple syrup, and yeast. Proof for about 10 minutes or until mixture is foamy.
Add oil, salt, all-purpose flour, bread flour, and enough whole wheat pastry flour to create dough that pulls away from sides of bowl.
Knead dough about 10-15 minutes, using remaining pastry flour, until smooth dough has been created.
Transfer dough to clean, lightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Proof in warm place for 25-30 minutes or until dough has doubled in size.
While dough is proofing, prepare 3 pizza peels dusted with cornmeal.
Fold/turn dough. Rise dough again.
Divide dough into 8 equal-sized balls. Using rolling pin, roll each ball of dough into 1/8-inch x 6-inch circle and transfer to pizza peels.
Loosely cover dough with damp towels and set aside in warm place. Rise for about 15 minutes.
Bake pita for approximately 3-4 minutes or until puffed. Don’t let pita brown, as that makes them too crisp.

title: CAROB CAKE WITH WALNUTS
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups + 2 tablespoons whole wheat pastry flour
6 tablespoons canola OR melted coconut oil
3/4 cup almond milk OR soymilk, room temperature
1/2 cup walnuts, coarsely chopped (optional for top of cake)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Oil and flour one 8-inch cake pan and line bottom with parchment.
Combine dry ingredients in bowl and whisk together to combine. Set aside.
In separate bowl, whisk together wet ingredients and pour into dry. Mix well.
Pour batter into prepared baking pan.
Sprinkle walnuts on top.
Bake for 35 to 40 minutes or until toothpick inserted in center of cake comes out clean.

title: GENOISE
ingredients: 1 cup coconut sugar, measured then powdered in Vitamix
1 cup sifted whole wheat pastry flour
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Oil and flour two 8-inch cake pans and line bottoms with lightly buttered or oiled parchment.

Add eggs, coconut sugar, and vanilla to stand mixer bowl and beat at high speed for about 6-10 minutes until mixture has tripled in volume and has become light in color and foamy. Mixture should be almost consistency of stiff peaks.
Gently transfer egg mixture into clean large bowl. Sift flour in small batches (3 times) into egg mixture while folding batter with large rubber spatula.
Divide batter into 2 prepared pans.
Bake for about 15-20 minutes or until cake is golden brown and springy to touch.
Using paring knife, cut cake away from sides of pan immediately, leaving cake in pans to cool for 10 minutes.
Remove cakes from pans, remove parchment from bottoms of cakes, and cool completely on wire racks. Use cakes as two layers or split each of them and use as four layers.

title: SHORTBREAD COOKIES
instructions: Preheat oven to 300° F and line half sheet tray with parchment paper.
Cream butter, sugar, vanilla and salt in stand mixer.
Gradually sift in flour until well mixed.

Firmly press dough into flattened 6-inch oval in center of sheet tray. Cut dough into 16 equal-sized slices and pierce each slice with fork in decorative design (be sure to pierce through to pan to allow excess steam to escape).
Bake about 45-50 minutes or until entire dough is golden brown and firm to touch.
Remove from oven and cool on wire rack. Retrace cuts in dough to loosen cookies but allow to remain on sheet tray to fully cool (do not move cookies as they are very fragile at this stage).

title: BANANA CUPCAKES
ingredients: 1/2 banana, cut in thin rounds for topping
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection oven). Prepare miniature muffin tins with cupcake liners.
Cream butter and sugar together in stand mixer until light and fluffy. Add egg and mashed banana, mixing thoroughly.
Sift and whisk dry ingredients together in bowl; add to butter and egg mixture in mixer.
Whisk to combine milk, vinegar, and vanilla; add mixture to batter in mixer.
Fill cupcake liners using ice cream scoop, filling 2/3 full. Top with slice of banana.
Cook for 15-20 minutes. Check doneness by with toothpick in center (should come out clean).

title: BLACK AND WHITE COOKIES
ingredients: 3 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted and cooled
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection). Line 2 half-sheet pans with parchment paper.
Whisk together flours, baking powder, salt and lemon zest in bowl and set aside.
Beat eggs and sugar in bowl until smoothly blended. Add milk, butter, and vanilla extract.
Slowly add egg/sugar mixture into flour mixture, blending until smooth.
Using 1-ounce scoop, drop cookies 2 inches apart on sheet tray. Bake for about 15 minutes, until lightly golden and tops feel firm to touch.
Remove cookies to wire racks and cool at room temperature before frosting.
Whisk confectioner's sugar in bowl with 2-3 tablespoons boiling water until thick but smooth.

Remove frosting from heat and reserve 3 tablespoons of frosting to make 'black' frosting; use remainder to frost half of each cookie (if frosting becomes too thick, add drops of boiling water again until it is spreadable).
Melt chocolate in small bowl over simmering water. Stir in corn syrup.

Add reserved white frosting to chocolate and stir to spreadable consistency (adding more hot water, if necessary, to thin). Spread bare half of each cookie with chocolate frosting. Set cookies aside until frostings set.

title: GHIRARDELLI AWARD WINNING CHOCOLATE BROWNIES
instructions: Preheat oven to 375° F (325° F in convection oven). Grease and flour 8 x 8-inch square baking pan and line with parchment paper.
Whisk eggs with sugar and vanilla; add melted butter.
Whisk together dry ingredients (cocoa, flour, baking powder, and salt) and fold into egg mixture. Add nuts.
Bake for 20 minutes or until toothpick inserted in middle of brownie comes out clean.

title: GINGERBREAD MUFFINS
ingredients: 2 tablespoons light OR dark corn syrup
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection oven). Prepare miniature muffin tins with muffin papers. Place muffin tins on sheet pan and set aside.
In small saucepan, on low flame, heat and whisk together butter, molasses, and corn syrup until mixed and butter is melted. Add ginger juice and set aside.
In medium bowl, sift together flour, sugars, ground ginger, cinnamon, baking soda, and salt. Use whisk to incorporate ingredients.
Pour melted butter, molasses, corn syrup, and ginger juice into bowl with sifted dry ingredients. Stir to combine.
Pour batter into electric mixer, add eggs, and beat at medium speed for 1 1/2-2 minutes.
Using small ice cream scoop, fill muffin papers half way. Bake 10-12 minutes. Gently remove from pan. Cool completely.

title: Peanut Butter Cookies
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection oven). Prepare 2 sheet trays with parchment paper.
Cream sugars and butter in stand mixer until light and fluffy.
Add egg, peanut butter, and vanilla to butter-sugar mixture.
Whisk together flour, salt, and soda and add to mixer until dough is smooth.
Using 1-ounce ice cream scoop, place dough balls every 2 inches on half-sheet trays.
Press cookies flat with fork. Bake 15 minutes.

title: Big Chewy Oatmeal-Raisin Cookies
ingredients: 1 stick unsalted butter, softened but still firm
instructions: Heat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection oven). Prepare 2 half sheet trays with parchment paper.
In stand mixer, beat butter with sugars until creamy, approximately 3 minutes. Beat in egg.
Separately whisk flour, salt, baking powder, and nutmeg together. Add dry ingredients into butter-sugar mixture. Stir in oats and optional raisins.
Form dough into 24 (1-inch) balls using 1-ounce scoop, placing each cookie every 2 inches apart on sheet trays.
Bake until cookie edges turn golden brown, 20 to 25 minutes. Halfway during baking, rotate trays front to back and switch them from top to bottom.
Slide cookies cooling rack and let cool at least 15 minutes before serving.

title: Carrot Cake
ingredients: 1/3 cup (4 oz.) of crushed pineapple (drained and squeezed to extract all liquid)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection oven). Prepare miniature muffin tins with muffin papers.
Whisk dry ingredients together in large bowl.
Whisk together wet ingredients. Add raisins and walnuts.
Pour wet ingredients into dry ingredients and mix. Using 1-ounce ice cream scoop, fill muffin papers 2/3 full and bake for 25-30 minutes or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.

title: BANANA CUPCAKES
ingredients: 1/2 banana, cut in thin rounds for topping
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection oven). Prepare miniature muffin tins with cupcake liners.
Cream butter and sugar together in stand mixer until light and fluffy. Add egg and mashed banana, mixing thoroughly.
Sift and whisk dry ingredients together in bowl; add to butter and egg mixture in mixer.
Whisk to combine milk, vinegar, and vanilla; add mixture to batter in mixer.
Fill cupcake liners using ice cream scoop, filling 2/3 full. Top with slice of banana.
Cook for 15-20 minutes. Check doneness by with toothpick in center (should come out clean).

instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection oven). Prepare 2 sheet trays with parchment paper.
Cream sugars and butter in stand mixer until light and fluffy.
Add egg, peanut butter, and vanilla to butter-sugar mixture.
Whisk together flour, salt, and soda and add to mixer until dough is smooth.
Using 1-ounce ice cream scoop, place dough balls every 2 inches on half-sheet trays.
Press cookies flat with fork. Bake 15 minutes.

ingredients: 1 stick unsalted butter, softened but still firm
instructions: Heat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection oven). Prepare 2 half sheet trays with parchment paper.

Slide cookies cooling rack and let cool at least 15 minutes before serving.

title: Carrot Cake
ingredients: 1/3 cup (4 oz.) of crushed pineapple (drained and squeezed to extract all liquid)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F (300° F in convection oven). Prepare miniature muffin tins with muffin papers.
Whisk dry ingredients together in large bowl.
Whisk together wet ingredients. Add raisins and walnuts.
Pour wet ingredients into dry ingredients and mix. Using 1-ounce ice cream scoop, fill muffin papers 2/3 full and bake for 25-30 minutes or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.

title: Fudgy Chocolate Cake
ingredients: 6 tablespoons cocoa powder plus 2 teaspoons for dusting pan
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (300°F in convection). Oil one 8-inch cake pan. Dust pan with 2 teaspoons of cocoa powder. Line bottom of pan with parchment paper.
Sift together dry ingredients in medium bowl and whisk to combine. Set aside.
In separate bowl, whisk together wet ingredients and pour into dry. Mix well.
Add finely chopped chocolate and mix to combine. Pour batter into prepared pan.
Bake 35-40 minutes until cake is springy to touch and pulls away from sides.
Cool in pan on wire rack 10-15 minutes. Remove from pan and completely cool on rack.

title: Vanilla Cake
ingredients: 2 vanilla beans, split and paste extracted
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (300°F in convection oven). Oil and flour one 8-inch cake pan. Line bottom of pan with parchment.
Sift together dry ingredients in medium bowl and whisk together to combine. Set aside.
In separate bowl, whisk together wet ingredients and pour into dry. Mix well.
Pour batter into prepared pan.
Bake 30-40 minutes until cake is springy to touch and pulls away from sides of pan.
Cool in pan on wire rack for 10-15 minutes. Remove from pan and completely cool on rack.

title: Zesty Lemon Cake
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (300°F in convection). Oil and flour two 8-inch cake pans and line bottoms with parchment.
Sift together dry ingredients in medium bowl and whisk together to combine. Set aside.
In separate bowl, whisk together wet ingredients and pour into dry. Mix well.
Pour batter into prepared pans.
Bake for about 15-20 minutes until cakes are springy to touch and pull away from sides of pans.
Cool in pans on wire rack for 10-15 minutes. Remove from pans and completely cool on rack.

title: Orange Cake
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (300°F in convection). Oil and flour two 8-inch cake pans and line bottoms with parchment.
Sift together dry ingredients in medium bowl and whisk together to combine. Set aside.
In separate bowl, whisk together wet ingredients and pour into dry. Mix well.
Pour batter into prepared pans.
Bake for about 15-20 minutes until cakes are springy to touch and pull away from sides of pan.
Cool in pans on wire rack for 10-15 minutes. Remove from pans and completely cool on rack.

title: Genoise
ingredients: 1 cup whole wheat pastry flour, sifted
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (300°F in convection oven). Oil and flour two 8-inch cake pans and line bottoms with lightly oiled parchment.

If eggs are cold from refrigeration, combine them in medium bowl and set bowl atop pot of simmering water. Whisk eggs until they are room temperature (cold eggs do not achieve sufficient peak when beaten). Eggs should neither be cool nor warm to touch.

Add eggs, maple crystals, and vanilla to stand mixer bowl and beat at high speed for about 6-10 minutes until mixture has tripled in volume (foam should almost fill up bowl of mixer) and has become light in color and foamy. Mixture should be almost consistency of stiff peaks.
Gently transfer egg mixture into clean large bowl. Sift flour in small batches (3 times) into egg mixture while folding batter with large rubber spatula.
Divide batter between 2 prepared pans.
Bake for 15 minutes or until cake is uniformly golden brown and springy to touch.
Using paring knife, cut cake away from sides of pan immediately, leaving cake in pans to cool for 10 minutes.
Remove cakes from pans, remove parchment from bottoms of cakes, and cool completely on wire rack. Use each whole cake for two-layer cake or torte each cake to create four layers.

title: Spiced Carrot Cake
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups walnuts, lightly toasted and coarsely chopped
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (300°F in convection). Oil and flour two 8-inch cake pans and line bottoms with parchment.
Sift together dry ingredients in large bowl and whisk together to combine. Set aside.
In separate bowl, whisk together wet ingredients and pour into dry. Mix well. Add additional ingredients and mix to combine.
Pour batter into prepared pans.
Bake for about 45-50 minutes until cakes are springy to touch and pull away from sides of pans.
Cool in pans on wire rack for about 10-15 minutes. Remove from pans and allow to completely cool on rack.

title: Almond Torte
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Oil and flour 8-inch spring form pan.
Powder almonds in food processor. Add eggs, maple syrup and almond extract. Process until smooth.
Pour batter into pan.
Bake for 40 minutes. Top should be firm to touch and golden brown.

title: GINGER CAKE
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (300°F in convection). Oil and flour two 8-inch cake pans and line bottoms with parchment.
Sift together flour, baking soda, ginger, cinnamon, and cloves in medium bowl and whisk together to combine. Set aside.
Place softened butter in bowl of stand mixer. On low speed, mix just until butter is fluffy. Add sea salt and maple crystals; mix until creamy and well combined, about 2 minutes.

Add one egg at a time. Slowly add in maple syrup, orange juice, milk, and extracts. Combine on medium speed until well incorporated (mixture may look curdled, but will come back together once remaining ingredients are added).
Spoon combined dry ingredients into mixer a little at a time and mix until completely incorporated into butter mixture, scraping down sides of bowl as necessary.
Pour batter into 2 prepared pans. Bake about 20 minutes or until tops are golden brown and springy to touch.
Cool on wire rack for 10-15 minutes. Remove from pans and completely cool on rack.

title: BANANA CAKE
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (300°F in convection). Oil and flour two 8-inch cake pans and line bottom of pans with parchment.
In medium bowl, sift together dry ingredients. Whisk to combine.
In food processor, combine wet ingredient and process until smooth.
Using rubber spatula, transfer wet ingredients from food processor to bowl with dry ingredients. Mix to combine.
Pour batter into two prepared pans. Bake about 20-25 minutes or until top are golden brown, and cakes are springy to touch.
Cool on wire rack for 10-15 minutes. Remove from pans and completely cool on rack.

title: LEMON WHIPPED COCONUT CREAM
ingredients: 2 cans (5.4 ounces each) coconut cream, melted
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine coconut milk, agar, and coconut cream. Set aside for at least 5 minutes.
Prepare ice bath. Bring coconut mixture to simmer constantly stirring until all the agar is dissolved.
In Vitamix, combine coconut mixture, agave syrup, lemon zest, lemon juice, and salt. Blend until smooth. Slowly add coconut butter and blend to combine.
Transfer to bowl and set over ice bath until chilled.
Process frosting in food processor until smooth.

title: ORANGE WHIPPED COCONUT CREAM
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine coconut milk and agar. Set aside for at least 5 minutes.
Prepare ice bath. In Vitamix, combine melted coconut cream, agave syrup, orange zest, orange juice, and salt. Blend until smooth. Slowly add coconut butter and blend to combine.
Bring coconut-agar mixture to simmer. Simmer until all agar powder has dissolved, stirring constantly to avoid scorching.
Slowly add boiling coconut-agar mixture to Vitamix with other ingredients and blend to combine.
Using spatula, transfer mixture to medium bowl and set over ice bath until chilled.
Process frosting in food processor until smooth.

title: CHOCOLATE-PEANUT BUTTER FROSTING
ingredients: 8 ounces coconut cream (1 cup), room temperature
6 ounces semi-sweet dark chocolate (1 cup), ground, and melted
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine coconut milk and agar powder. Set aside for at least 5 minutes.
Prepare ice bath.
In food processor, combine coconut cream, peanut butter, maple crystals, cocoa powder, and salt. Blend until smooth.
Bring coconut milk-agar mixture to boil. Simmer until agar powder has dissolved, stirring constantly to avoid scorching.
Slowly and carefully add coconut milk-agar mixture and melted chocolate to processor with other ingredients and process to combine.
Using spatula, transfer contents from food processor to small bowl over ice bath until chilled.

title: CHOCOLATE-ALMOND GANACHE
instructions: Place chocolate in medium bowl.
In 2 1/2 quart pot, bring almond milk to boil and pour over chocolate, whisking vigorously, until chocolate melts and mixture is smooth.
Let ganache cool to room temperature.
Beat with stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment for several minutes, until ganache becomes lighter and somewhat thicker.
Chill ganache in refrigerator until it acquires spreadable consistency.

title: COCONUT-CASHEW FROSTING
ingredients: 37 ounces (4 1/2 cups) coconut milk, room temperature
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine coconut milk and agar flakes. Let sit for 10 minutes. Add salt, vanilla, and maple syrup.
Over medium-high heat, bring coconut milk mixture to boil, stirring often, while scraping agar from sides and bottom of pot.
Reduce heat to very low and simmer, partially covered, stirring and scraping often, until agar is dissolved, about 15 to 20 minutes.
Pour coconut mixture into shallow pan and chill until set (about 15 minutes in freezer or 30-40 minutes in refrigerator).
In food processor, combine coconut milk-agar mixture with cashew butter and coconut extract. Process until very smooth.

title: Lemon Soy Cream Frosting
ingredients: 2 pounds firm tofu, blanched and lightly pressed
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine juice, agar flakes, and salt. Let sit for 5 minutes. Simmer mixture until agar is fully dissolved, about 10 minutes.
In food processor, puree tofu with maple crystals until very creamy. Add maple syrup, oil, and extracts, and process until very smooth.
Slowly, with processor running, drizzle hot juice-agar mixture into tofu mixture. Fold in zest.
Transfer frosting to medium bowl and refrigerate to set up.

title: Meringue Frosting
instructions: In medium bowl, whisk egg whites, agave, vinegar, salt, and cream of tartar. Place bowl over 2 1/2 quart pot of boiling water. Make sure water underneath bowl is rapidly boiling.
Whisk mixture for about 10 minutes (mixture will become white and frothy).
Remove from heat and add vanilla extract.
Using hand mixer, whip at medium-high speed until light and fluffy, about 4-5 minutes.

title: Mocha Frosting
ingredients: 12 ounces (1 1/2 cups) coconut milk, room temperature
8 ounces coconut cream (1 cup), room temperature
instructions: Prepare coffee by combining instant coffee, salt, and boiling water. Set aside.
In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine coconut milk, prepared coffee, agar powder, and coconut cream. Set aside for 5 minutes.
Bring coconut milk-coffee mixture to boil. Lower heat and simmer until all agar powder has dissolved. Transfer to ice bath.
In food processor combine cashew butter and maple crystals. Add coconut-coffee mixture. Blend well until smooth.
Add melted chocolate and mix to combine. Transfer mixture to small bowl.
Place bowl over ice bath until mixture is firm.
Blend frosting in food processor until smooth and pipe-able.

title: Vegan Pastry Crust
ingredients: 2/3 cup unbleached all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
1/4 cup coconut oil, partially solid (refrigerate if necessary)
instructions: In medium bowl, sift together dry ingredients. Use whisk to fully combine ingredients.
Add oil to bowl. Using pastry cutter, blend oil into flour. Dough should have coarse, sand-like consistency.
Add vanilla and maple syrup to dough with wooden spoon. Mix to combine.
Slowly add water to dough one tablespoon at a time. Dough should just hold together and be moist (but not wet).
Place dough in plastic wrap, twisting so it holds together. Flatten to disc shape and refrigerate 10-15 minutes.
Remove dough from refrigerator; rest it until workable and can be rolled without cracking.

Lightly dust a sheet of parchment with all-purpose flour. Lay dough atop parchment and lightly dust surface of dough. Place second piece of parchment paper on top of dough, and roll out, flipping halfway. Remove second piece of parchment and continue rolling until dough is 1/8 inch thick.

Invert 9-inch pie plate over center of rolled out dough. Using sharp knife, cut a circle using cake pan as a guide. If dough is too soft, refrigerate briefly until it is firm enough to work with but not so firm that is cracks when assembling the galette.

title: KABOCHA SQUASH PIE
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F. Place squash cut side down on parchment-lined half sheet pan and bake until fork tender, approximately 1 hour.
Discard squash seeds and pulp (or save seeds for toasting). Scoop and reserve 2 cups of squash from its skin for this recipe.
Prepare an ice bath.

Puree squash, maple crystals, spices, and salt in food processor until smooth. Transfer mixture to 2 1/2 quart pot and slowly whisk in milk and cream. Simmer over medium heat, stirring constantly, until mixture thickens, about 3-5 minutes. Remove pot from heat and place in ice bath for 10 minutes before slowly whisking in eggs.
Pour filling into pie shell and bake for 30-40 minutes or until filling is lightly cracked around edges and center jiggles slightly. Cool for 1 hour before slicing.

title: SWEET POTATO PECAN PIE
ingredients: 3 tablespoons kuzu dissolved in 1/4 cup soymilk
1 tablespoon canola OR melted coconut oil
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Roll out pie dough and line pie plate. After dough has rested in refrigerator for 15 minutes, trim and form edges, cover and chill for another 30 minutes.
In bowl of food processor, combine yams, maple crystals, kuzu and soymilk, oil, spices, and extracts. Process until well blended, scraping sides of bowl often.

In 2 1/2 quart pot, over medium heat, bring agar and water to boil. Reduce heat, cover, and cook until agar is dissolved. Add agar mixture to yam mixture in food processor and blend for 1-2 minutes. Set aside.
Combine maple syrup and rice syrup and set aside.
Pour filling into pie shell. Arrange pecans on top of filling and drizzle syrup mixture over top of pie.
Loosely cover crust with tented foil to keep edges from browning too quickly.
Bake for 40 minutes or until knife inserted in center comes out clean and crust is firm. Remove foil from edges 10 minutes before removing from oven.
Cool pie to set filling before serving (about 30 minutes).

title: CRANBERRY PEAR PIE
ingredients: 1 pound pears OR apples, peeled, cored, diced
1 tablespoon + 1 1/2 teaspoons arrowroot dissolved in 2 tablespoons water
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.

Roll out first pie dough and line pie plate. After dough rests in refrigerator for 15 minutes, trim edges, cover, and chill for at least 30 minutes more. Roll out second dough and cut into 1/2 inch x 10 inch strips. Transfer strips to baking sheet, cover, and chill until ready to use.
In 2 1/2 quart sauce pan, combine cranberries, syrup, and orange juice. Bring to boil and reduce to simmer for 5 minutes or until all cranberries have popped. Add pears or apples.
Stir in arrowroot slurry and cook until mixture loses chalky appearance and thickens, about 1 minute. Transfer mixture to bowl to cool.
Spoon cooled filling into chilled pie shell. Arrange lattice strips on top. Trim ends of strips flush with overhang of shell and form edge.
Loosely cover crust with tented foil to keep edges from browning too quickly.
Bake for 35-40 minutes. Remove foil from edges 10 minutes before removing from oven.
Cool and set pie before serving (at least 30 minutes).

title: SWEET CRANBERRY TART
ingredients: 1 tablespoon kuzu dissolved in 1/3 cup orange juice
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, mix cranberries, maple syrup, and currants. Bring mixture to boil, reduce to simmer, and cook until all cranberries have popped.
In 2 1/2 quart pot, bring apple juice and agar to boil. Reduce heat and cook until agar is dissolved. Stir into cranberry mixture.
Mix together kuzu/orange juice slurry and orange zest. Stir kuzu slurry into cranberry mixture and cook until mixture is clear. Remove from heat and add walnuts. Chill to cool.
Spoon filling into baked tart shell and chill to set.

title: SWISS BURNT PECAN TART
ingredients: 3/4 cup rice syrup OR 1/2 cup honey
2 teaspoons barley malt syrup dissolved in 2 teaspoons water
instructions: Preheat oven to 275°F.
Roast pecans for about 40 minutes. Remove from pan and allow to completely cool.
Raise oven temperature to 350°F.
Roll out half the dough and transfer to tart pan. Chill 15 minutes. Trim edges, allowing 1-inch overhang. Chill rolled dough for another 15 minutes.
Roll out other half of dough and transfer to sheet pan. Cover and chill until ready to use.
In bowl of food processor, grind pecans to coarse flour. Add syrups, arrowroot, and vanilla. Mix to combine.
Pour filling into tart shell. Place top dough over filling, trim and seal edges. Cut a few steam vents.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until crust is firm. Brush top with glaze 10 minutes before removing from oven.
Cool and set before serving (about 30 minutes).

title: POACHED PEAR TART WITH CORNMEAL CRUST
ingredients: 1 tablespoon kuzu dissolved in 1/2 cup water
instructions: Poach pears in grape juice until fork tender. Transfer pears to plate and chill; reserve poaching liquid.

In 2 1/2 quart pot, simmer 1 1/2 cups poaching liquid with agar flakes until agar is dissolved. Stir in kuzu slurry and cook, stirring gently, until mixture turns clear. Remove from heat, cover with lid, and set aside.
Spread layer of raspberry jam in bottom of prepared crust. Arrange sliced or fanned pears on top of jam. Top with glaze. Chill until set (about 20 minutes).
In blender, puree raspberries and maple syrup. Strain out seeds and pour into squeeze bottles. Serve with tart.

title: CORNMEAL CRUST
ingredients: 1/4 cup canola OR melted coconut oil
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Combine cornmeal, pecans, and oats in food processor. Grind to fine meal.

Whisk together oil, maple syrup, and sea salt. Add wet ingredients to dry in processor until cookie-like dough is formed. Press dough into lightly oiled tart pan. Bake tart 12-15 minutes or until firm and lightly browned. Cool.

title: APPLE CRUMB PIE
ingredients: 1/3 cup canola OR melted coconut oil
2 1/2 pounds apples, peeled, cored, and thinly sliced
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Roll out pie dough and line pie plate. Trim and form edges, cover, and chill for at least 30 minutes.

In small bowl, whisk together flours. In separate bowl, whisk together oil and maple syrup. Slowly drizzle oil and maple syrup mixture into flour mixture. Stir lightly to form small, moist crumbs. Cover and chill.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil. Add apple slices and sauté until apples are slightly softened. Set aside to partially cool.
In large bowl, combine apples, maple syrup, lemon juice, vanilla, spices, salt, and flour. Toss until apples are well coated.
Place filling in pie shell. Top with crumbs. Loosely cover crust with tented foil to keep edges from browning too quickly.
Bake pie for 40-45 minutes or until apples are tender and crust is firm. Remove foil 10 minutes before removing pie from oven.
Cool pie before serving.

title: FRENCH WALNUT TART
ingredients: 1/3 cup rice syrup OR 1/2 cup honey
3 1/2 cups walnuts, toasted and coarsely chopped
1 tablespoon arrowroot dissolved in 2 tablespoons cold water
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.

Roll out first dough and transfer to tart pan; chill for 15 minutes. Trim edges of rolled dough, allowing 1-inch overhang. Cover and chill for 30 minutes more. Roll out other half of dough and transfer to sheet pan. Cover and chill until ready to use.
In one-gallon pot, combine sweeteners. Bring to boil, lower heat and add lemon juice, salt, almond milk, and walnuts.
Whisk in nut butter, extracts, and brandy. Add arrowroot slurry. Mix on medium low heat until mixture thickens slightly. Remove from heat and cool.
Pour walnut mixture into tart shell. Place top dough over filling, trim and seal edges. Cut a few steam vents.
Bake for 30-40 minutes until crust is firm and golden.
Cool before serving (about 25 minutes).

title: CARAMELIZED ONION TART
ingredients: 3-4 tablespoons unsalted butter OR olive oil
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter or oil 9-inch tart pan with removable bottom.
Roll crust between parchment; chill for 15 minutes. Transfer crust to prepared pan and rest in fridge again for 30 minutes more.
Heat butter or oil in 12-inch sauté pan over medium/low heat. Add onions and cook over (stirring occasionally) until caramelized, about 20 minutes.
While onions cook, half blind bake tart crust (with pie weights) for approximately 10-12 minutes (or until lightly golden).
Remove crust from oven; turn temperature down to 335°F.
Spread caramelized onions into tart shell (do not pat down).
Whisk together custard ingredients and pour over onions.
Bake until custard is set and crust is golden brown, approximately 12-15 minutes.

title: COCONUT-LIME CREAM TART
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F. Oil 9-inch tart pan with removable bottom.

Using your fingertips, blend together flour, macadamias, shredded coconut, maple crystals, salt and coconut oil until mixture resembles coarse meal. Press mixture into tart pan. Refrigerate for 15-20 minutes. Bake for 10-15 minutes or until golden brown. Set aside to cool.

Combine coconut milk into 2 1/2 quart saucepan with agar flakes, rum, maple syrup, brown rice syrup and salt. Bring to boil over medium heat, lower heat and simmer (whisking occasionally) about 30 minutes or until agar is completely dissolved.

In second 2 1/2 quart saucepan, combine semolina flour and cold water. Over high flame, whisk constantly until mixture thickens (consistency should resemble thick cream of wheat). Turn off heat and whisk into coconut milk mixture. Add lime juice and zest. Whisk to combine.
Pour filling into large bowl to cool, stirring constantly, to prevent mixture from setting up. When filling is warm (not hot), pour into cooled tart shell. Garnish with toasted coconut and lime zest.

title: SHEPHERD’S PIE
ingredients: 1 onion (12 ounces), cut into medium dice
3-4 tablespoons olive oil for browning seitan
1/2 pound seitan, ground in food processor
1 cup mushroom stock reduced to 1/2 cup
1 teaspoon arrowroot dissolved in 3 teaspoons cold water
2 pounds peeled Yukon gold potatoes, large dice
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Partially 'blind bake' pie shell (about 10 minutes) and set aside.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat olive oil. Add onion, garlic, carrots, parsnips, and fresh thyme. Sauté until slightly tender. Add couple tablespoons water to deglaze pan.
Mix together sesame oil, tamari, and mustard; stir into vegetable mixture in sauté pan. Continue to cook another 10 minutes, and transfer mixture to bowl.
In separate 10-inch sauté pan, heat olive oil, add seitan and cook until seitan is slightly browned. Add wine and continue cooking until wine is absorbed. Add seitan to bowl with cooked vegetables.

Thicken reduced stock by adding arrowroot slurry and simmering until mixture thickens. Add this mixture to bowl containing vegetables and seitan. Season mixture with salt and pepper to taste. Set aside to cool.
In 1-gallon pot, boil potatoes until tender. Drain. Process potatoes through ricer or food mill. Season with soy milk, butter or olive oil, salt and pepper. Cover and set aside.
Fill partially baked pie shell with vegetable/seitan mixture. Using pastry bag fitted with tip, pipe potato mixture onto top of pie.
Bake for about 20-25 minutes or until vegetables are fork tender and crust is fully baked. Remove from oven and garnish with chopped parsley.

title: GLUTEN-FREE SWEET POTATO PECAN TART
ingredients: 1 cup pecans, lightly toasted and cooled
3 tablespoons gluten free breadcrumbs mixed with 2 tablespoons maple crystals
1 1/2 pounds garnet yams (2 large) cooked and mashed (about 2 1/4 cups)
1 3/4 cups whole pecans OR 1 cup thick coconut flakes
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F and lightly grease tart pan with small amount of softened butter or oil.

Grind pecans and maple crystals to fine meal in food processor. Add flours and pulse to combine about 30 seconds more. Transfer mixture from food processor to bowl. In separate bowl, whisk together melted butter, water and, salt. Pour wet mixture into dry and mix to combine. If mixture is too moist, refrigerate for 5-10 minutes before pressing out crust.
Press crust mixture into tart pan. Refrigerate 15-20 minutes or until crust is firm to touch. Pierce crust with fork and partially bake for 15-20 minutes. Cool before filling.
Before filling tart shell, sprinkle breadcrumbs and maple crystals over crust (this prevents crust from getting soggy).
In food processor, puree yams. Add syrup, eggs, cream, vanilla, ginger juice, ground spices, and salt. Blend until mixture is creamy and smooth. Transfer puree to partially baked pie shell.
Decorate top of tart with pecans and brush nuts with maple syrup or top with coconut (omitting maple syrup wash).
Bake tart 30-35 minutes until filling is just firm. Allow to cool completely before slicing.

title: VEGAN PASTRY CRUST
ingredients: 2/3 cup unbleached all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
1/4 cup coconut oil, partially solid (refrigerate if necessary)
instructions: In medium bowl, sift together dry ingredients. Use whisk to fully combine ingredients.
Add oil to bowl. Using pastry cutter; blend oil completely into flour. Dough should have coarse, sand-like consistency with no chunks of oil.
Add vanilla and maple syrup to dough with plastic spatula. Mix to combine.
Slowly add ice water to dough one tablespoon at a time. Dough should just hold together and be moist (but not wet).
Transfer dough to plastic wrap, twisting so it holds together. Flatten to disc shape and refrigerate 10-15 minutes.
Remove dough from refrigerator; rest dough on table until workable and can be rolled out without cracking.

Lightly dust a sheet of parchment with all-purpose flour. Lay dough atop parchment and lightly dust surface of dough. Place second piece of parchment paper on top of dough, and roll out, flipping halfway through rolling. Remove second piece of parchment and continue rolling until dough is 1/8 inch thick.

title: BUTTER CRUST
instructions: Sift then mix flour and salt into bowl. Using two knives or pastry cutter, cut butter into flour to form very coarse meal.
With rubber spatula, lightly toss flour while sprinkling water in mixture 1 tablespoon at a time until dough forms. Wrap in plastic and chill 20 to 30 minutes.
Roll on lightly floured parchment to desired shape.

title: OLIVE OIL CRUST
instructions: In medium bowl, sift together flour, salt and baking powder.

Cut oil into flour mixture all at once but do not mix. Toss flour around lightly coating fat to create little balls of flour coated fat. A light touch is important here. Final blending will take place on pastry board in rolling.
Add just enough water (one tablespoon at a time) to form ball, being careful not over-mix and create too much gluten.
Flatten dough into disc, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for 15 minutes to relax the dough. Roll to desired shape. Transfer rolled dough to pie or tart pan and chill for 30 minutes or more.
Heat oven to 375°F, fill crust and bake.

title: SESAME PRESSED CRUST
ingredients: Chilled juice OR water to bind as needed
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Oil 9-inch pie or tart pan.
Process together whole wheat pastry flour, oatmeal, sesame seeds and salt until mixture is uniformly flour-like.
Whisk together oil and syrup.
Add oil-syrup mixture to processor and mix until thoroughly combined. Add juice or water if more liquid is needed.
With wet fingertips, press mixture into oiled pie plate.
Bake 20 to 25 minutes, until golden.

title: PRESSED NUT CRUST
ingredients: 1/4 cup canola oil OR 2 tablespoons melted coconut oil
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Oil 9-inch pie or tart pan.
Grind almonds in food processor to form fine almond meal.
Grind oats coarsely in food processor.
In medium bowl, combine almonds, oats, oat flour, cinnamon and salt; mix well.
In separate bowl, whisk together maple syrup, and canola or melted coconut oil.
Mix wet ingredients into dry ingredients.
With wet fingertips, press crust into oiled pie or tart pan.
Bake crust 20 to 25 minutes or until golden and completely baked.

title: RICH ALMOND MILK
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, bring water to boil. Add almonds and remove pot from flame. Let sit covered for 20-30 minutes.
Transfer almonds and water to Vitamix. Process for few minutes until well homogenized.
Strain milk through nut bag or chinois lined with 2 layers of moistened cheesecloth. Reserve almond pulp and set aside.
Add vanilla and salt. Whisk to combine.

title: ALMOND PASTRY CREAM
ingredients: 1 tablespoon kuzu, dissolved in 2 tablespoons water
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, combine agar, water, and maple syrup. Let mixture sit for 10 minutes.

Bring agar mixture to boil. Lower heat and simmer uncovered or with pan lid slightly ajar until agar is dissolved, about 15 minutes. Stir often, scraping agar from sides and bottom of saucepan with spoon. Whisk mixture as necessary to break up bits of un-dissolved agar.
Stir almond milk, salt, and vanilla extract into agar mixture. Bring to boil, whisking often.
Lower heat and add kuzu slurry, whisking vigorously until mixture comes back to simmer. Cook a few minutes longer.
Pour mixture into hotel pan and chill in freezer or refrigerator until set.

In food processor, puree chilled mixture until very smooth and creamy. Halfway through processing, stop machine and scrape sides of work bowl with rubber spatula. If sweeter cream is desired, add additional maple syrup to taste.
Chill almond cream thoroughly, if thicker texture is desired.

title: HAZELNUT-MOCHA AND ALMOND PARFAIT
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, combine water, espresso, almond milk, and agar flakes. Let mixture sit 10 minutes or more. Over high heat, bring mixture to boil. Lower heat and simmer uncovered or with pan lid slightly ajar until agar is dissolved, scraping sides and bottom of pan often with spoon.
Add maple syrup, salt, vanilla extract, and chocolate chips, whisking until smooth. Simmer a few minutes longer.
Pour mixture into hotel pan and transfer to freezer or refrigerator until set.
Puree mixture with hazelnut butter in food processor until very smooth. Stop machine several times and scrape sides of work bowl with rubber spatula. Chill puree thoroughly.
To serve, spoon into glasses, alternating with Almond Pastry Cream (recipe preceding) and garnish with hazelnuts.

title: Chocolate Pudding
ingredients: 3 tablespoons kuzu, dissolved in 1/2 cup water
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, combine almond milk and agar flakes. Let mixture sit 10 minutes. Bring mixture to boil, lower heat, and simmer uncovered or partially covered until agar is fully dissolved, about 10 minutes. Stir often, scraping sides and bottom of pot with spoon to dissolve agar.
Whisk in cocoa powder, salt, maple syrup, vanilla, and chocolate chips. Bring mixture to boil.

Whisk kuzu slurry into saucepan with cocoa mixture, stirring constantly until liquid returns to boil. Continue to cook 1 minute longer until mixture clears (loses chalky appearance) and thickens. Remove from heat.
Pour cocoa mixture into hotel pan and cool in freezer or refrigerator until firm.
Transfer cocoa mixture to food processor and puree until very smooth and creamy.

title: Avocado and Coconut Terrine
ingredients: 3 cups coconut milk, room temperature (two 13.66-ounce cans)
instructions: Avocado layer: Blend avocados, lime juice, salt, coconut milk, coconut cream, and agave syrup in Vitamix until creamy.

Transfer mixture to ice cream machine and freeze until scoop-able consistency is achieved. Transfer mixture to half hotel pan and freeze for 20 minutes or until mixture firms to scoop-able consistency.
Coconut layer: In medium bowl, whisk together coconut milk, salt, vanilla extract, coconut extract, and maple syrup.
Transfer to ice cream machine and freeze until scoop-able consistency is achieved. Transfer to half hotel pan and freeze until mixture firms to scoop-able consistency.

Brush terrine mold lightly with oil and line with parchment paper. Spread avocado sorbet evenly in bottom of terrine mold. Swirl in coconut sorbet. Smooth surface of terrine and transfer to freezer to set until firm.

title: Orange Sorbet
instructions: In medium bowl, whisk together orange juice, maple syrup, rice syrup, lemon juice, vodka, and sea salt.

Pour mixture into ice cream machine and freeze until scoop-able consistency is achieved. When mixture starts to firm up, add zest and continue to churn until incorporated. Transfer sorbet to freezer to firm consistency.

title: TRI-COLOR FRUIT TERRINE
ingredients: 2 large, ripe frozen bananas, peeled (2 cups)
1 cup stemmed, frozen strawberries (8 ounces) OR 1/2 bag (10-ounce) frozen strawberries
2 cups pitted frozen cherries (1 1/2 pounds) OR 1 (10-ounce) bag frozen cherries
instructions: Lightly brush terrine mold with oil and line with parchment paper, making sure inside of pan is covered entirely.

For first layer of terrine, process pineapple and one banana (broken into pieces) through Champion juicer using blank screen. Spoon pineapple-banana mixture into terrine mold and spread with offset spatula until surface is flat and even. Transfer terrine to freezer while preparing next layer.

For second layer, process frozen strawberries and remaining banana (broken into pieces) through Champion juicer using blank screen. Adjust flavor of blended fruit with lemon juice and maple syrup to taste, stirring well to combine. Spoon and smooth strawberry-banana mixture atop pineapple-banana mixture. Transfer terrine to freezer while preparing next layer.

For final layer, repeat previous step with frozen cherries. Add maple syrup to cherry puree and spoon atop strawberry-banana mixture. Cover terrine with plastic wrap, and freeze entire terrine until firm to touch (1 1/2 to 2 hours).
To serve, turn terrine out of mold and remove parchment. Allow terrine to thaw slightly (about 2 minutes) before slicing.

title: FRENCH VANILLA ICE CREAM
ingredients: 1 whole vanilla bean, split and seeded, pods reserved
instructions: Prepare ice bath.

In 2-quart saucepan, combine milk, 1/4 cup of maple syrup, sea salt, vanilla pulp, and vanilla pod. Whisk mixture to combine. Heat mixture on medium flame over flame tamer, stirring occasionally, until temperature reaches 175°F. Remove from heat.

While milk mixture is warming, combine remaining 1/4 cup maple syrup, rice syrup, egg yolk, and vanilla extract in medium-sized bowl. Beat with electric hand mixer until contents turn pale yellow and form semi-thick ribbons (about 5 minutes).

Slowly and gradually whisk 1/2 cup of hot milk mixture into beaten egg mixture, being careful not to cook yolks. Then carefully whisk milk and egg combination back into pot with remaining milk mixture. Heat over medium flame, stirring constantly, until temperature reaches 180°F (remove from heat to accurately take temperature).
Remove vanilla pod. Strain mixture into non-reactive bowl. Whisk in heavy cream.
Cool mixture in ice bath. Pour mixture into ice cream machine and freeze to scoop-able consistency.

title: CHOCOLATE GELATO
instructions: Prepare ice bath.
In 1-gallon pot, heat milk, heavy cream, and 3/4 cup of maple crystals until crystals dissolve and milk starts to simmer.
Whisk in cocoa powder and bittersweet chocolate. Set aside.
Combine egg yolks and remaining 3/4 cup of maple crystals in electric mixer fitted with paddle attachment. Beat on high speed for 3-5 minutes or until mixtures lightens in color and mixture thickens.
Lower speed on mixer and slowly pour chocolate-cream mixture into egg mixture.
Transfer combined mixtures to 2 1/2 quart saucepan and simmer over low heat stirring constantly until thickened (candy thermometer should read at about 180 degrees). Do not bring mixture to boil.
Strain mixture into medium bowl through nut bag or chinois lined with double layer of moistened cheesecloth, and then stir in Kahlua, vanilla, and salt.
Transfer mixture to ice bath and cool to room temperature.
Pour mixture into ice cream machine and churn until firm and scoop-able.

title: BLACK SESAME SEED ICE CREAM
ingredients: 3 tablespoons black sesame seeds, toasted then ground
instructions: Prepare ice bath. Combine coconut milk, maple syrup, rice syrup, salt, and sesame seeds in pot over high heat. When mixture boils, turn heat down and whisk for 1-2 minutes. Remove mixture from heat, and transfer pot to ice bath to cool mixture to room temperature.
Pour mixture into ice cream machine and churn until creamy and somewhat firm.

title: Basil Ice Cream
instructions: Prepare ice bath. Bring water to boil. Add salt. Blanch basil for 2-3 seconds then shock in ice bath. Drain basil and completely wring water from it.
Combine coconut milk, maple syrup, rice syrup, vanilla extract, lemon zest, salt and basil in blender.
Process mixture until well emulsified.
Pour mixture into ice cream machine and churn until creamy and somewhat firm.

title: Orange Blossom Panna Cotta
ingredients: 3 tablespoons + 1 teaspoon agar flakes, combined with 1 1/2 cups water (soaked for 10 minutes)
1/2 cup honey, whisked with 2 tablespoons water
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, combine heavy cream, maple syrup, and salt. Cook uncovered on low simmer, stirring constantly for about 5-7 minutes. Turn off heat and set aside.

Pour soaked agar and water mixture into separate 2 1/2 quart pot. Bring to boil, cover pot, lower heat, and simmer until all agar is completely dissolved, about 5 minutes. Add to pot with heavy cream mixture and cook 2-3 minutes longer. Add orange blossom water and orange zest.
Add buttermilk to heavy cream/agar mixture.
Ladle mixture into 4-ounce ramekins. Refrigerate until set.
Combine orange supreme with rice syrup and serve as garnish with nuts.

title: Tapioca Pudding
ingredients: 1/2 cup large pearl tapioca, soaked in 2 cups water for 30 minutes, drained
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, combine soaked and drained tapioca, almond milk, salt, rice syrup, and maple syrup. Whisk together. Bring mixture to simmer over burner with flame tamer, stirring constantly to keep tapioca from sticking to bottom of pot.
Continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until mixture starts to thicken, and tapioca just becomes translucent, about 10-15 minutes. Do not overcook or mixture will become glue-like.
Stir in vanilla extract, cinnamon, nutmeg, lemon zest, and lemon juice. Remove from heat. Let mixture cool down, and chill further in refrigerator.

title: Almond Pulp Cookies
ingredients: 2 cups almond pulp (left over from making Rich Almond Milk)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Combine all ingredients in bowl with spatula.
Form cookies on parchment-lined 1/2 sheet pan with 1-ounce scoop.
Bake cookies 10-12 minutes, or until lightly golden.

title: WATERMELON GRANITA
ingredients: 6 cups watermelon (about 2 pounds), seeded and cut into chunks
instructions: Blend watermelon in Vitamix and strain out excess pulp with nut bag or chinois lined with 2 layers of moistened cheesecloth.
Transfer watermelon juice to medium bowl and add lime juice, agave, and salt.
Pour mixture into half hotel pan (mixture should be about 1/4 inch up side of pan) and transfer to freezer for about 1 hour.
