Remove pan from freezer and use fork to scrape any ice crystals that have formed on side or bottom of pan. Return to freezer and repeat scraping every 30 minutes for 3-4 hours.
Once mixture is thoroughly frozen, scrape once more with fork, returning granita to “dry” in freezer for another half hour before serving.
Garnish granita with mint sprigs and serve.

title: COFFEE GRANITA
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart sauce pan, bring water to boil. Add instant coffee and maple crystals and stir until coffee and maple crystals are dissolved. Add Kahlua and salt. Whisk to combine and remove from heat.
Pour mixture into half hotel pan (mixture should be about 1/4 inch up side of pan) and transfer to freezer for about 1 hour.
Remove pan from freezer and use fork to scrape any ice crystals that have formed on side or bottom of pan. Return to freezer and repeat scraping every 30 minutes for 3-4 hours.
Once mixture is thoroughly frozen, scrape once more with fork, returning granita to “dry” in freezer for another half hour before serving.

title: STRAWBERRY SORBET
instructions: In blender, puree strawberries, maple syrup, agave syrup, and balsamic vinegar until smooth. Transfer mixture to medium bowl and whisk in salt, pepper, and basil.

Transfer mixture to ice cream machine and freeze according to directions. Remove sorbet from canister and transfer to 9-pan. Freeze forty minutes to one hour or until sorbet firms to scoop-able consistency.

title: GRAPEFRUIT-CAMPARI GRANITA
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, over low flame, simmer juice with water and maple crystals until maple crystals dissolve. Add Campari and stir.
Pour mixture into half hotel pan (mixture should be 1/4 inch up side of pan) and transfer to freezer for about 1 hour.
Remove pan from freezer and use fork to scrape any ice crystals that have formed on side or bottom of pan. Return to freezer and repeat scraping every 30 minutes for 3-4 hours.
Once mixture is thoroughly frozen, scrape once more with fork, returning granita to “dry” in freezer for another half hour before serving.

title: POMEGRANATE GRANITA
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, over low flame, simmer pomegranate and orange juices with maple crystals until crystals dissolve.
Pour mixture in half hotel pan (mixture should be 1/4 inch up side of pan) and transfer to freezer for about 1 hour.
Remove pan from freezer and use fork to scrape any ice crystals that have formed on side or bottom of pan. Return to freezer and repeat scraping every 30 minutes for 3-4 hours.
Once mixture is thoroughly frozen, scrape once more with fork, returning granita to 'dry' in freezer for another half hour before serving.

title: GREEN APPLE SORBET
ingredients: 8 medium Granny Smith apples (4 pounds), chopped to fit through feeding tube juicer
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot over medium flame, simmer thyme leaves, agave, water, and salt for 15 minutes.
Remove pot from flame. Pour mixture into blender and puree to combine. Strain and set aside in medium bowl.
In separate bowl, combine juiced lemons with agave-thyme mixture.
Juice apples in Champion juicer and combine with lemon juice and agave-thyme mixture.
Transfer mixture to ice cream machines and freeze until scoop-able consistency is achieved. Transfer sorbet to 9-pan and freeze forty minutes to one hour or until sorbet firms up.

title: RAW "CHEESECAKE"
instructions: Line 9-inch spring-form pan with parchment paper.
In food processor combine dates, pecans, coconut oil, and sea salt. Process until dough binds when pressed with fingers. Do not over-blend.
Press dough evenly into bottom of spring-form pan.
Freeze until crust is set, about 15 minutes.
Drain and rinse cashews, discarding soaking water.
In high-powered blender, puree cashews, coconut milk, agave nectar, coconut oil, lemon juice, vanilla bean, and salt. Blend until very smooth.
Decorate surface of cheesecake with droplets or lines of puree.
Using skewer or dinner knife, swirl mixture to create marbled effect.
Freeze cheesecake until solid.

title: BLUEBERRY PUREE
ingredients: 1 cup fresh or frozen and thawed blueberries
instructions: Puree blueberries and agave nectar in blender until smooth.

title: RASPBERRY PUREE
instructions: Puree raspberries and maple syrup in blender until smooth. Strain puree to remove seeds.

title: MANGO PUREE
ingredients: 1 ripe mango, peeled, pitted, and chopped
instructions: Puree mango, lime juice, and agave nectar in blender until smooth.

title: CARAMEL
instructions: Combine all ingredients in sauté pan. Bring sauce to simmer and cook uncovered for 10-12 minutes until sauce is reduced and thick enough to coat back of spoon. Remove from heat and let cool completely.

title: Mint-Chocolate Ganache Truffles
instructions: Bring coconut milk and salt to simmer in 2-quart pot.
Remove coconut milk from heat. Add chocolate and mint extract. Stir with spatula until smooth.
Pour chocolate mixture into shallow baking pan and chill in freezer for 30 minutes, or until firm enough to scoop.
Form 1-inch diameter truffles using small half-ounce scoop or teaspoon.
Return truffles to freezer to firm.
Dip truffles in Chocolate Coating (recipe following). Serve truffles at room temperature.

title: Lime-Sea Salt Chocolate Ganache Truffles
instructions: Bring coconut milk, 1/4 tsp lime zest, and 1/4 tsp salt to simmer in 2-quart pot.
Remove coconut milk from heat. Add chocolate. Stir with spatula until smooth.
Pour chocolate mixture into shallow baking pan and chill in freezer for 30 minutes, or until firm enough to scoop.
Form 1-inch diameter truffles using small half-ounce scoop or teaspoon.
Return truffles to freezer to firm.
Dip truffles in Chocolate Coating (recipe following) and garnish with lime zest and sea salt. Serve truffles at room temperature.

title: Chocolate Coating (for Truffles)
ingredients: 2 cups finely chopped semisweet chocolate, divided
instructions: Create double boiler: Fill 1-gallon pot with 4 cups water. Bring water to simmer. Cover pot with heatproof bowl. Combine 1 1/3 cups chocolate and coconut oil in bowl and stir until chocolate is completely melted and smooth.
Immediately remove bowl from pot and wipe moisture from bottom of bowl with dry towel. Stir in remaining 2/3 cup chocolate until chocolate is again smooth.
Using slotted spoon, dip truffles one by one into melted chocolate. Let excess chocolate drip off.
Transfer covered truffle to parchment-lined sheet pan. Sprinkle with garnish (crushed pistachios, toasted coconut, or lime zest/sea salt).
Repeat process with remaining truffles. Refrigerate until chocolate coating has set.

title: Salted Pecan and Rosemary Brittle
instructions: Line 2 half-sheet pans with Silpats and set aside. Have 2 more Silpats ready for use.
In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, combine brown rice syrup, water, coconut oil, agave, and salt. Set pot over medium flame, stirring occasionally, until mixture comes to boil. Reduce heat to simmer.
Once mixture reaches 260°F as measured on candy thermometer, add rosemary. Do not stir mixture again but swirl pot occasionally and keep cooking until candy thermometer reads 280°F.
Stir in nuts and mix well. Remove mixture from heat and thoroughly stir in baking soda.

Quickly divide brittle mixture between 2 half-sheet pans, using lightly oiled spatula. Spread brittle into thin even layer. Place second large Silpat on top of brittle and roll more thinly with rolling pin.
Let brittle cool completely, about 30 minutes. If making brittle on hot, humid day in kitchen without air conditioning, then you may have to cool brittle in refrigerator.
When cooled, turn brittle upside down from Silpat onto parchment paper. Break brittle in 1/4-inch sized pieces.

title: Salted Almond and Thyme Brittle
instructions: Line 2 half-sheet pans with Silpat and set aside. Have 2 more Silpats ready for use.
In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, combine brown rice syrup, water, coconut oil, agave, and salt. Set pot over medium flame, stirring occasionally, until mixture comes to boil. Reduce heat to simmer.
Once mixture reaches 260°F as measured on candy thermometer, add thyme. Do not stir mixture again but swirl pot occasionally and keep cooking until candy thermometer reads 280°F.
Stir in nuts and mix well. Remove mixture from heat and thoroughly stir in baking soda.

Quickly divide brittle mixture between 2 half-sheet pans, using lightly oiled spatula. Spread brittle into thin even layer. Place second large Silpat on top of brittle and roll more thinly with rolling pin.
Let brittle cool completely, about 30 minutes. If making brittle on hot, humid day in kitchen without air conditioning, then you may have to cool brittle in refrigerator.
When cooled, turn brittle upside down from Silpat to parchment paper. Break brittle in 1/4-inch sized pieces.

title: Chocolate Bark
ingredients: 2 cups ground bittersweet chocolate (10 oz chocolate ground in food processor = about 2 cups)
Sprinkle of coarsely ground sea salt
instructions: Line half-sheet pan with parchment paper. Prepare ice bath in small bowl. Have offset spatula and candy thermometer at the ready.
Bring 4 cups water to simmer in 2-quart pot.

Pour 1 1/2 cups ground chocolate in small bowl. Set bowl over simmering water and whisk chocolate until completely melted. Remove bowl from heat and take temperature of melted chocolate. Temperature should not exceed 125°F. Between 110 and 125°F is best.
When chocolate is in 110 and 125°F range, whisk in remaining 1/2 cup of ground chocolate. Cool chocolate to 85°F, transferring bowl to ice bath, if necessary.

Transfer bowl atop simmering water again, and whisk chocolate until it reaches temperature between 87 and 91°F degrees (be careful as temperature rises quickly). If temperature goes too high, again use ice bath to cool it quickly.
Pour chocolate on parchment-lined half-sheet pan. Spread with offset spatula to 1/8-inch thickness.
Sprinkle nuts, dried fruit, cacao nibs, and salt over surface.
Cool bark in refrigerator and break into pieces.

title: Flourless Chocolate Cake
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups date sugar, ground to fine powder
1/2 cup boiling water mixed 1 heaping tablespoon instant coffee
2 sticks butter (8 ounces), room temperature, cut into pieces
instructions: Heat convection oven to 325°F. Brush 9-inch spring-form pan with oil. Line bottom with parchment paper and outside, bottom of pan with tin foil.
Grind chocolate in food processor to fine meal, add date sugar, and continue to grind until well combined.
Slowly pour hot coffee into food processor through feeder tube and pulse until chocolate is melted.
Add butter pieces and cocoa powder. Pulse to combine. Add eggs, salt, and vanilla. Process until smooth. Batter should be pourable and creamy.
Pour batter into spring-form pan. Bake on half-sheet pan as a little batter may seep out.
Bake about 45 minutes or until puffed and lightly cracked. Use toothpick to check for doneness.

title: Flourless Coconut Cake
ingredients: 2 cups dried coconut, ground to fine meal, toasted, and cooled
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F. Brush 8-inch cake pan with oil and line with parchment.

In Kitchen Aid, whip coconut sugar and egg yolks about 5 minutes or until ribbons form. Add dried coconut and salt and carefully mix to combine. Remove bowl with egg yolks and coconut sugar from Kitchen Aid and set aside.
Fit Kitchen Aid with new, clean bowl. Whip egg whites until stiff peaks form. Using rubber spatula, carefully fold egg whites into other bowl with sugar and egg yolks. Transfer batter to cake pan.
Bake about 30 minutes or until cake is golden and set in middle.

title: ROLLED FIG COOKIES
ingredients: ¾ cup whole wheat pastry flour
¼ cup + 2 tablespoons blanched almonds, ground to fine meal (measure then grind)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line two half-sheet trays with parchment paper and set aside.
In food processor, sift together flours, almond meal, and cinnamon. In small bowl, whisk together salt, syrup, and oil.
Add wet mixture to dry in food processor. Pulse to combine.
Divide dough evenly in half. Flatten dough into 2 discs and wrap each in plastic wrap. Refrigerate 10-15 minutes to firm.
In 2-quart pot, combine figs, apple juice, vanilla, cinnamon, cloves, and salt. Simmer 5-7 minutes or until figs are softened. Drain figs, reserving cooking liquid.
Puree fig mixture in food processor, adding cooking liquid as necessary, to make smooth, spreadable paste. Set aside to cool.
Roll each dough separately on parchment paper (dough should be about 1/8 inch thick. Trim excess dough so that dough roughly measures 10” by 6” rectangle.

Spread cooled or room temperature filling over rolled out dough. Roll dough jelly roll style as tightly as possible. Wrap dough in parchment paper and transfer to freezer for 15-20 minutes until hard.
Using bread knife, slice dough into ¼-inch rounds. Transfer to sheet trays and bake 10-15 minutes or until lightly golden. Cool and serve.

title: SESAME ANISE BISCOTTI
ingredients: ¾ cup + 1 tablespoon unbleached all-purpose flour, sifted
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line half-sheet tray with parchment paper.
In food processor, grind almonds to fine meal. In spice grinder, grind sesame seeds and anise seeds into fine powder. Add sesame seeds, anise, and baking powder to food processor and process.
In separate small bowl, whisk together oil, syrup, extracts, and sea salt.

Add wet ingredients to dry in food processor. Pulse until dough comes together. Separate dough into 2 equal parts. Pour sesame seeds on plate. Gently press and roll logs in sesame seeds to cover all sides.
On cutting board, shape both doughs into perfectly matching rectangles (approximately 3 inches wide by 6 inches long) using 2 dough scrapers. Freeze dough before slicing.
Using sharp chef’s knife, slice each log into ¼ inch thick slices.
Transfer slices on sheet tray to oven and cook approximately 7 minutes each side or until thoroughly golden and crisp.

title: JAM DOT COOKIES
ingredients: ½ cup extra virgin canola oil, olive oil, melted butter OR 6 tablespoons melted coconut oil
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line 2 half-sheet trays with parchment paper.
Grind almonds, flour, oats, and cinnamon in food processor to fine meal.
In separate small bowl, whisk together oil, maple syrup, and sea salt. Add liquid to dry mixture in food processor and mix until well combined.

Form cookies using level 1-ounce scoop. Round them into walnut sized balls. Place on baking sheet and form indentation in middle of cookie using floured ¼ teaspoon measure. Fill indentation with about ½ teaspoon of jam and bake 15-20 minutes or until golden.

title: COCONUT MACAROONS
ingredients: 3 ½ cups shredded coconut (pulsed in food processor to fine powder)
½ cup finely chopped pistachios or almonds
instructions: Preheat oven to 325° F. Line 2 half-sheet pans with parchment and lightly oil or use Silpats.

In 2 ½ quart pot, combine maple crystals, water, and salt. Bring to boil and reduce to simmer. Cook until mixture thickens and reaches softball stage (about 20-25 minutes/234-240°F). Soft ball stage is reached when small droplet of syrup poured into ice water and forms soft, pliable ball. Remove from heat, cover mixture, and set aside to cool to about 155°F before combining with beaten eggs.
Combine egg whites and vanilla in medium bowl and beat with whisk by hand to form stiff peaks.
Very gradually, fold beaten egg whites into slightly cooled syrup mixture just until combined. Carefully fold in pulsed coconut. Refrigerate dough 10-15 minutes to let set before scooping.

Form cookies with 1-ounce scoop, rounding each into a ball, and then rolling them in crushed pistachios. Transfer cookies to lightly oiled, parchment lined baking sheets. Bake for 15-20 minutes or until firm and bottoms of cookies are slightly golden.

title: WALNUT TEA COOKIES
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Line 2 half-sheet trays with parchment paper.
Cream butter and maple crystals in stand mixer until fluffy and well combined, about 5 minutes. Add vanilla and salt until mixed.

In separate smaller bowl, whisk together flours. Slowly sift flours into butter mixture in stand mixer, until all flour is incorporated. Add in walnuts. Divide dough in 2 and wrap each in plastic. Refrigerate 10-15 minutes.
Remove 1 dough from refrigerator. Roll between parchment paper to 1/4" thickness. Freeze dough until hard. Cut shapes from dough using cookie cutters. Repeat process with second dough.
Bake about 6 minutes. Rotate baking pans and continue to bake about 5-6 more minutes or until lightly golden. Remove cookies from pans to racks to cool.

title: PEANUT BUTTER COOKIES
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Line 2 half-sheet trays with parchment.
In medium bowl, sift flour, baking powder, and salt together.
In stand mixer, blend peanut butter, oil, maple syrup, and vanilla.
Add flour mixture to peanut butter mixture in stand mixer.
Using 1-ounce scoop, form cookies onto parchment-lined baking sheets and press cookies with lightly floured fork.
Bake 10-15 minutes or until bottoms are golden brown. Transfer cookies from pans to racks to cool.

title: CHOCOLATE CHERRY PECAN BARS
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Oil, flour, and parchment line 9-inch by 9-inch square baking pan.

In stand mixer, beat butter, date sugar, maple crystals, and vanilla until fluffy. In bowl, whisk together all-purpose and whole wheat pastry flours. Slowly sift flours into butter mixture until combined.
Press dough in even layer in prepared pan. Bake for about 10 minutes (rotate pan half way through cooking to ensure even baking) or until edges are lightly browned. Set aside to cool.
In stand mixer, beat date sugar, maple crystals, flour, salt, vanilla, and eggs until smooth. Stir in pecans, cherries, orange zest, and chocolate chips and mix just to combine.
Spread filling over crust to create even layer. Bake for 20-25 minutes or until edges are golden brown and filling is set.
Cool in pan for 15 minutes before unmolding onto cooling rack. Transfer to freezer to cool completely before cutting into bars.

title: Raspberry Shortbread Linzer Cookies
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Line two half-sheet trays with parchment paper.
In stand mixer, cream butter and maple crystals together until fluffy.

Whisk together flours and arrowroot in separate medium bowl. Slowly sift flour mixture into butter mixture while continuing to mix until well combined. Add cashews and continue to mix until incorporated. Divide dough equally into 2 and wrap each piece in plastic. Chill in refrigerator for 15-20 minutes, until dough firms.

Roll out both doughs to about 1/8” thickness. Freeze dough until hard. Cut dough with lightly floured cookie cutters. For each cookie, cut solid bottom and mate with center cut out using smaller cutter. Continue process until all dough is used.

Place cookies on 2 sheet trays (bottoms on one tray and tops with centers cut out on other). Bake about 5-6 minutes and rotate pans. Continue to bake until edges are lightly browned. Remove from pans and cool on racks.

When cool, spread 1 tablespoon preserves on solid cookie bottom. Lightly sprinkle cookie tops (with holes) with powdered sugar, if using. Carefully press top cookie with hole on jam-topped bottom. Continue process until all cookies are assembled.

title: Chocolate Biscotti
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F. Line one half-sheet tray with parchment paper.
In small double boiler, melt chocolate chips. Remove from heat and cool to room temperature.
In bowl whisk together flour, cocoa powder, baking soda and salt. Set aside.
Using stand mixer, mix butter, maple crystals, and melted chocolate. Add egg whites. Blend one minute more. Scrape sides of bowl with spatula and blend another thirty seconds.
Add dry ingredients (flour, cocoa, baking soda, salt) to mixer to combine. Dough will very sticky. Divide dough in two.
Roll each dough in chopped nuts and then form each into log shape on sheet tray (6 inches apart). Flatten logs into length of about 8 1/2 inches and width of 3 inches.
Bake 20 minutes or until firm to touch. Remove from oven, lower heat to 300° F, let logs cool 10-12 minutes.
Using bread knife, slice logs into 1/4 inch to 1/2 inch diagonal slices.
Cook slices 7-10 minutes more on each side. Cool and serve. (These get crunchier as they sit.)

title: Pecan Tuiles
ingredients: 1/2 cup pecans, toasted in oven and coarsely chopped
2 tablespoons melted coconut oil OR canola oil
instructions: Preheat convection oven to 300° F (350° F in conventional oven). Line 5 half-sheet trays with Silpats.
Transfer chopped pecans to medium bowl and add flour and arrowroot. Whisk to combine.
In separate bowl, whisk oil, rice syrup, maple syrup, vanilla, and salt until thoroughly combined. Add to pecan/flour mixture, mixing until combined. Let batter sit for 15 minutes.

Use teaspoon measure to drop batter onto Silpat-lined baking sheets. Place only 5 cookies per sheet – one in each corner and one in middle. Bake one sheet at a time for 10 minutes until cookies are bubbly.

Cool cookies for approximately 30 seconds before removing from Silpats. Working quickly (because cookies cool and set hard very fast), remove tuiles from sheet tray using small offset spatula and wrap around cannoli molds. Repeat procedure with remaining sheet trays until all batter is used.

title: Gluten-Free Chocolate Chip Cookies
instructions: Preheat oven to 375° F. Line 2 half-sheet pans with parchment paper.
In medium bowl whisk sorghum flour, buckwheat flour, tapioca starch flour, brown rice flour, white sweet rice flour, and baking powder together. Set aside.
Cream butter and maple crystals with paddle attachment in stand mixer.
Add egg, vanilla, and salt to butter/maple crystal mixture, and salt. Beat on slow speed for 30-60 seconds more.
Stop mixer and add flour mixture to other ingredients. Mix to combine. (Mixture will be stickier than type of “drop” batter prepared with wheat flour.) Add chocolate chips.
Using 1-ounce scoop, portion dough onto baking sheets. Place approximately dozen cookies per sheet with good distance between, as cookies spread.
Cook for about 7-10 minutes or until cookies are nicely golden (do not under-bake).
Transfer to racks until cooled and firmed up.

title: GLUTEN-FREE CRISP GINGER COOKIE
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°F. Line 3 half-sheet trays with Silpats.
In medium bowl whisk almond meal, sorghum flour, arrowroot flour, ground ginger, and black pepper.
In separate small bowl, whisk together oil, rice syrup, maple syrup, vanilla, water, and salt.
Pour syrup mixture into nut/flour mixture. Batter should be consistency of heavy cream. If it is too thick, add more water, 1 tablespoon at a time, until you have proper consistency.

Using teaspoon measure, drop batter onto sheets (5-6 cookies a sheet). Decorate each cookie with one sliced almond. Bake 15-20 minutes or until cookies are golden. Cool before removing from sheet pan.

title: WILD RICE SALAD
ingredients: 1/2 cup wild rice, soaked overnight, drained, and rinsed
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Combine rice in 2 1/2 quart pot with water and salt. Bring rice to boil, reduce heat, and simmer, covered, until grains are tender but still chewy (about 20-25 minutes). Drain rice.
Combine all dressing ingredients except for oil in blender. Slowly drizzle oil into blender until emulsified. Pour half of dressing over still warm rice.
When rice is completely cooled, combine with cranberries, walnuts, carrots, radishes, scallions, chives, and parsley. Season with salt and pepper, if needed.
Toss radicchio and arugula with remaining dressing, arrange on plate, and top with rice salad.

title: TRADITIONAL CAESAR SALAD
ingredients: 8 ounces (1/2 loaf) whole grain bread, crust removed, medium dice
Poached garlic cloves (2) from crouton recipe
2 large eggs, boiled for 3 minutes, cooled, peeled
1 1/2 pounds (2 small heads) romaine lettuce, bite-size pieces
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F.
In 8-inch sauté pan, heat oil and garlic on low heat. Poach until garlic just begins to turn golden. Remove garlic and reserve for dressing.
Toss bread cubes in flavored oil and bake on half sheet pan in oven for about 10 minutes or until crisp. Set aside.
In blender, combine all dressing ingredients except for oil. Slowly drizzle oil into blender until dressing is creamy.
Combine dressing with romaine lettuce. Garnish with Parmesan and croutons.

title: VEGAN CAESAR SALAD
ingredients: 8 ounces (1/2 loaf) whole grain bread, crust removed and medium dice
1 ounce silken tofu, blanched (tied in cheesecloth) and drained
6 tablespoons cup extra virgin olive oil
1 1/2 pounds romaine lettuce (2 small heads), bite-size pieces
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F.
Toss bread cubes in medium bowl with oil, salt, and pepper. Transfer to half sheet pan and bake about ten minutes or until golden and crisp.
In blender, combine dressing ingredients until mixture is creamy.
Combine romaine in medium-size bowl with dressing and toss.
Mix croutons into salad just before service. Garnish with nori strips.

title: MUSHROOMS A LA GRECQUE
ingredients: 2 pounds cremini mushrooms, cleaned, stemmed, halved if large
Drizzle of extra virgin olive oil
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, combine mushrooms, wine, lemon peel, lemon juice, olive oil, fennel seeds, garlic, thyme sprigs, salt, pepper, and water.
Bring mixture to boil, reduce heat, and simmer, covered, for about 5-7 minutes.

Remove lemon peel, thyme sprigs, and smashed garlic. Remove mushrooms from pot with slotted spoon. Serve mushrooms atop mesclun, garnished with tarragon. Drizzle small amount of cooled cooking liquid over salad just before serving.

title: MEDITERRANEAN-INSPIRED PASTA SALAD
ingredients: 1/2 head garlic roasted, garlic paste extracted
1/2 teaspoon lemon zest (same lemon for juice)
12 ounces broccoli rabe (1/2 bunch), stemmed and chopped
6 cups salted water (1 tablespoon sea salt)
8 ounces gluten-free or whole wheat fusilli
1/3 cup sun-dried tomatoes, rehydrated in hot water until soft, drained, chopped
1/2 cup loosely packed fresh basil, chiffonade (1 ounce)
Sea salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: In blender, combine all dressing ingredients until creamy. Set aside.
Heat oven to 350°F.
Toss eggplant in bowl with olive oil and salt. Transfer to parchment-lined half sheet pan and roast in oven until eggplant is tender (about 20 minutes). Set aside.
Bring 1-gallon pot of salted water to boil. Add broccoli rabe and blanch about 2-3 minutes. Shock in ice water. Wring excess moisture from broccoli rabe and add to bowl with dressing.
In same pot, boil pasta for 8-10 minutes or until al dente. Drain and transfer to bowl with dressing and broccoli rabe; toss to combine.
Add eggplant, sun-dried tomatoes, olives, pine nuts, currants, and basil to pasta, broccoli rabe, and dressing. Toss to combine. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Serve at room temperature.

title: FRISEE SALAD WITH GRILLED BABY CARROTS, CUCUMBER, AND RADISHES WITH AVOCADO DRESSING
ingredients: 1 pound baby carrots, peeled (retain 1/4-inch of stem)
7 ounces radishes (1 bunch), thinly sliced rounds
4 ounces English cucumber (1/2 cucumber), peeled, thinly sliced
4 ounces frisee, chopped into bite-size pieces
2 tablespoons seeded and minced jalapeno (1 jalapeno)
2 teaspoons minced garlic (1 small clove garlic)
4 ounces English cucumber (1/2 cucumber) peeled, sliced, seeded
instructions: Toss carrots in bowl with olive oil and salt. Heat grill. Grill carrots until grill marks are visible. Set aside.
In medium bowl, combine radishes, cucumber, frisee, and jalapeno.
Pour lime juice over avocado slices and set aside.
Combine dressing ingredients in blender until creamy.
Toss salad with dressing. Garnish with carrots and avocado slices.

title: ASIAN SLAW
ingredients: 2-3 tablespoons black sesame seeds, to garnish
instructions: Combine cabbage, snow peas, carrots, jalapeno, and cilantro in a medium/large bowl.
Combine dressing ingredients in blender until creamy.
Pour dressing over vegetables and mix well.
Garnish with black sesame seeds.

title: WATERCRESS AND DANDELION GREENS WITH BABY ARTICHOKES
ingredients: 1 1/2 pounds baby artichokes (6), trimmed, cleaned, tossed in 2 tablespoons lemon juice (1 lemon)
7 ounces radishes (1 bunch), stemmed, sliced into thin rounds
1 recipe Creamy Artichoke Dressing (see recipe below)
instructions: Arrange baby artichokes in steamer basket set in 1-gallon pot filled with 2 cups water. Steam artichokes until just tender when pierced with a toothpick. Cool and cut in half lengthwise. Toss with olive oil and salt. Set aside.
Heat grill. Grill artichoke hearts until grill marks are visible. Remove from grill and set aside.
Combine watercress and dandelion greens in large bowl.
Toss watercress and dandelion greens with dressing (see below). Garnish with radishes and artichokes.

title: CREAMY ARTICHOKE DRESSING
ingredients: 1/4 teaspoon sea salt or to taste
1/4 to 1/2 cup artichoke cooking liquid or vegetable stock
instructions: Arrange artichokes in steamer basket (see technique above) and steam until tender, about 20 minutes. Set artichokes aside to cool. Reserve any extra steaming liquid.

Remove leaves and choke from cooled artichokes, leaving just heart and stem. Peel outer layer of stem. Cut each artichoke heart into 4 pieces an remove any fibrous material. Blend artichoke hearts with garlic, lemon juice, rice syrup, chives, salt, pepper, and olive oil, adding artichoke water as necessary for desired creamy consistency.

title: RAW JERUSALEM ARTICHOKE SALAD
ingredients: 1 pound Jerusalem artichokes (6 large), peeled, matchsticks
instructions: Combine all dressing ingredients in blender until creamy.
Combine Jerusalem artichokes and radishes in small bowl with half the dressing. Marinate 30 minutes.
Dress chicory with remaining dressing.
Toss chicory with Jerusalem artichokes and radishes.

title: WILTED GREENS WITH SPRING ONIONS
ingredients: 1/2 pound spring onions (2 bunches), trimmed and halved lengthwise (leave about 1-inch stem intact)
3 tablespoons sun-dried tomatoes, rehydrated, drained, chopped
Sea salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
instructions: Combine onions in medium bowl with oil, vinegar, salt and pepper. Toss to combine. Marinate 15-20 minutes.
Heat grill. Grill onions on both sides until grill marks form and onions are softened. Set aside.
Combine spinach, chicory, and radicchio in medium bowl.
In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil over low heat, sweat garlic and salt until garlic begins to turn golden.
In small bowl, whisk together vinegar and syrup until well combined.
Add spinach, chicory, and radicchio to pan and sauté until just barely wilted. Add balsamic-rice syrup mixture and toss to combine.
Transfer greens to plate and garnish with grilled spring onions, sun-dried tomatoes, and pine nuts.

title: WILTED BITTER GREENS WITH CIPOLLINI ONIONS
ingredients: 8 ounces Cipollini onions, root end trimmed and outer skins removed
3 tablespoons pistachios, lightly roasted, coarsely chopped
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F.
Toss onions with olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper in medium-size bowl.
Transfer onions to parchment-lined half sheet pan and roast until tender. Set aside.
Combine radicchio, romaine, and chicory in medium bowl.
In separate small bowl, whisk together balsamic vinegar and rice syrup until well combined.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over low flame and sweat garlic with salt until garlic begins to turn golden.
Add salad greens to pan; sauté until just wilted. Remove pan from heat. Plate greens and lightly drizzle balsamic vinegar-rice syrup mixture over them.
Garnish salad with onions, currants, and pistachios. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

title: Wild Watercress Salad with Citrus
ingredients: 1 pound fennel bulb (1 medium), stalks and core removed, thinly sliced
2 ounces red onion (1/2 small), thin rounds
1/4 cup oil-cured black olives, pitted, coarsely chopped
instructions: Combine citrus supreme in bowl. Set aside.
Combine juices from citrus supreme in separate bowl. Set aside 1/2 cup for use in dressing.
Cover sliced fennel with cold water in separate bowl. Set aside.
Combine dressing ingredients except for oil in blender. Slowly drizzle oil in blender until creamy and emulsified.
Drain fennel well. Combine fennel and watercress in medium bowl. Toss with dressing. Garnish with citrus supreme, red onion, olives, and watercress.

title: Coleslaw
ingredients: 1/3 teaspoon sea salt or to taste
instructions: Combine almonds in 2 1/2 quart pot with water and salt. Bring to boil, cover, and remove pot from heat. Let almonds soak for 20 minutes.

In blender, combine almonds, soaking water, garlic paste, mustard, agave syrup, vinegar, lemon juice, canola oil, salt, and pepper. Blend until creamy, adding water a little at a time until consistency is similar to mayonnaise. Set aside.
In large bowl, combine cabbage (green and red) with yellow pepper and carrots.
Toss dressing with vegetables. Fold in parsley and dill.

title: Massaged Kale Salad with Roasted Chickpeas and Pickled Red Onion
ingredients: 1/2 cup chickpeas, soaked overnight, drained, and rinsed
1 pound Lacinato kale (1 bunch), stemmed, chiffonade
1/4 cup pitted Kalamata olives, coarsely chopped
instructions: Combine chickpeas in pressure cooker with salt, bay leaf, and thyme sprigs. Add water to cover by 2 inches. Bring pot to pressure, lower heat, and cook for 15 minutes. Release pressure by running cold water over lid. Drain beans.
Heat oven to 350°F. Combine and toss chickpeas in medium bowl with olive oil, fennel, cumin, paprika, pepper flakes, and additional salt to taste.
Transfer chickpeas to parchment-lined half sheet pan and roast until crispy, around 15 minutes. Set aside.
Combine red onions in medium bowl with boiling water and let sit 5-10 minutes. Drain. Add vinegar, salt, and agave. Mix to combine.
Combine kale in medium bowl with salt, lemon zest and lemon juice. Massage with gloved hands until moisture is released and kale begins to soften in texture.
Mix kale with chickpeas and olives. Garnish with pickled onions.

title: Kabocha Salad
ingredients: 2 pounds kabocha squash (1 medium), peeled, seeded, and bite-size chunks
1/4 cup crumbled firm feta cheese (2 ounces)
1/4 cup pomegranate juice (use juice from fresh pomegranate)
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F. Toss squash with oil, salt, and pepper in medium-size bowl. Arrange squash on parchment-lined half sheet pan and roast about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned and tender. Remove from oven and set aside.
Toss pumpkin seeds in small bowl with cumin, salt, ginger, and oil. Bake in oven until seeds are lightly toasted, 5-7 minutes.
Combine all dressing ingredients in blender until creamy. Toss arugula with dressing.
Place about 3/4 cup of squash on plate.
Arrange arugula on atop or alongside squash. Garnish arugula with nuts, pomegranate seeds and feta.

title: NIÇOISE SALAD
ingredients: 1 pound small redskin potatoes, scrubbed, halved
12 ounces romaine lettuce (2 heads), bite-size pieces
instructions: In blender, combine all dressing ingredients until creamy. Season to taste. Set aside.

Heat oven to 350°F. Combine potatoes in medium bowl and toss with 1 teaspoon of salt and olive oil. Transfer to parchment-lined half sheet pan and roast until golden and tender. Toss with 2-3 tablespoons of the dressing. Set aside.

In 2 1/2 quart pot, bring 6 cups of water to boil. Add haricots verts and remaining 1/2 teaspoon of salt and blanch until tender crisp, about 3 minutes. Shock in ice cold water. Drain for a few minutes in colander. Cut haricots verts in half lengthwise and toss with 2-3 tablespoons of the dressing. Set aside.

Combine eggs in 2 1/2 quart pot with water to cover by one inch. Cover pot, bring to boil, and remove pot from heat. Allow eggs to remain in hot water for 10 minutes. Shock eggs in cold water. When cool enough to handle, peel and slice each egg into 4 wedges. Drizzle with 2 tablespoons of the dressing. Set aside.

Heat cast iron grill pan. Drizzle fish with olive oil and season with salt. Grill fish on each side for about 1-2 minutes, depending on thickness of fish. Remove fish from grill. Let fish rest about 7-10 minutes before slicing into 1/4-inch slices.
Pour remaining 1/4 cup of dressing over lettuce and mix to combine. Arrange potatoes, haricots verts, eggs, sliced fish, tomatoes, and olives on mound of dressed greens. Garnish with capers.

title: POACHED CHICKEN AND ROASTED ASPARAGUS
ingredients: 3 skinless and boneless chicken breasts (1 pound)
2 ounces fennel bulbs (1 small), cored, thinly sliced, soaked bowl of cold water
2 ounces red onion (1/2 small), thin rounds
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F. Toss asparagus with oil, salt, 1/4 teaspoon curry powder, and pepper. Transfer asparagus to parchment-lined half sheet pan. Roast until just tender and slightly browned. Set aside.

In 1-gallon pot, bring poach ingredients – chicken stock, coconut milk, bay leaves, thyme, cilantro, salt, remaining 1/2 teaspoon curry powder, and pepper – to simmer. Add chicken, lower heat, cover pot, and poach 15 minutes or until chicken is tender and flesh is opaque.
Remove pot from heat; let chicken remain in stock 20 to 25 minutes. Remove chicken from stock, cool, and cut into 1/3-inch thick diagonal slices. Set aside.
In blender, combine dressing ingredients until creamy. Drain fennel and toss with 2-3 tablespoons of dressing.
On large plate, arrange mesclun, poached chicken, fennel, endive, onion, and asparagus. Drizzle dressing over salad ingredients and chicken. Garnish with cilantro.

title: ASIAN BUCKWHEAT NOODLE SALAD
ingredients: 1/2 pound fresh shiitake mushrooms (18), stemmed, thinly sliced
1/2 pound buckwheat noodles (soba), boiled, rinsed in cold water, drained
4 ounces English cucumber (1/2 medium), thin diagonal slices
instructions: In 2-quart saucepan, combine mushrooms, vinegar, tamari, water, and rice syrup. Bring mixture to boil; reduce heat, and simmer uncovered 3-5 minutes or until mixture slightly thickens. Add carrots and simmer another 30 seconds. Whisk in sesame oil.
Combine vegetables with pasta and toss to combine.
Fan cucumbers around plate, top with noodle salad, and garnish with scallions and sesame seeds.

title: CABBAGE SALAD WITH PEANUT DRESSING
ingredients: 6 ounces snow peas, julienned and blanched
instructions: Combine tofu in 12-inch sauté pan with coconut milk, 1/4 cup of the tamari, lime juice, and rice syrup. Simmer uncovered for 20-25 minutes or until mixture thickens. Set aside.

Prepare an ice bath. Bring 3 quarts water to boil. Add 1 tablespoon salt. Blanch cabbage in for 2 minutes. Transfer cabbage with slotted spoon to ice bath. Drain cabbage in colander for about 5 minutes. Squeeze out any excess moisture from cabbage and set aside.

Bring water back to boil. Transfer noodles to large bowl. When water comes to boil, pour water over noodles and let noodles steep for 10-15 minutes or until tender. Drain and rinse under cold water. Toss noodles with remaining 1/4 cup of tamari.
In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, heat oil on medium flame. Sweat onion, garlic, salt, and chili powder until onions are translucent. Add lime zest, lime juice, coconut sugar, and tamari.
In a small bowl, whisk coconut milk and peanut butter, stirring until smooth. Add to onion-garlic mixture and simmer gently 2-3 minutes or until sauce thickens to desired consistency.
Remove sauce from heat and cool to room temperature. Toss half the peanut sauce with noodles.
Pool remaining peanut sauce on bottom of plate. Arrange plate with tofu, noodles, cabbage, snow peas, cucumbers, and peanuts.

title: CELERIAC WITH GRILLED ASPARAGUS AND EGGS
ingredients: 2 pounds celeriac (1 large), peeled, thin half-moon slices
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, bring water to boil with salt. Add celeriac, vinegar, and rice syrup. Lower heat and simmer until celeriac is just tender. Transfer celeriac with slotted spoon to medium bowl.

Add lemon juice and 6 tablespoons olive oil to pot with cooking liquids. Increase heat to medium-high and reduce liquid until syrupy in texture. Season with salt and pepper and pour half of reduced cooking liquid over celeriac. Set aside.

Combine egg with one-inch water to cover in 2 1/2 quart pot. Cover pot and bring to boil. Remove pot from heat and allow egg to remain in hot water for about 8 minutes. Shock egg in cold water. When cool enough to handle, peel and grate egg through a grater or sieve. Set aside.
Combine asparagus in bowl with remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil, salt, and pepper. Mix to combine.
Heat cast iron griddle grill until hot. Grill asparagus until slightly charred but still firm in texture. Toss with remaining half of reduced cooking liquid and set aside.
Arrange spinach on plate. Arrange bundle of celeriac next to bundle of asparagus. Garnish with grated eggs and hazelnuts.

title: GREENS WITH CUCUMBER ICE AND LIME VINAIGRETTE
ingredients: 1 English cucumber (7 ounces), seeded and cut into chunks
1 avocado (8 slices), drizzled with 1 tablespoon lime juice (1 lime)
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, heat lime juice and rice syrup over medium heat until syrup has dissolved.
Puree cucumber in Vitamix with rice syrup-lime juice mixture, salt, and mint. Blend until smooth.
Transfer mixture to ice cream maker and process until frozen. Set aside in freezer.
In small bowl, combine dressing ingredients – lime juice, rice syrup, salt, and pepper. Slowly whisk in oil until dressing emulsifies.
Arrange salad greens on plate. Scoop cucumber ice into center. Drizzle with dressing. Garnish with tomatoes, avocado, cilantro, and mint leaves.

title: BEET SALAD WITH GREENS AND PISTACHIO-CRUSTED GOAT CHEESE MEDALLIONS
ingredients: 4 ounces semi-soft goat cheese, cut into approximately 1/4-inch wide rounds
1 head red Belgian endive, leaves separated
instructions: In medium-size pressure cooker, combine beets with salt and water to cover by 2 inches. Bring to full pressure, lower heat, and cook beets until just tender when pierced with a sharp knife (large beets – 30 minutes; medium beets – 20 minutes; small beets – 15 minutes).
Drain, cool, and peel beets. Cut into thin 1/8-inch rounds with a mandolin. Set aside.
Puree all dressing ingredients in blender until creamy. Pour half of the dressing over the cooked beets and toss.
Roll edges of cheese rounds in pistachios. Transfer rounds to refrigerator until service.
Toss watercress with remaining dressing.
Arrange plate with endive, watercress, beets, orange supreme, and cheese rounds.

title: Phyllo Triangles with Feta and Kale
ingredients: 1 tablespoon + ¼ teaspoon sea salt, divided
1 pound Lacinata kale, stemmed, leaves roughly chopped
½ cup ricotta cheese, strained 30 minutes in strainer lined with wet cheesecloth
½ pound whole wheat phyllo (8 sheets)
¼ to ½ cup butter, melted
instructions: Heat convection oven to 350°F. Line half sheet tray with parchment. Set aside.
In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium flame. Sweat onion, garlic, ½ teaspoon salt, nutmeg, and red pepper flakes until onions are translucent. Set aside.

In 2-gallon pot, bring 1 gallon water to boil with remaining 1 tablespoon salt. Prepare an ice bath. Blanch kale until tender, about 3-5 minutes. Shock in ice water and drain in colander. Squeeze excess moisture from kale and finely chop.
Return pan with onions to burner over medium flame and add kale. Sauté mixture until kale is heated through.
When mixture has cooled, transfer to medium bowl. Add feta and ricotta cheeses and mix to combine. Season to taste.
Unwrap phyllo and lay one sheet on parchment paper horizontally. Cover remaining sheets with damp towel. Brush phyllo with butter.

Spoon seven 1-tablespoon dollops of mixture across bottom edge of phyllo at equal distances. Cut phyllo into strips between each dollop, and fold up “flag” style (see diagram) to make triangular shape. Transfer finished phyllo triangles to prepared sheet pan. Brush tops with more butter. Repeat with remaining phyllo.
Just before baking, brush all triangles with butter again. Bake 20 minutes or until evenly golden.

title: Phyllo Triangles with Tofu and Spinach
ingredients: 1 pound spinach, stemmed, steamed, and wrung of excess moisture
½ pound whole wheat phyllo (8 sheets)
¼ to ½ cup extra virgin olive oil
instructions: Heat convection oven to 350°F. Line half sheet tray with parchment. Set aside.
Press tofu between paper towels on sheet pan with weighted plate for amount 30 minutes. Transfer tofu to medium bowl and crumble it with fingertips. Set aside.

In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium flame. Sweat shallots, salt, fennel, cumin, and paprika until shallots are translucent. Transfer mixture to bowl with tofu. Add miso and umeboshi. Mix to combine. Chop spinach and combine with tofu mixture. Season to taste.
Unwrap phyllo and lay one sheet on parchment paper horizontally. Cover remaining sheets with damp towel. Brush phyllo with oil.

Spoon seven 1-tablespoon dollops of mixture across bottom edge of phyllo at equal distances. Cut phyllo into strips between each dollop, and fold up “flag” style (see diagram) to make triangular shape. Transfer finished phyllo triangles to prepared sheet pan. Brush tops with more oil. Repeat with remaining phyllo.
Just before baking, brush all triangles with butter again. Bake 20 minutes or until evenly golden.

title: Deviled Eggs with Beet Chips
ingredients: 1 quart fresh beet juice (2 pound beets)
instructions: Prepare ice bath.
In 2 ½ quart saucepan, bring water and beet juice to boil. Add vinegar and salt. Set aside.
In 1-gallon pot, combine eggs with enough water to cover by one inch. Cover pot and bring water to boil. Immediately remove pot from the heat and allow eggs to sit covered for 8-9 minutes.
Transfer eggs to ice bath, allowing them to cool for 1 full minute. Remove eggs ice bath, crack shells on hard surface, and peel eggs carefully.
Transfer eggs to pot with beet juice for about 1 hour. Remove eggs from pot and gently dry them.
Slice eggs in half and remove yolks. Transfer yolks to food processor with yogurt, mustard, salt, and pepper. Blend to creamy, pipe-able consistency. Season to taste.
Place seasoned yolk mixture in pastry bag with star tip and pipe about 1 tablespoon into each dyed egg white half. Garnish with chives and beet chips (recipe below).

title: Beet Chips
ingredients: 6 ounces beet (1 small), peeled, sliced into 1/8 inch thick rounds with mandolin
instructions: Heat oven to 200°F.
Combine beets in small bowl with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Transfer beets to Silpat and bake until crispy, about 2 hours.

title: Fried Shrimp with Wasabi Garnish
ingredients: 2 lemons, cut into 24 wedges for garnish
1 quart coconut or canola oil for frying
instructions: In food processor, combine shrimp, garlic, miso, tamari, curry powder, and salt. Process to smooth paste. Add 1 tablespoon of chives and pulse once or twice until incorporated into mixture. Transfer mixture from processor to small bowl.
Using 1-ounce scoop, portion shrimp balls and roll in breadcrumbs.
Refrigerate breaded shrimp for 20-30 minutes.
Heat oil in wok to 350°F. Deep fry shrimp until golden. Drain shrimp on paper towels.
In small bowl, mix wasabi powder with water and juice. Add enough liquid to achieve smooth, pipe-able consistency. Transfer mixture to small pastry bag with small round tip.
Sprinkle shrimp with salt and a squeeze of lemon juice. Skewer shrimp with toothpick and garnish with small dab of wasabi paste and remaining 1 tablespoon of chives. Garnish tray with lemon wedges.

title: WHITE BEAN SPREAD WITH SUNDRIED TOMATO/KALAMATA TAPENADE ON ROSEMARY-GARLIC PIZZELLE
ingredients: 1/2 cup cannellini beans, soaked overnight, drained, and rinsed
1/4 teaspoon lemon juice or more to taste (1/2 lemon)
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
12 fresh sage leaves, cut in half lengthwise (1/4 ounce)
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, bring beans, stock, bay leaf, sage leaf, rosemary sprig, and salt to boil. Lower heat and simmer partially covered until beans are tender, about 40 minutes.
Drain beans and remove bay leaf, sage leaf, and rosemary sprig. Reserve any remaining bean liquid.
In 8-inch sauté pan, heat olive oil over low flame. Add garlic and sweat until garlic just begins to turn golden in color. Remove from heat immediately.

In food processor, combine beans, garlic, and lemon juice. Blend to creamy, pipe-able consistency, using reserved cooking liquid if necessary. Transfer from processor to small bowl. Season to taste and set aside.
Combine sun-dried tomatoes, olives, and parsley in small bowl. Use about 1/4 teaspoon to garnish each pizzelle.
Toss sage leaves in arrowroot. In small sauté pan heat canola oil on medium-high heat. Fry each sage leaf and drain on paper towels.
Transfer bean paste to small pastry bag with small star tip. Pipe onto pizzelle (recipe following). Garnish with tapenade and sage leaf.

title: ROSEMARY AND GARLIC PIZZELLE (FOR WHITE BEAN SPREAD)
ingredients: Pinch sea salt or to taste
Small amount of olive oil to grease pizzelle maker
instructions: In small bowl, sift together dry ingredients.
In separate small bowl, whisk together wet ingredients.
Using whisk, mix wet ingredients into dry ingredients. Mixture should be consistency of heavy cream. Add extra water, if necessary.
Lightly brush or spray small amount of oil on cooking surface of pizzelle maker. Plug in machine and wait until light turns red. When machine is ready, ladle 1/4 teaspoon of batter for each pizzelle.
After pouring mixture, close cover of machine and cook 1 1/2 minutes until pizzelles are lightly browned and crisp. Remove from machine with an offset spatula.

title: CURRIED CHICKEN SALAD ON PAPADAM WITH YOGURT RAITA
ingredients: Sachet (3 sprigs thyme, 1/2 teaspoon coriander seeds, 1/2 teaspoon peppercorns, 1 bay leaf, 1 cinnamon stick)
Sea salt and pepper to taste
24 mini papadam, fried in coconut oil
4 ounces English cucumber (1/2 medium), thinly sliced, halved
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, braise chicken thighs with stock, sachet, spices (ginger, curry, cumin, and cayenne, and cinnamon stick), and salt for 45 minutes. Remove chicken from pan. When cooled, remove skin and bones. Chop chicken into small pieces, set aside.
Reduce braising liquid until you have about 1/4 cup syrupy liquid. Remove sachet. Reserve syrupy liquid for dressing.
In bowl, combine chicken, red onion, cilantro, and pistachios.
In small bowl, whisk together yogurt, reduced braising liquid, lime, salt and pepper. Add dressing to chicken salad.
Place approximately 2 teaspoons chicken salad on fried papadam. Garnish with dab of yogurt, slice of cucumber, and sprig of cilantro.

title: PINE NUT RICOTTA ON TREVISO LEAVES
ingredients: 1 cup pine nuts, soaked overnight in 2 cups water, and drained
1 head garlic, roasted, cooled, and paste extracted
Pinch of sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In food processor, puree pine nuts, garlic paste, oil, miso, lemon juice, and umeboshi paste. Season with salt and pepper. Transfer mixture to medium bowl or pastry bag with round tip.
Dollop or pipe 1 heaping tablespoon of nut filling in each Treviso leaf and garnish with chives, yellow and red peppers, and pine nuts.

title: ZUCCHINI LATKES WITH CRÈME FRAÎCHE AND HERBS
ingredients: 1 pound zucchini, shredded (large side of grater)
instructions: In medium bowl, combine zucchini, onions, dill, egg, flour, salt, and pepper. Drain in colander for 10 minutes and squeeze out excess liquid.
Form latkes using tablespoon measure.

In 8-inch, non-stick sauté pan, heat oil over medium-high flame. Fry latkes in batches until golden on each side, about 2 minutes. Drain on paper towels and serve with dollop of crème fraîche and sprig of dill.

title: SCALLION, EDAMAME, AND LEEK DUMPLINGS
ingredients: 10 ounces leeks (3 medium), washed, trimmed, cut into 1/8-inch half moons
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 ounce fresh ginger, peeled and cut into julienne (1 ounce)
2 teaspoons diagonally, thinly sliced scallions (1 scallion)
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, bring 6 cups water and 1 tablespoon sea salt to boil. Blanch edamame until tender, about 5-7 minutes. Shock and drain in colander for about 5 minutes. Set aside.

In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium flame. Add leeks and sweat until tender. Add garlic and remaining 1/4 teaspoon of salt. Continue sweating a few minutes more. Transfer leeks and garlic to food processor and puree with edamame to form a creamy pipe-able consistency.
Transfer mixture from food processor to medium-size bowl. Add ginger juice, sesame oil, mirin, lemon juice, cilantro, chives, and mint. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Set aside.
In small bowl, slurry arrowroot and water to smooth consistency. Set aside.

Place 1 scant tablespoon filling in middle of wonton wrapper. Lightly brush outer edge of wonton wrapper with arrowroot mixture. Fold wrapper in half to form half moon, pinching edges together. Repeat with remaining wonton wrappers and filling.

In 12-inch sauté pan, heat 1/4 cup oil over medium-high flame. Add 6-7 wontons at a time and pan fry until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side. Transfer with slotted spoon to paper towel to drain.
Combine and whisk all dipping sauce ingredients in small bowl and serve with dumplings.

title: SWEET POTATO LATKES WITH PEAR-FENNEL MARMALADE
ingredients: 1/2 pound fennel (1/2 small), cored, thinly sliced
2 Bosc pears, peeled, cored, and thinly sliced
1 teaspoon minced garlic (1 small clove garlic)
1 pound sweet potatoes, peeled, grated (large hole side of grater)
1/2 cup cashews, soaked 4 hours to overnight (discard soaking liquid)
1/4 teaspoon sea salt of to taste
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium flame. Add fennel and salt. Sweat until fennel is translucent. Add pears and sweat for an additional 3-5 minutes or until fennel and pears are barely tender.

Add garlic and coriander; continue to sweat for another minute. Add vinegar and water. Simmer for about 5 minutes or until liquid has been absorbed. Season to taste. When cool, add chives and mix to combine. Set aside.

In medium bowl, combine grated sweet potatoes, scallions, flour, arrowroot, salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Mix to combine. Form latkes using tablespoon measure. Heat canola oil in 12-inch straight-sided sauté pan over medium-high heat.

Fry latkes in batches until golden, about 2 minutes on each side. Drain on paper-lined half sheet pan to absorb any excess oil. Season with additional salt to taste. Serve topped with marmalade, cashew cream and fennel fronds.
Combine all ingredients in food processor until smooth and creamy. Season to taste.

title: ROSEMARY, SQUASH, AND LEEK DUMPLINGS
ingredients: 1 1/2 pounds butternut squash (1 large), peeled, seeded, cut into 1-inch cubes
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
6 ounces leeks (1 large), washed, trimmed, cut into 1/8-inch half moons
1 ounce fresh ginger, peeled, cut into julienne (1 tablespoon)
2 teaspoons thinly and diagonally sliced scallion (1 scallion)
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F. In medium bowl, combine the squash, 2 tablespoons olive oil, rosemary, salt and pepper to taste. Toss to coat. Transfer squash to parchment-lined half sheet pan and roast 20 minutes or until tender. Set aside.
When cool, transfer squash to food processor and blend until smooth. Set aside.
Heat remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil in 12-inch sauté pan over medium flame. Sweat leeks and garlic until tender, about 3-5 minutes. Add ginger juice.
Transfer vegetables to medium bowl and add sesame oil, mirin, lemon juice, and pureed squash. Mix to combine and season to taste.
In small bowl, slurry arrowroot and water to smooth consistency. Set aside.

Spoon 1 tablespoon filling in middle of wonton skin. Lightly brush outer edge of wonton wrapper with arrowroot mixture. Fold wrapper in half to form half moon, pinching edges together. Repeat with remaining wonton wrappers and filling.

In 12-inch sauté pan, heat 1/4 cup oil over medium-high flame. Add 6-7 wontons at a time and pan fry each side until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes a side. Whisk and combine all dipping sauce ingredients in small bowl and serve with dumplings.

title: CAULIFLOWER TERRINE
ingredients: 1/3 cup navy beans, soaked, rinsed, drained
1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil, divided
1.5 pounds cauliflower - 1 pound cored and cut into florets, 1/2 pound cut into florets and sliced 1/4-inch thick for garnish
instructions: In pressure cooker, combine beans with 1 cup stock, thyme, and salt. Bring cooker to pressure and cook beans 20 minutes. Lower pressure, drain beans, and remove thyme stems. Reserve for step 2.

Blend remaining 1 1/2 cups vegetable stock and blanched almonds in VitaMix until smooth. Strain milk through nut bag or chinois into 2 1/2 quart pot. Add agar powder and bring to milk to simmer. Stir constantly until agar dissolves, about 10 minutes. Add beans to pot and set aside. Rinse out Vitamix for use in step 5.

In 10-inch sauté pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat. Turn heat down, and sweat onions, garlic, 1 pound cauliflower florets, and salt, stirring occasionally, until cauliflower is tender. Transfer vegetables to large bowl, and add bean-almond milk mixture. Set aside.
In same sauté pan, heat remaining 2 tablespoons oil. Sear sliced cauliflower on each side until golden. Drain on paper towels. Set aside.
In VitaMix, combine almond milk-bean mixture and vegetables (minus the sliced/seared cauliflower). Blend until smooth. Fold chives into mixture. Adjust seasonings.
Line terrine mold with parchment. Arrange seared cauliflower slices on bottom of mold. Pour puree into mold. Refrigerate and set until firm, about 2 hours.

title: RED LENTIL TERRINE
ingredients: 3 tablespoons white miso dissolved in 3 tablespoons warm water
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F. Line terrine mold with parchment paper and set aside.

In 1-gallon pot, combine lentils, stock, salt, and bay leaf. Cover pot and bring to boil. Lower heat and simmer until lentils are tender, about half an hour. Drain lentils in colander for 10 minutes, transfer to medium bowl, and set aside.

In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium flame and sweat onions and garlic until translucent. Continue to sweat, stirring occasionally, until onions are soft and uniformly browned, about 15 minutes.
Deglaze pan with mirin. Combine onions with lentils. Add walnuts, umeboshi paste, miso, salt, pepper, cilantro, and parsley. Mix to combine. Season to taste.
Fold mixture into terrine mold. Bake until firm, about 20 to 30 minutes.
Refrigerate terrine until it sets, about 1 hour.

title: SMOKED TROUT TERRINE
ingredients: 8 ounces shrimp, peeled, deveined, roughly chopped
instructions: Heat oven to 300°F. Line terrine mold with plastic. Prepare an ice bath.
In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, heat cream to 160°F and add pinch of saffron. Transfer pot to ice bath and cool cream to room temperature.
Combine trout, lemon juice, salt, pepper, egg whites, and infused cream in food processor.

Puree until smooth and fully combined. Transfer puree to bowl. Fold in shrimp, scallops, parsley, and basil. Transfer mixture to terrine mold. Fold ends of plastic wrap or parchment over top of terrine.
Poach terrine in 170°F water bath for about 35 minutes.
Remove terrine from water bath and cool before refrigerating at least 3-4 hours.

title: GREEN LENTIL TERRINE
ingredients: 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves, minced (1/4 ounce)
2 tablespoons white miso dissolved in 2 tablespoons warm water
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F. Line terrine mold with parchment.

In 1-gallon pot, combine lentils, salt, vegetable stock, and bay leaves. Cover pot and bring to boil. Lower heat, and simmer for 30-40 minutes or until lentils are tender. Drain lentils in colander for 10 minutes.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium-high flame. Sauté onions until browned. Add thyme, coriander, and nutmeg. Sauté 2 minutes more. Set aside.
Transfer lentils to food processor and pulse to form thick paste.

Transfer lentils to medium-size bowl. Add onions, 1 cup bread crumbs, parsley, miso, and tahini. Mixture should be thick but moist. If mixture is too loose, add more bread crumbs. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Pour mixture into terrine mold and bake until firm, about 20 to 25 minutes. Chill terrine until it reaches room temperature, about 1 hour.

title: Truffled Portobello Mousse
ingredients: 4 large Portobello mushrooms (approximately 1 1/2 pounds), sliced with stems
2 large yellow onions (approximately 1 1/2 pounds), medium dice
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.

Combine navy beans and bay leaf in pressure cooker. Add water to cover by 2 inches. Cover cooker and bring beans to pressure. Reduce heat and cook for 20 minutes. Release pressure by placing pressure cooker under cold water and drain beans, reserving 1 cup of cooking liquid.
Toss 2 tablespoons of olive oil with sliced mushrooms and 1/2 teaspoon sea salt. Spread mushrooms on parchment-lined sheet tray and roast 15 minutes, or until golden.
In large sauté pan, heat remaining 2 tablespoons oil. Add onions and remaining 1/2 teaspoon of salt and cook over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes or until onions are golden. Deglaze pan with sherry.

Combine agar flakes and reserved cup of bean cooking liquid in 2-quart saucepan. Bring mixture to a simmer, cook for 8 to 10 minutes (with lid ajar) or until the agar is completely dissolved, stirring occasionally.
Combine navy beans, Portobello mushrooms, onions, dissolved agar mixture and truffle salt in Vitamix. Using tamper, blend mixture for 3 to 5 minutes, or until completely smooth.
Pour mixture into silicone molds and refrigerate for 30 to 60 minutes to set.
Coat sides of unmolded mousse with thyme leaves before serving.
Serve chilled atop sliced baguette with fig-thyme preserves (see below for recipe).

title: Fig-Thyme Preserves (for Portobello Mousse)
ingredients: 2 cups dried figs, stemmed and quartered
instructions: Combine figs, thyme, apple juice, salt, pepper, and apple cider vinegar in 2-quart saucepan.
Bring mixture to simmer and cook for 20 minutes with lid slightly ajar.
Use immersion blender to roughly puree mixture slightly (mixture can be chunky).
Chill preserves before serving.
Serve fig-thyme preserves with Portobello mousse and sliced baguette (see previous recipe).

title: Beet Terrine
ingredients: 1 cup beet juice (without foam), strained (approximately 1 pound beets)
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, combine grapefruit, orange, and beet juices with agave. Cover pan and bring to boil.
Reduce heat to simmer and add agar powder, stirring continuously for 5-10 minutes or until agar is dissolved. Add salt and stir to combine.
Pour 1/2 cup of mixture into each ramekin. Refrigerate until mixture sets, about 20 minutes. Using small offset spatula, carefully remove terrine from ramekin mold.
Top each terrine with dollop of sour cream, chives and micro-greens.

title: Polenta, Goat Cheese, and Black Bean Terrine
ingredients: 1 cup black beans, soaked, rinsed, drained
2 red peppers (10 ounces), roasted, peeled, seeded, and pureed in blender
instructions: Combine beans in small pressure cooker with water, bay leaves, garlic, and salt. Bring to pressure, lower heat, and cook 15 minutes. Bring down pressure, remove bay leaf, and drain beans in colander. Transfer beans to food processor.

Melt butter in 8-inch sauté pan over low flame. Add cumin, salt, pepper, oregano, thyme, basil, and cayenne. When spices are fragrant (30 seconds), add them to beans. Puree beans in food processor. Stiffen mixture with breadcrumbs if needed to create texture in which spoon will stand.

In 2-quart saucepan, bring water and salt to boil. Slowly whisk in polenta. Exchange whisk for spoon and cook another 20 minutes, stirring frequently. Add in butter, coriander, white pepper, and cayenne and cook 5 minutes more. Adjust seasonings.
Line loaf pan with parchment paper.

Pour two-thirds polenta into terrine mold. Fold goat cheese and cilantro into remaining one-third polenta. Spread in mold as second layer. Spread bean puree in mold as third layer. Refrigerate until thoroughly chilled, about 1 hour.
Garnish with cilantro sprigs. Serve with crackers and roasted red pepper puree.

title: Green Bean and Walnut Pâté
ingredients: 1 1/4 cups walnuts, well toasted and cooled
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Prepare an ice bath. In 1-gallon pot, bring 3 quarts of water to boil with 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Add green beans and blanch until tender, 5 to 7 minutes. Shock beans in ice water and drain in colander. Slice 8 beans thinly on diagonal for garnish. Cut remaining beans into 1-inch pieces. Set aside.

In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium flame. Add onions and remaining 1/4 teaspoon of salt. Cover and sweat onions over low heat until very soft, about 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Uncover pan and continue cooking and stirring often until onions are caramelized, about 5 minutes.
Add wine to onions and continue cooking until wine is reduced and mixture is almost dry.
Puree 1 cup of walnuts with green beans, onions, miso, and vinegar in food processor until smooth. Season to taste. Chop remaining 1/4 cup of walnuts for garnish.
Present pâté garnished with string beans and chopped walnuts.

title: Chicken Liver Pâté
instructions: Prepare an ice bath. In 12-inch sauté pan, melt 2 ounces butter over medium-high heat. Add chicken livers and sauté until brown on outside and still very pink on inside. Transfer livers from pan to bowl and chill over ice bath.

Using same pan, lower heat and add onions. Sweat with salt until translucent. Lower heat, and continue to cook until onions soften and color, stirring often to prevent burning. Continue until onions are caramelized, about 15-20 minutes.

Add garlic along with bay leaves, thyme, and pepper and sweat until fragrant, about 1 minute more. Remove onions from pan and deglaze with Cognac or brandy. Add onions back to fond and stir to combine.
Discard bay leaves. Transfer onion mixture to bowl and cool over ice bath to room temperature.
Puree chicken livers and onion mixture in food processor, adding remaining 2 ounces of chilled butter in pieces while pulsing to blend. Adjust seasoning, to taste.
Pack and smooth pâté into 3 individual ramekins or small molds (about 4 ounces each). Refrigerate until firm, about 2 hours.

title: Mushroom-Walnut Pâté
ingredients: 2-3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided
1 pound button mushrooms, cleaned, stemmed, sliced
1 cup plus 2 tablespoons walnuts, well toasted and divided
instructions: Combine dried porcini mushrooms with boiling water in small bowl. Soak until mushrooms are soft and pliable, about 20 to 30 minutes. Strain mixture through chinois. Set aside mushrooms and reserve 6 tablespoons soaking liquid.

In 12-inch sauté pan, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over low heat. Sweat onion until translucent. Add garlic and continue sweating, stirring occasionally, until onions are soft and evenly browned, about 15 minutes. Add minced sage and cook 1 minute more. Transfer onion-garlic mixture from pan to bowl and set aside.

Add another tablespoon olive oil to same pan, raise heat, and sear mushrooms in batches, making sure that they are in one, even layer in pan. Add more oil as necessary between batches. Add seared mushrooms to bowl with onion-garlic mixture.
Deglaze pan with sherry. Reduce sherry to about 1/4 cup.

In food processor, combine garlic-onion-mushroom mixture, deglazing liquid, 1 cup of walnuts, porcini soaking liquid, and balsamic vinegar. Pulse to thick, spreadable consistency. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

In 8-inch sauté pan, heat canola oil over medium-high flame. Toss sage leaves with arrowroot. Fry dusted sage leaves for a few seconds in oil until crisp. Transfer leaves from pan to plate lined with paper towel to drain.
Chop remaining 2 tablespoon of walnuts to use as garnish with parsley and fried sage leaves.

title: Sun-Dried Tomato and Sunflower Seed Pâté
ingredients: 3/4 cup sunflower seeds, toasted and divided
1/2 teaspoon minced rosemary leaves (1 small sprig)
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Soak sun-dried tomatoes in small bowl with boiling water until soft and pliable, about 15 minutes. Strain tomatoes, reserving 3/4 cups soaking liquid. Set both aside.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over low flame. Sweat onion until translucent. Add garlic and continue sweating, stirring occasionally, until onions are soft and evenly browned, about 15 minutes.
In food processor, puree onions, sun-dried tomatoes, 1/2 cup sunflower seeds, rosemary, vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Slowly add enough water so mixture becomes spreadable paste.
Use remaining 1/4 cup sunflower seeds for garnish.

title: BERRIES AND CREAM TERRINE
yield_amount: 6 cups, enough to make three 6 x 4-inch rectangular terrines
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups cashews, soaked and drained (approximately 2 cups soaked)
1 1/2 cups cashews, soaked and drained (approximately 2 cups soaked)
instructions: For vanilla layer: In a high-speed blender, combine soaked cashews, coconut milk, agave syrup, coconut oil, vanilla extract, lemon juice, scraped vanilla bean and sea salt.
Blend on high for a few minutes until mixture is completely smooth.
Transfer filling into rectangular 6 x 4-inch silicone molds, filling them halfway up (approximately 1 cup per mold) and place in the freezer.
For blueberry layer: Add frozen blueberries, coconut milk and agave syrup into a small saucepan.
Bring mixture to a simmer over high heat. Reduce for 10 to 15 minutes, stirring frequently, until mixture is reduced to 1 1/2 cups. Cool over ice bath.
Pour cooled mixture into high-speed blender. Add soaked cashews, coconut oil, lemon juice, and salt.
Blend on high for a few minutes until the mixture is completely smooth.
Gently spoon mixture on top of vanilla layer already setting in silicone molds.
Freeze for one hour, or until firm.
To serve: Let terrines sit for 5 to 10 minutes at room temperature before serving.
Place terrines on a serving plate. Top with cut blackberries and mint.
Store any leftover terrines in the freezer.

title: RED LENTIL PÂTÉ
ingredients: 1 1/2 teaspoon white miso dissolved in 1 tablespoon water
instructions: Combine lentils in 2 1/2 quart pot with water or stock, bring to boil, and simmer covered 20 minutes.
Heat oil in 8-inch sauté pan. Add onions, garlic, and herbs and cook until caramelized.
Oil and line loaf pan with parchment and sprinkle with half the breadcrumbs.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
When lentils are cooked, mash by stirring, add onion mixture, remaining breadcrumbs, and parsley. Season with black pepper, umeboshi vinegar, and white miso.
Fold pâté into pan and bake 20-30 minutes. Allow to set, cool, and slice. Garnish with dill.

title: OATMEAL DULSE CRACKERS
ingredients: 1/2 teaspoon Sea Seasonings Dulse Granules with Garlic
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
In food processor, finely grind oats, sunflower seeds, and flour together. Add dulse granules and salt.
In separate small bowl, whisk together coconut oil and rice syrup.
Add oil-syrup mixture to dry ingredients in food processor, adding in water as needed. (Dough should be stiff, yet pliable.)
Between parchments sheets, roll out dough with rolling pin to uniform thickness, 1/8 inch. Remove top sheet of parchment.
Mix together equal amounts of sesame, poppy and caraway seeds and sprinkle liberally over rolled dough. Cover with top sheet of parchment again and roll seeds into dough.

Remove top sheet of parchment again. Score dough all the way through using pizza cutter or knife at 2-inch intervals vertically and horizontally to create squares. Score diagonally to make triangles. Slide dough with bottom parchment onto half sheet pan.
Bake until lightly browned, about 15 to 20 minutes. (Caution: crackers on outer edges may brown more quickly than those in center.)

title: FOCACCIA WITH HERBS
ingredients: 1 small cooked, peeled and mashed potato, room temperature
1/2 cup of mixed fresh herbs + more for garnish
instructions: For sponge: Make sponge by mixing yeast, sweetener, water, and high-gluten flour together.
Cover sponge with plastic, transfer to warm place until doubled in size (20-30 minutes).
For dough: Preheat oven to 475°F. Place pizza stone in oven to heat up.
Incorporate into sponge mixture: potato, warm water, olive oil, salt, semolina flour, fresh herbs, and enough whole wheat bread flour to form knead-able dough.
Transfer dough to floured work surface and knead. Dough should be soft and a little sticky. Knead for about 10 minutes.
Transfer dough to oiled bowl, cover with plastic. Rise until doubled in size (30-45 minutes).
Fold and turn dough. Flatten and form focaccia on parchment-lined pizza peel. Cover with damp towel and let rise again 15-20 minutes more, or until doubled in size again.
Brush top of focaccia with olive oil and sprinkle with mixture of fresh herbs, sea salt, and fresh cracked pepper. Dimple surface with fingertips, and transfer to pizza stone. Bake 20-25 minutes.

title: Vegan Caesar Salad
ingredients: 1 ounce soft tofu, blanched and drained
1 head romaine lettuce, cut or torn into bite-size pieces
instructions: In blender, combine garlic, miso, lemon juice, vinegar, mustard, umeboshi paste, shoyu, tofu, nori, and oil. Blend until mixture is smooth and creamy.
Toss salad with croutons and dressing; serve immediately.

title: Croutons for Vegan Caesar Salad
ingredients: 1/2 loaf French OR Italian whole wheat bread, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F.
In deep bowl, toss bread cubes with oil and salt until each cube is well covered.
Spread croutons in single layer on parchment-lined sheet tray. Bake until croutons are crisp and evenly brown (20-30 minutes), turning often.

title: Whole Poached Norwegian Salmon
instructions: Combine ingredients for court bouillon in deep hotel pan large enough to hold salmon. Bring to poach temperature.
Wrap salmon in double layer of cheesecloth leaving about 6 - 8 inches at each end of fish.
Twist and knot ends of cheesecloth. Cheesecloth is used to lift salmon out of pan when cooking is completed.
Lower salmon into court bouillon. For accurate cooking time, measure fish at its thickest point. Allow 10 minutes to the inch. At completion, internal temperature of fish should be 140°F.
Remove hotel pan from heat, and let fish cool in bouillon. Drain.
When totally cooled remove skin with paring knife. Decorate surface of fish with thinly sliced cucumbers.
Finish garnishing plate with watercress, lemon slices, cherry tomatoes, radishes, etc.

title: Cucumber-Citrus Salsa (for Poached Salmon)
ingredients: 6 oranges, supreme and each section halved
instructions: Combine all ingredients and adjust seasonings.

title: Lemon-Dill Mayonnaise (for Poached Salmon)
instructions: Whisk together egg yolk, mustard, lemon juice, water and salt in bowl.

Drop by drop add oil, whisking constantly. When half the oil is used up, mayonnaise will be thick enough to add remainder about one tablespoon at a time, until it’s all used up. Add lemon zest. Refrigerate.

title: CHICKPEA-ARUGULA SALAD
ingredients: 1/2 bunch arugula, cleaned and chopped into bite sized pieces (5 cups)
sea salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Combine chickpeas, water, cumin, and salt in stockpot.
Bring beans to simmer, lower heat, and cook 45-60 minutes, adding water as needed, until beans are tender. Drain beans.
While beans are still warm, toss with dressing. (recipe below).
Let beans cool to room temperature and, right before service, add arugula, olives, parsley, and basil. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

title: SESAME-GARLIC DRESSING
ingredients: 1 head garlic, roasted and paste extracted
instructions: Combine all ingredients (except salt) in blender. Blend to creamy consistency. Season with sea salt.

title: TEMPEH VEGETABLE KEBOBS
ingredients: 6 large cremini mushrooms, stemmed and cut in half
1/2 zucchini, cut in half lengthwise, then into 1/2" slices
1/2 yellow pepper, cut into twelve equal pieces
1 tablespoon arrowroot, dissolved in 2 tablespoons apple juice
12 (8-inch) skewers for kebobs, soaked in water
instructions: Whisk together tempeh marinade ingredients in bowl; pour into 1-gallon pot.

Cut each package of tempeh in two. Bring marinade to boil, add tempeh, lower heat, and simmer covered for 45 minutes. Drain tempeh and pat dry, reserving marinade (there should be 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons).

Whisk together vegetable marinade ingredients in mixing bowl. Add all vegetables to marinade and let sit for 20-30 minutes, stirring often. Remove vegetables with slotted spoon (set aside marinade for later use).

Cut tempeh into 24 cubes and toss with oil. Preheat grill while preparing kebobs. Use one piece of each vegetable and two pieces of tempeh for each skewer. Grill kebobs few minutes on each side until grill marks form and vegetables are tender.
Bring reserved tempeh marinade to boil in 2 1/2 quart saucepan; whisk in dissolved arrowroot, stirring constantly until sauce comes back to boil and becomes clear. Use sauce for kebobs.

title: HERBED QUINOA
instructions: Toast quinoa in 2 1/2 quart saucepan over high heat, stirring constantly until it smells nutty and browns lightly (5-7 minutes).
Remove quinoa from heat. Add boiling water and salt. Lower heat and simmer, covered, for 15 minutes or until quinoa is cooked and all liquid is absorbed.
Fluff with fork and add tarragon before serving.

title: LACINATA KALE WITH MATCHSTICK CARROTS
ingredients: 2 large carrots, peeled and cut into 1/8-inch wide matchsticks
4 bunches Lacinata kale, washed and chopped into bite sized pieces
Sea salt and pepper to taste
instructions: In 2-gallon stockpot bring about 6 quarts of water to boil. Add salt. Prepare ice bath.
Blanch carrots in water for about 30 to 40 seconds; shock in ice water and drain. Set aside.
In same pot, blanch kale for 3 to 4 minutes until tender but not mushy. Shock in ice water, drain and set aside.
In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil, add garlic slices and sauté until garlic slices are just golden. Do not burn! Add kale and carrots. Cook until heated through.

title: Almond Fruit Torte
ingredients: 1 tablespoon kuzu, dissolved in tablespoon water
instructions: Oil and flour 8 1/2 inch spring form pan.
Powder almonds in food processor. Add eggs, 1/2 cup maple syrup, and almond extract. Process until smooth. Pour batter into pan.
Bake at 350°F for 20 minutes. Top should be firm to touch and brown. Do not remove cake from pan.
In one-quart saucepan, soften agar flakes in water for 10 minutes. Cook covered, stirring often until agar is dissolved.
In separate pot, combine strawberries with 1/4 cup maple syrup and cook over medium flame, covered, until soft. Puree in blender, and put through sieve to remove seeds.
Add strawberry puree to agar mixture, stirring well. Add dissolved kuzu to pot stirring constantly until thick and clear and mixture comes to boil.
Adjust sweetness with remaining maple syrup, if desired.
Let strawberry mixture cool slightly. Pour over cake before removing sides of spring-form pan.
Let mixture set, then remove sides of spring-form pan.
Garnish with kiwis and oranges and serve.

title: Tapenade
ingredients: 1 cup rinsed and pitted Niçoise OR Kalamata olives
instructions: Combine all ingredients in food processor. Pulse to spreadable paste-like consistency.
