title: Cream of Mushroom Soup
ingredients: 1 1/2 teaspoons sherry vinegar, or more to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat butter or olive oil. Add shallots and sauté until translucent. Add flour; stir 4-5 minutes, until toasted.
Add stock 1 cup at a time, whisking to blend. Bring soup to simmer, add salt.
Add mushrooms to soup. Simmer 30 minutes.
Puree soup in blender. Add sherry and black pepper. Adjust seasonings. Serve garnished with parsley sprig.

title: KIDNEY BEAN SALAD
ingredients: 1 cup kidney beans, soaked overnight, drained and rinsed
instructions: Combine beans with water and bring to boil over high heat. Add pinch of sea salt.
Lower heat and simmer until beans are tender (about 45 to 60 minutes). Drain beans.
Whisk together mustard, lime juice, apple cider vinegar, and salt. Slowly whisk in olive oil. Pour dressing over warm beans.
When beans have cooled, toss together with radishes, scallions and parsley.

title: TEMPEH SCALOPPINI
ingredients: 1 (8-ounce) package tempeh, cut in half crosswise
2-3 ounces sliced fresh shiitake AND/OR cremini mushrooms
instructions: In medium saucepan, combine shoyu, ginger, kombu, sea salt, and tempeh and water. Cover pan and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer about 25 minutes. Drain tempeh.
Slice tempeh on diagonal into 6-8 thin slices. Dredge each slice in flour. Shake off excess flour.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium-high heat and fry tempeh until golden on both sides. Drain any excess oil. Add water or stock and wine to tempeh. Cover and bring to boil.
Add sliced mushrooms and garlic, reduce heat to low, and cook covered until mushrooms release juices and become tender.
Add fresh herbs; simmer until liquid thickens (needs to be saucy).
Remove from heat and stir in lemon juice and salt to taste.
Garnish with parsley and serve.

title: RICE PILAF
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil and sweat onions until translucent.
Add rice and stir to coat with oil.

Add stock, salt, pepper, and thyme. Bring to boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer 45 minutes or until all water is absorbed. Remove from heat and keep covered to steam for at least another 15 minutes. Garnish with fresh herbs.

title: HASHED BRUSSEL SPROUTS WITH CARROTS AND POPPY SEEDS
ingredients: 1 1/4 pound Brussels sprouts, ends trimmed and finely sliced
instructions: In large bowl toss Brussels sprouts with lemon juice.

In 12-inch sauté pan, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat. Add carrots and sauté for about 2 minutes. Add 1 tablespoon of wine, lower heat and continue to cook until carrots are bright orange but still crunchy.
Add Brussels sprouts, garlic, poppy seeds and remaining 2 tablespoons of oil. Add remaining 3 tablespoons of wine. Cook until Brussels sprouts are tender (about 3-4 minutes).
Warm right before service and add salt and pepper. Add parsley just before service.

title: RUGELACH
ingredients: 1/2 cup walnuts, toasted, and finely chopped
instructions: In food processor, combine cream cheese and butter. Process until smooth and creamy. Add maple crystals and vanilla; process until incorporated, scraping sides of bowl as necessary.
In small bowl, whisk together flour and salt. Add to food processor. Pulse until dough starts to clump together and form ball.
Remove dough from food processor. Separate dough equally in two and wrap each piece in plastic and flatten to disc shape. Refrigerate for at least one and one half hours until firm.
In medium bowl, combine currants, walnuts, maple crystals, and cinnamon. Set aside. Place preserves into separate small bowl. Set aside.
Pre-heat oven to 350°F, line 2 half sheet pans with parchment paper.

Work with one dough at a time, leaving other in refrigerator. Liberally dust dough with flour. Roll between 2 pieces of plastic wrap to 9-inch circle. Thickness should be 1/8 inch. Use inverted nine-inch cake pan to trim edges.
Using offset spatula, spread dough with half of preserves. Sprinkle with 1/2 of filling. Divide circle into 16 even “slices” (using bench knife, pizza cutter, or sharp knife).
Starting at wide end of triangle, roll each slice tightly as possible. Transfer to sheet tray and refrigerate 15-20 minutes more. Repeat steps 7-10 with remaining dough.
Before baking, brush each cookie with egg wash. Bake for 15-20 minutes or until lightly golden.

title: APPLE-OAT CRUMBLE
ingredients: 4 Granny Smith apples, peeled, cored, cut in into 1/8-inch slices
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 350°F. While cutting apples, soak slices in acidulated water (juice of 1/2 lemon and 4 cups water).
In large bowl, whisk together salt, arrowroot, cinnamon, maple crystals, and apple juice. Drain apples, and combine with cinnamon-maple mixture. Transfer to 9-inch square pan.
In medium bowl combine oats, flour, and cinnamon. Whisk to combine.
In separate bowl whisk oil, syrup, and salt.
Pour wet into over dry and toss until mixture is completely coated.
Spread topping over apples. Bake 45 to 55 minutes until topping is golden and apples and filling is bubbly. Serve garnished with drizzle of pomegranate reduction.

title: POMEGRANATE REDUCTION
instructions: Combine all ingredients in 2 1/2 quart pot. Simmer until thickened to nappé consistency.

title: TEMPEH “BOLOGNESE”
ingredients: 1 Spanish onion (12 ounces), peeled and small diced
1 teaspoon dry basil (3 tablespoon fresh leaves)
2 tablespoons rice syrup (if tomatoes seem too acidic), optional
1 pound pasta cooked al dente (to the tooth)
instructions: Sauté tempeh in 3 tablespoons of olive oil until browned.
Add remaining olive oil and onion with 1 teaspoon of salt. Keep cooking until onions are soft.
Add garlic, oregano and basil (if using fresh oregano and basil, then add in last 10 minutes of cooking). Cook for few minutes longer. Add 1 cup of red wine and reduce until almost dry.
Pour in tomato puree and cook covered for 15-20 minutes.
Season with salt, pepper and rice syrup (if needed).
Have large pot set up with boiling, salted water and strainer or basket that can be easily lifted out.
In 8-inch sauté pan, heat approximately 1/2 cup of Bolognese sauce.
Drop one serving pasta (about 4 ounces) in strainer or basket and place in boiling water. Heat through about one minute. Drain pasta.
Combine pasta with sauce and toss to coat. Plate pasta and pour over any sauce left in pan. Garnish plate and serve.

title: PHYLLO CUPS WITH SAUTÉED APPLES
ingredients: 2 sticks melted butter OR 1 cup no taste oil
1 Granny Smith apple, peeled, cored and thinly sliced
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F degrees. Oil muffin tins (3 tins, 6 muffins each).
Layer 6 sheets of phyllo, brushing each layer with either melted butter or oil. Sprinkle each layer with pinch of maple crystals and pinch of cinnamon.
Cut layered phyllo into six equal pieces, making six 4-inch by 4-inch squares. Repeat process until all phyllo is used.
When all 18 squares are complete, carefully press them into muffin tins. Bake for 7-10 minutes or until phyllo cups are golden.
Remove cups from tins and cool. Fill with sautéed apples (recipe following) and ice cream. Garnish plate with cinnamon, toasted nuts, and fresh mint.
Heat clarified butter in 8-inch sauté pan until hot. Add apples and brown 3-5 minutes. With slotted spoon, transfer apples to warm plate.
Pour off clarified butter and deglaze with juice, then add syrup and vanilla or rum. Reduce by little more than half, or until bubbles cover surface of pan.
Cool sauce for minute or two. Mount with cold butter.
Transfer apples to phyllo cup. Coat apples with 1 to 2 tablespoons of sauce and dust with cinnamon and nuts. Top with ice cream. Use remaining sauce as underliner for phyllo cups.

title: GRILLED PINEAPPLE CHUTNEY
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil and sweat onion until translucent.

While onion is cooking, roast red pepper over open flame. When skin is blackened, place in paper bag for 10 minutes. Remove from bag, let cool and remove blackened skin. Cut pepper into small dice and add to pan with red onion.
Add jalapeño to onion and pepper mixture. Cook 5 minutes more and set pan aside.
Peel and cut pineapple into 4 wedges of equal size. Grill slices and set aside. When slices are cool, cut into medium-small dice and add to onion and pepper mixture.
Season with salt and lime juice and garnish with minced cilantro.

title: CHICKEN BREASTS IN MUSHROOM SAUCE
ingredients: 4 boneless, skinless, chicken breast halves, pounded to 1/2 inch thickness
3 tablespoons cold, sweet butter, cut into small pieces
instructions: In shallow bowl, dredge chicken in flour. Shake off excess flour.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat 3 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Cook chicken in oil until golden brown, about 3 minutes per side.
Remove chicken from pan, season immediately with salt and pepper, cover to keep warm, and let rest for 10 minutes.

Add remaining oil to pan and heat over medium-high flame. Add mushrooms and cook for 2 minutes. Add shallots and garlic and continue cooking until mushrooms turn golden brown (2 to 3 minutes), stirring often to prevent garlic and shallots from burning.
Pour wine into pan and cook over high heat until almost evaporated, scraping browned bits from bottom of pan. Add stock and cook until reduced by half.
Remove pan from heat and whisk in butter. Season to taste with salt, pepper and lemon juice.
Slice chicken thinly on bias and arrange by fanning slices.

title: QUICK TOFU TERIYAKI
ingredients: Juice from 1/4 cup peeled and grated ginger
1 pound firm or extra firm tofu, pressed
instructions: In blender, combine marinade ingredients. Blend until smooth.
Slice tofu into four slabs of equal thickness.
In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine tofu with marinade. Bring to boil and reduce to simmer, covered, for 35 minutes.
Drain tofu (reserving marinade) and blot with paper towels to absorb excess moisture.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil and pan-fry tofu over medium-high heat until golden on both sides.
After tofu is cooked, drain any excess oil out of pan and place tofu in warm oven.
Transfer marinade to blender with kuzu and process until smooth.
Pour blended mixture into pan and cook for about two minutes, until liquid is thickened, stirring constantly.
Add tofu to sauce, stirring until well coated.
Serve with rice and steamed vegetables.

title: CREAMY CARROT SOUP WITH POTATO
ingredients: 1 large yellow onion (___ ounces), medium dice
1/2 medium potato (___ ounces), peeled, large dice
1 teaspoon lemon juice, or more to taste
1 1/2 teaspoons ginger juice, or more to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil over medium-low heat. Add onions and salt. Sweat until onions are softened (about 5-8 minutes). Stir often to prevent browning.
Add carrots, cover pot and sweat carrots over low heat for 5-6 minutes. Stir to prevent browning.
Add stock and potatoes to pot. Raise heat and bring to boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer 25 minutes, covered, until carrots are very tender.
Blend soup until creamy, adding additional stock to achieve desired consistency.
Add lemon and ginger juice. Readjust seasonings.
Garnish with dill and serve.

title: ROMAINE SALAD WITH SHERRY VINAIGRETTE
instructions: Whisk together vinegar, mustard, shallot, and salt in bowl. Slowly drizzle in olive oil while whisking until dressing is properly emulsified.
Toss lettuce, celery, and apple in bowl with enough dressing to coat.
Divide salad into portions, ladling on extra dressing as needed. Garnish each salad with raisins and pecans.

title: CREAMY FRESH HORSERADISH DRESSING
ingredients: 3 tablespoons peeled, freshly grated horseradish (3/4 ounces)
1/2 pound silken tofu (1/2 block), blanched for 2-3 minutes (wrap in cheesecloth first)
Tabasco OR hot sauce to taste
instructions: Combine horseradish, tofu, oil, miso, water, garlic, lemon, vinegar, umeboshi paste, and mustard in blender. Process until creamy and smooth.
Add pepper and Tabasco to taste, blending until incorporated.

title: TOFU "SOUR CREAM"
ingredients: 1/2 pound soft tofu, blanched and drained
1 tablespoon chickpea miso or more to taste
1 tablespoon umeboshi paste or to taste
instructions: In food processor, combine all ingredients; process until smooth and creamy. Adjust seasoning to taste.

title: TOFU TERIYAKI
instructions: Slice tofu in half horizontally. Place tofu on plate between paper towels and weigh down for 20-30 minutes to extract water. Slice pressed tofu into 1/2 inch slabs.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium to medium-high heat and sear tofu slices until golden on both sides.
Drain any excess oil. Add teriyaki sauce to tofu and reduce over high heat until sauce is syrupy (about 15 minutes).

title: TERIYAKI SAUCE
instructions: In blender, combine all ingredients and process until smooth.

title: Pan-Fried Tempeh
instructions: In sauce pan, combine marinade ingredients.
Slice tempeh into diagonal slabs about 1/4 inch thick.
Combine tempeh with marinade.
Bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes.
In colander, drain marinade from tempeh.

In sauté pan, heat enough olive oil to cover bottom half of tempeh slabs. Prepare a plate with paper towels for draining. Pan-fry tempeh on both sides until golden. If broiling, brush tempeh with olive oil and broil on both sides.
Drain cooked tempeh slices before serving.

title: Miso-Marinated Tofu
ingredients: 14 ounces tofu (1 block), sliced into 6 slices (lengthwise)
instructions: Turn salamander on high heat.
Pour marinade in 12-inch sauté pan. Place tofu slices in pan.
Bring marinade to boil then reduce to low simmer.
Simmer tofu for 15 minutes on one side, then gently flip tofu and simmer for another 15 minutes on other side. As tofu simmers, marinade should reduce considerably and become thick.
Remove tofu from marinade. Spread any remaining marinade on top of tofu.
Broil tofu under salamander for approximately 5-7 minutes or until it begins to caramelize.

title: Miso Marinade
instructions: Combine all ingredients in blender until smooth and well mixed.

title: Miso-Marinated Salmon
ingredients: 1 1/2 pounds salmon filet, skinned and cut into 6 portions
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F degrees.
Combine salmon filets in large bowl with miso marinade. Marinate salmon for 30-40 minutes.
Remove filets from marinade and transfer to parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake salmon for 8-10 minutes or until flesh is springy to touch.

Alternatively, heat large sauté pan and add sesame oil. Sear top (presentation side) of salmon filets until caramelized, flip filets, then place sauté pan in oven. Cook 8-10 minutes or until flesh is springy to touch.

title: Homemade Tofu
ingredients: 2 cups soybeans, soaked overnight and drained
2 teaspoons nigari OR 1/2 cup lemon juice OR 1/2 cup vinegar
instructions: Line wooden tofu mold with 2 layers of wet cheesecloth. Set aside.
Heat 7 1/2 cups water in a 2-gallon pot over medium-high heat.
Puree soybeans in 2 batches with 6 cups cold water in blender (about 2 minutes per batch). Add puree to pot with heating water. Stirring constantly, bring contents of pot to boil.

Strain soybean-water mixture through fine chinois (or through strainer lined with double layers of cheesecloth) into a second large pot, pressing out all excess liquid. Discard soy pulp ('okara') or reserve for later use.
Bring strained soymilk to boil. Reduce heat and gently simmer, uncovered, 10 minutes. Remove pot from heat.

Dissolve nigari or other solidifier in 1 cup cold water. Add 1/3 of solidifier mixture slowly to hot soymilk, while gently stirring. Cover pot and allow to stand 3 minutes or until milk has separated into soft white curds.
Add remaining solidifier in two more batches, stirring each time and covering for 3 minutes. Soymilk should be completely separated into curds and whey.
Using slotted spoon, ladle bean curd into tofu mold; press with 2-3 pound weight for 25-30 minutes until firm and solid.

title: Basic Tempeh
ingredients: 8 ounce package tempeh, cut in two equal halves
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine shoyu, ginger, kombu, sea salt, and tempeh. Pour in enough water to cover tempeh (about 3 cups). Cover pan and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer about 20-25 minutes.
Drain tempeh and set aside. Reserve cooking liquid for use in Tempeh Scaloppini.

title: Tempeh Scaloppini
ingredients: 8-ounce package tempeh, simmered in marinade (see Basic Tempeh recipe)
3/4 cup reserved marinade (from Basic Tempeh recipe)
1/3 - 3/4 cup white wine (or red wine or sherry)
2-3 ounces sliced fresh shiitake and/or cremini mushrooms
instructions: Cut tempeh prepared in Basic Tempeh Recipe in half widthwise on diagonal. Slice each half on diagonal into 6-8 thin slices.
Dredge each slice in flour. Shake off excess.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium-high flame and fry tempeh until golden on both sides. Drain any excess oil if necessary.
Add reserved liquid from Basic Tempeh recipe, water, and wine to tempeh. Cover and bring to boil.
Add sliced mushrooms and garlic, reduce heat to low and cook covered until mushrooms release juices and become tender.
Add basil, oregano and thyme; simmer until liquid thickens (needs to be saucy).
Remove from heat and stir in lemon juice to taste.
Garnish with parsley and serve with rice, noodles or sautéed greens.

title: Family Style Bean Curd
ingredients: 14-ounce block of firm tofu, pressed 30 minutes
1 1/2 teaspoons peeled ginger root, cut into thin matchsticks (1/2-inch knob)
3 dried shiitake mushrooms (rehydrated in hot water until completely tender), cut into paper thin slices
1/3 cup canned water chestnuts, peeled and sliced
1 1/3 cups hot stock OR hot water
Garnish: 1 scallion, cut into thin diagonal
instructions: Cut tofu into 6 (1/2-inch) slices, then into triangles.
Heat wok and add coconut oil. Prepare plate with paper towel for draining. Deep fry tofu until golden brown.
Transfer fried tofu to paper towel to drain. Let oil cool completely before removing from wok.
Place deep fried tofu in small pot with water to cover. Add shoyu and ginger juice.
Simmer 15 minutes.
Heat wok and add sesame oil. Add scallions and ginger root. When their aroma is imparted, add carrots, mushrooms, water chestnuts, and fried tofu to wok.
Add hot stock, shoyu, mirin, and brown rice syrup and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
Dissolve kuzu in cold water and add to wok. Simmer 1 minute. Adjust ginger juice and shoyu or tamari if necessary.
Serve over rice or noodles. Garnish with scallions.

title: Barbecue Tofu
ingredients: 3-4 tablespoons canola or coconut oil (for grilling)
instructions: Cut tofu in half horizontally (about 3/4” thick). Press fifteen minutes between paper towels.
Slice tofu into finger-sized pieces (about 1 inch wide).
Heat grill, brush with canola or coconut oil. Place tofu on griddle. Grill on each side about 2-3 minutes or until grill marks appear.
In large sauté pan, combine all marinade ingredients. Transfer tofu to marinade and simmer gently 25-30 minutes or place sauté pan in 350° oven for 20 minutes.
Remove tofu from pan. Reduce sauce to desired consistency.

title: TEMPEH SAUSAGE
instructions: Cut tempeh into 2-inch pieces and steam for 20 minutes in steamer basket over simmering water. Cool to room temperature.
Grate tempeh on large holes on box grater. Combine grated tempeh with garlic, water, olive oil, tamari, sage, marjoram, thyme, paprika, cayenne, fennel seeds, pepper, and oat flour. Mix well.
Form mixture into walnut-sized balls using 1-ounce ice cream scoop and press into patties. Dredge in cornmeal or flour.
Prepare plate with paper towel for draining. In sauté pan, heat oil and fry patties until well browned on both sides. Drain tempeh.

title: TOFU BURGERS
ingredients: 1 cup cooked millet OR rice OR bread crumbs
2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil OR sesame oil
1 teaspoon curry powder OR other spice blend
1/4 teaspoon black pepper or more to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Mix seeds and shoyu together. Roast on parchment paper until dried and toasted.
Prepare millet (2 cups water to 1 cup millet for 30 minutes) or brown rice (2 1/2 cups water to 1 cup rice for 55 minutes).
Press tofu to remove excess water by placing tofu between paper towels and putting weighted plate on top. Let sit at least 5-10 minutes.
Soak arame in water 10-15 minutes. Drain and chop finely.
Heat oil, sauté garlic, onion, spices, carrot and celery for 2-3 minutes.

Grind sunflower seeds halfway in food processor. In a separate bowl, mash tofu, add sautéed vegetables, 1 cup cooked grain, ground sunflower seeds, arame, ume, tahini, and salt and pepper to taste. Adjust salt if needed. Mix well.
Form 1/2" thick burgers with about 1/2 cup mixture.
Bake burgers at 350°F until set (about 15-20 minutes) or pan fry (dredge in corn meal first) on each side, only turning once until golden (drain on paper towels).
Serve on whole wheat burger bun with lettuce, red onion slice, and mustard.

title: TEMPEH VEGETABLE KEBOBS
ingredients: 6 large cremini mushrooms, stemmed and cut in half
1/2 zucchini, cut in half lengthwise, then into 1/2 inch slices
1/3 yellow pepper, cut into twelve equal pieces
1 tablespoon arrowroot, dissolved in 2 tablespoons apple juice
instructions: Whisk together tempeh marinade ingredients in mixing bowl; pour into 2 1/2 quart pot.

Cut tempeh in two. Add tempeh to marinade. Bring marinade to boil, lower heat and simmer covered for 45 minutes. Drain tempeh and pat dry, reserving marinade (there should be 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons).

Whisk together vegetable marinade ingredients in mixing bowl. Add all vegetables to marinade and let sit for 20-30 minutes, stirring often. Remove vegetables with slotted spoon (set aside marinade for later use).
When tempeh reaches room temperature, cut into 24 cubes and brush all sides with oil.
Preheat grooved griddle over medium-high heat while preparing kebobs; use one piece of each vegetable and two pieces of tempeh for each skewer (alternating vegetables and tempeh).
Grill kebobs few minutes on each side; continue cooking few minutes longer, brushing kebobs with vegetable marinade on all sides, until vegetables are tender.

Bring reserved tempeh marinade to boil in 8-inch saucepan; whisk in dissolved arrowroot, stirring constantly until sauce comes back to boil and becomes clear and thick. Use sauce for kebobs and grain accompaniment. Kebobs can be baked in 400°F oven for 20-30 minutes as an alternative to grilling. Brush them occasionally with vegetable marinade as they cook.

title: SHAVED FENNEL & ORANGE SALAD W/ PISTACHIO COATED 'CHEESE' MEDALLIONS
ingredients: 3 Romaine hearts, sliced into 3/4 inch pieces
1 head radicchio, sliced into thin ribbons
4 oranges, pith removed and cut into rounds
1/3 teaspoon sea salt, more to taste
1 cup cashews, soaked overnight and drained
instructions: Toss arugula, romaine, and radicchio in bowl and refrigerate until ready to serve.
In separate bowl, combine shaved fennel and cut citrus to prevent oxidation.
Add zest, orange juice, salt, maple syrup, minced shallot and Dijon into medium bowl. Whisk to combine. Slowly pour in oil while whisking constantly to emulsify dressing.

Preheat oven to 300°F. Line baking tray with Silpat. Toss pistachios with maple syrup, paprika, and cayenne. Spread mixture onto tray and bake for 10 to 15 minutes, or until syrup crystalizes, stirring once. Cool slightly, and chop pistachios into small pieces.

Combine soaked cashews, lemon juice, miso, salt, melted coconut oil, and boiling water in food processor. Process for 1 minute, or until smooth, scraping down sides occasionally. Add pectin powder and pulse until combined. Stream in calcium water while engine is running. Transfer mixture to bowl and refrigerate for 10 to 20 minutes, or until slightly firm. Scoop into medallions using retractable ice cream scoop. Roll sides in crushed pistachios.
Toss greens with citrus dressing. Top with orange rounds and fennel. Garnish with nut 'cheese' medallions and fennel fronds.

title: ZUCCHINI PEANUT NOODLE BOWL W/ SRIRACHA STRING BEANS & LEMONGRASS TOFU
ingredients: 1 1/2 teaspoon sea salt, or to taste
1/2 teaspoon sea salt, or to taste
2 blocks firm tofu, cut into small even cubes
instructions: Cut zucchinis in half crosswise, and position on spiralizer. Turn to create zucchini noodles, trimming them with scissors if they are too long. Toss with cilantro before serving.
Add peanuts, water, garlic, sesame oil, maple syrup, lime juice, cayenne, coconut milk and salt to Vitamix, or high-speed blender, and puree until smooth.
Bring medium pot with steamer basket, filled with 2 inches of water to boil. Steam string beans 3 minutes, or until bright green and slightly tender. Cut in half on diagonal.
Heat coconut oil over medium heat in large sauté pan. Cook beans with salt for 2 minutes, stirring often. Add sriracha, syrup, and sesame seeds and cook for additional 30 seconds.
Preheat oven to 375°F. Arrange tofu in single layer in baking dish.

In medium bowl whisk together sesame oil, vinegar, apple juice, tamari, garlic and ginger. Pour over tofu, add lemongrass, and marinate for 15 to 30 minutes. Bake uncovered for 30 to 40 minutes, or until tofu is golden and has absorbed most of liquid. Discard lemongrass.
Combine turmeric and sesame in small saucepan. Warm for 30 seconds, remove from heat, strain out turmeric before using.
Arrange noodles in bowl. Top with string beans, tofu, turmeric oil, microgreens, cabbage, and peanut sauce.

title: AIRLINE CHICKEN BREASTS WITH COMPOUND BUTTER
ingredients: 2 pounds airline chicken breasts (4), skin intact
3 tablespoons chopped garlic (3 large cloves garlic)
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Combine chicken breasts in medium bowl with water and salt to brine. Refrigerate one hour. Remove breasts from water and dry well.
Combine butter, garlic, lemon zest, lemon juice, and basil in food processor. Process until ingredients are well incorporated and butter is smooth.
Remove compound butter mixture from processor and divide into 4 equal portions. Set aside.
Preheat convection oven to 400°F.
Gently separate skin from breast, taking care not to tear it. Spread compound butter evenly between skin and breast. Season skin with salt and pepper.
Heat ghee in 12-inch sauté pan. Place breasts skin side down in pan. Sear over medium-high heat to caramelize skin.
Transfer pan to oven and roast breasts for 15-20 minutes or until juices run clear. Do not turn chicken over while cooking. Serve skin side up.

title: BASIC BROILED CHICKEN
ingredients: 1/2 teaspoon sea salt or more to taste
instructions: Preheat broiler 20 minutes prior to using. Adjust rack to lowest setting.
Rub both skin side and inside of chicken with olive oil, salt, and fresh ground black pepper.
Heat 10-inch sauté pan over medium flame and place chicken, skin side down, in pan. Do not add any extra oil to pan. Transfer chicken to broiler.

Broil chicken skin side down for 15 minutes. Carefully flip chicken skin side up, and continue to broil for another 20 minutes. Depending on size of chicken, full cooking may need to be completed in preheated convection oven at 400°F for another 10-15 minutes.

title: BASIC CHICKEN STOCK
ingredients: 8 ounces yellow onion (1 medium), large dice
Sachet of 2 bay leaves, 4 sprigs thyme, 2 teaspoons black peppercorns, 1/2 ounce parsley stems
instructions: Combine bones, onion, carrot, celery, sachet, and water in large stock pot.
Cover pot and bring to boil. Remove cover and skim any foam off top.
Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 3-4 hours.
Strain stock immediately, first through china cap, then chinois.
Cool stock in ice bath and refrigerate.

title: BRAISED CHICKEN WITH VEGETABLES AND TARRAGON
ingredients: 1 cup whole wheat pastry flour, sifted
Sachet of 2 bay leaves, 10 black peppercorns, 6 sprigs thyme, 6 parsley sprigs, 1/2 ounce tarragon
instructions: Heat oven to 325°F.
Toss chicken legs in bowl with flour, salt, and pepper to thoroughly coat.
Heat oil in 12-inch sauté pan on medium-high flame.
When oil is hot, lower heat and sear chicken on both sides, being careful not to burn it.
Add carrots, celery, onion, and flour; sauté for 1 minute. Add potatoes, salt, pepper, and sachet. Stir in white wine to deglaze. Add stock, cover pot, and bring braise to boil.
Transfer pan to oven and cook for 40-45 minutes or until chicken is thoroughly cooked and vegetables are tender but not mushy.
Garnish with tarragon. Season to taste and serve.

title: CHICKEN UNDER A BRICK
instructions: Heat convection oven to 400°F.
Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in 10-inch sauté pan over high flame. When oil is hot but not yet smoking, add chicken, skin side down. Place foil covered brick on top of chicken. Sear for 5 minutes.
Transfer pan to oven and cook for 20 minutes. Remove from oven and remove brick.
Turn chicken skin side up, and carefully pour off excess fat.
Transfer pan back to oven for additional 5-10 minutes, with fan turned to high.
Serve immediately.

title: CHICKEN KOTLETKY (CHICKEN BURGERS)
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F.
Toss bread crumbs and milk in medium-size bowl until bread has soaked up all milk. Combine bread crumb mixture with ground chicken, onions, dill weed, salt, and pepper.
Using 1/4 cup measure, portion chicken burgers.
Heat oil in 12-inch sauté pan over medium-high flame, and lightly brown patties on both sides.
Transfer burgers from sauté pan to half sheet tray lined with parchment.
Transfer burgers to oven and bake for 15 minutes.
Garnish with sour cream and fresh dill sprigs.

title: CHICKEN POT PIE FILLING WITH TOASTED BREADCRUMBS
ingredients: 16 bone-in chicken thighs with skin (5 pounds)
1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
3/4 cup whole wheat pastry flour, sifted
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat convection oven to 400°F.

Combine thighs and oil in bowl. Coat thighs with 1/4 cup olive oil. Season with salt and pepper. Transfer chicken to parchment-lined half sheet pan, skin side up, and roast for 20 to 25 minutes, until cooked through but still moist. After chicken has cooled enough to handle, remove and discard skin and bones. Cut chicken into large dice and set aside.

In 1-gallon pot, melt butter over medium flame. Add onion and sweat until translucent (about 10 minutes). Stir in flour and continue to cook over low heat about 7-8 minutes, stirring constantly. Add chicken stock and simmer over low heat for another 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Bring 1 gallon water to boil and add 1 tablespoon salt. Prepare ice bath. Blanch carrots and celery for 5 minutes in boiling, salted water. Shock in ice water, drain, and set aside.
Add chicken, carrots, celery, peas, and fresh thyme to pot with chicken stock and continue to cook over low heat for an additional 5 minutes. Check seasoning and adjust if necessary.

Heat 10-inch sauté pan over low heat. Add Panko and remaining 2 tablespoons of oil. Carefully sauté Panko until golden brown. Add parsley and season lightly with salt and pepper, continuing to toast 2 minutes more.
Divide pot pie mixture equally between 8 bowls and top with breadcrumb mixture.

title: GRILLED CHICKEN TENDERS WITH PEANUT SAUCE
instructions: Soak 8 wooden skewers in bowl of water to prevent burning on grill.
In medium-size bowl, whisk together sesame oil, tamari, mirin, and red pepper flakes. Add tenders to mixture and marinate for 45 minutes.
Heat cast iron griddle grill over medium heat.
Thread tenders on skewers. Depending on size, use one or two tenders per skewer. Discard marinade.
Grill tenders each side about 4 minutes or until chicken has grill marks and is opaque in appearance. Serve with peanut sauce.

title: HOT WINGS WITH STILTON-YOGURT DRESSING
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups whole wheat pastry flour, sifted
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat convection oven to 400°F.
In medium bowl, combine flour, paprika, garlic powder, thyme, pepper, cayenne, and salt.
Toss wings in canola oil, then flour-spice mixture, and shake off excess.
Transfer wings to baker’s rack atop half sheet tray and roast for 15 minutes. Flip wings and roast an additional 15 minutes.
Melt butter and combine with Tabasco and maple syrup in medium bowl.
Add cooked wings to hot sauce-butter mixture and toss to coat.
Combine strained yogurt and cheese. Mix thoroughly. Serve with wings.

title: SAUTÉED CHICKEN BREAST MARSALA WITH CRIMINI MUSHROOMS
ingredients: 1/2 cup whole wheat pastry flour, sifted
1/2 pound cremini mushrooms, stemmed, thinly sliced
instructions: Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper, then lightly dust with flour and shake off excess.
Heat olive oil over medium-high flame in 12-inch sauté pan. Cook breasts on each side for approximately 3-4 minutes. Remove chicken from pan and set aside. Add shallots to pan and sauté for 1 minute.
Add mushrooms to pan and sear until caramelized. Sear in batches, if necessary. Do not crowd pan.
Remove pan from heat, add chicken back to pan, and pour Marsala wine over chicken. Place pan over flame again and tilt pan carefully toward flame to flambé.
When flames subside, add stock and simmer over medium to high heat to reduce stock au sec (almost dry), about 2 to 4 minutes.
Add cold butter cubes to pan one at a time and swirl until dissolved.
Garnish with chopped parsley.

title: SAUTÉED CHICKEN LIVERS WITH ONION, APPLE, AND FRISÉE
ingredients: 1 pound red onion (3 medium), sauté slice
16 chicken livers, cleaned, rinsed, patted dry
Sea salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
instructions: Heat 3 tablespoons oil in large sauté pan over medium heat. Sweat onions until translucent. Add vinegar and raise heat slightly. Continue to cook until onions begin to brown and caramelize, adding a bit of water if onions begin to stick to pan. When onions are tender and browned, add salt and pepper. Remove onions from pan and set aside.

Season livers with salt and pepper. Add remaining 2 tablespoons oil to same pan and heat over medium-high flame. Add livers, making sure not to crowd pan. Sauté livers about 2 minutes on each side. When livers are done, add brandy to pan, and swirl to combine.
Transfer cooked livers to paper-lined sheet tray to drain.
Add onions back to pan, followed by livers.
Toss lemon juice in bowl with apples, salt, and pepper. Add frisée and set aside.
In separate small bowl, combine vinegar, mustard, salt, and pepper. Slowly whisk in oil until dressing has emulsified.
Toss salad with dressing. Serve with caramelized onions and liver.

title: BASIC SEITAN RECIPE
ingredients: extra white bread flour for kneading
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, combine water, shoyu, ginger, garlic, bay leaf, and kombu. Bring stock to boil, reduce to simmer, and cover.
Combine flours in large mixing bowl, stirring well. Pour in water gradually, stirring to form dough that comes together in a ball. Add only enough water until dough is kneadable consistency.
Knead dough well to thoroughly and evenly develop gluten, about 15 minutes. Add extra flour if dough gets too sticky.
Cover dough with cold water in bowl and keep submerged for at least 30 minutes. Dough can be stored in refrigerator overnight and rinsed next day. As dough rests, gluten continues to develop.
Transfer dough from bowl to colander and place in sink. Under warm running water, carefully knead dough, rinsing out starch and bran. Water will become milky as starch comes out.

At a certain point, it will seem as though dough is falling apart. After several minutes of rinsing and kneading, however, gluten will start to tighten again. Continue to knead dough until it has firm, tight, and rubbery texture.
Pull pieces of gluten into small billiard-size balls. Drop gluten into stock, one piece at a time, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
Simmer seitan for 1 ½ to 2 hours, depending on size of pieces.
For improved flavor and firmer texture, cool seitan to room temperature in broth. Store in refrigerator, submerged in broth, or freeze.

title: GLAZED SEITAN STIR FRY
ingredients: 12 ounces seitan, cut into ½-inch wide strips and patted dry
4 ounces snow peas, cut in half on bias
1 tablespoon arrowroot dissolved in 2 tablespoons cold water
½ cup cashews, toasted, coarsely chopped
instructions: In wok, heat oil over medium-high flame. Add seitan and stir-fry until caramelized.

Add onions and stir-fry until lightly caramelized. Add carrots and continue stir-frying until carrots are just tender. Add garlic, ginger, snow peas, and red pepper flakes. Continue to stir-fry until all vegetables are tender but crisp.
In small bowl, combine all glaze ingredients and whisk to combine. Pour glaze over vegetables and seitan. Mix to combine, cooking until mixture thickens and all ingredients are evenly coated.
Drizzle with toasted sesame oil and garnish with cashews and scallions.

title: SEITAN STEW
ingredients: 2 ribs celery, ½-inch thick diagonal slices
6 ounces burdock (1 piece), ½-inch roll cut
12 ounces carrot (1 large), ½-inch roll cut
1 pound daikon (1 medium), ½-inch half moon slices
½ pound shiitake mushrooms, stemmed and halved
2 pounds butternut squash (1 medium), peeled, 1-inch dice
4 ounces green beans, trimmed and cut into 2-inch pieces
5 tablespoons kuzu, dissolved in ¼ cup cold stock
Sea salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
2 scallions, thin diagonal slice, for garnish
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat olive oil over medium flame and sweat onion until softened. Add celery, burdock, carrots, and daikon. Sweat until vegetables begin to soften, about 5-7 minutes. Add mushrooms and sweat until tender, about 1 minute.

Add squash and stock to just cover vegetables, about 6 cups. Cover pot and bring to boil. Lower heat and add bay leaves, seitan, and tamari. Cover and simmer 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until all vegetables are tender and squash has started to break down. Add green beans and simmer until beans are just tender.
Add kuzu slurry and simmer until mixture slightly thickens. Stir in ginger juice. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Garnish with scallions.

title: Seitan Croquettes
ingredients: 1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil, divided
2 tablespoons flax, ground, and mixed with 3 tablespoons water
1/2 cup toasted and ground sunflower seeds
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat 1 tablespoon oil over medium-high flame. Sauté onion until caramelized. Transfer to medium bowl and set aside.

Squeeze all excess liquid from seitan. In food processor, pulse seitan to texture of ground beef. Transfer seitan to bowl with onions. Mix in flax mixture, salt, sage, fennel seeds, and sunflower seeds to combine.

Form seitan mixture into small patties using 1/4 cup measure. Dredge in flour. Heat remaining 3 tablespoons oil in same 10-inch sauté pan used to caramelize onions. Pan-sear croquettes for several minutes on both sides.
Combine red onion in small bowl with vinegar, agave, and salt. Mix to combine.
Garnish croquettes with mustard, red onion and chives.

title: Seitan Bordelaise
ingredients: 2 ounces (12) shiitake mushrooms, stemmed, thinly sliced
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Dredge seitan in flour and shake off any excess.
In 10-inch sauté pan over medium-high flame, heat oil and sear seitan until lightly caramelized on both sides. Remove seitan from pan and set aside. Add shallots to pan and sauté until caramelized.
Add seitan back to pan with stock, red wine, shiitake mushrooms, and thyme. Simmer until liquid is reduced to nappé consistency.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: Seitan Kebabs with Yellow Rice
ingredients: 1 pound fresh seitan, cut in 2-inch chunks
1 pound crimini mushrooms, cleaned, stemmed, and halved
2 tablespoons kuzu dissolved in 2 tablespoons water for thickening marinade
2 cups long grain brown rice, rinsed and drained
instructions: Soak 25 skewers in cold water for 30 minutes.

In large bowl, combine seitan, onions, mushrooms, and cherry tomatoes with marinade ingredients for 30 minutes. Strain seitan and vegetables from marinade, but reserve marinade to make sauce (step 5).
Heat griddle grill. Make skewers with 2 pieces seitan, 2 pieces mushrooms, 2 pieces tomato, and red onion, alternating seitan and vegetables.
Grill kebabs 3-5 minutes on each side or until grill marks appear. Set aside.
Bring marinade to simmer. Whisk in kuzu slurry, stirring constantly, until marinade thickens to sauce consistency (approximately 5 minutes).
Serve atop or alongside Yellow Rice. Pour marinade sauce over kebabs or serve alongside.
In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine rice, water, salt, and turmeric. Bring rice to boil, cover pot, reduce heat, and simmer for approximately 40-50 minutes or until all water is absorbed.
Remove rice from heat, and set rice aside covered to steam 10 minutes more.
Fluff rice lightly with fork. Add parsley and mix to combine. Serve alongside or underneath Seitan Kebabs.

title: Indian-Style Seitan Kebabs with Mango Coulis
ingredients: 1/3 cup dried mango, soaked in 1 cup boiling water until soft (drain and reserve soaking liquid)
6 ounces purple pearl onions, trimmed (50 pieces)
1 1/2 cup chopped fresh mango (excess from mango pit and unused mango from skewers)
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F. Heat griddle grill. Toss seitan with oil in small bowl. Grill seitan on all sides and set aside. When cool, cut seitan into 1/2-inch pieces (approximately 50). Set aside.

In food processor, puree mango, tomato paste, agave, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, smoked paprika, cumin, fennel, turmeric, and salt, adding apple juice a bit at a time. Mixture should have thick, sauce-like consistency. If more liquid is needed, use reserved mango soaking liquid.
Transfer sauce from food processor to half-hotel pan. Add seitan and roast in oven approximately 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until much of sauce is absorbed.

In 2 1/2 quart pot, bring 1 quart water to boil with 1/2 teaspoon sea salt. Prepare an ice bath. Blanch pearl onions for one minute, shock in ice water, and drain. Remove onion skins and toss onions in small bowl with vinegar. Grill pearl onions until slightly charred. Set aside.
Dice mango into 25-30 half-inch pieces. Reserve mango scraped from pit and other unused cubes for mango coulis.
Place seitan on skewer followed by red onion and mango. Repeat sequence, finishing with one more piece of seitan, mango, and pearl onion.
Transfer leftover mango meat to blender. Add pinch of sea salt and splash of hot sauce. Blend mango mixture with leftover dried mango soaking liquid until smooth consistency is achieved.
Reheat skewers on sheet tray in oven and garnish with mango coulis and cilantro.

title: Jerk Seitan Sloppy Joe's
ingredients: 1 tablespoon plus 1 teaspoon olive oil, divided
1 pound seitan, pulsed in food processor to texture of ground beef
1 avocado, sliced into 12 wedges, tossed with 1 tablespoon lime juice (1 lime)
instructions: In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil on medium-high flame. Add onions and sauté until slightly caramelized.
Add seitan and sauté until caramelized, lower heat, and add garlic, thyme, paprika, salt, allspice, red pepper flakes, cinnamon, and black pepper. Sweat until onions are tender.
Add tomatoes, maple syrup, and mustard. Simmer 5-10 minutes or until desired 'stewy' consistency.
Season to taste with lime and ginger juices.
Heat cast iron pan or griddle on medium-low flame. Place tortillas on pan or griddle and heat until softened and flexible.
Dollop 1/4 cup of seitan mixture into each tortilla. Garnish with 1 slice of avocado and cilantro.

title: Almond-Fruit Tart with Almond Crust
ingredients: 2 Tbsp kuzu dissolved in 2 Tbsp water
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (300°F in convection). Gather 6 four-inch, non-stick paper tart pans.
In food processor, grind almonds, oats, and oat flour together until pulverized to fine meal.
In small bowl, combine maple syrup, salt, and oil. Whisk until emulsified.
Pour maple-oil mixture into food processor, pulsing to form cohesive dough.
Using fingers, press dough evenly into miniature tart pans. Bake 15-20 minutes or until edges of crust are browned. Set aside to cool.
Combine agar, water, and maple syrup in 2 1/2 quart saucepan. Soak agar 10 minutes.
Bring agar mixture to boil, lower heat and simmer until agar is dissolved, about 15 minutes. Whisk or stir often, scraping agar from sides and bottom of saucepan.
Stir almond milk, salt, and vanilla and almond extracts into agar mixture. Bring mixture to simmer, whisking often.
Add kuzu slurry, whisking vigorously, until mixture comes to boil. Cook few minutes longer.
Pour mixture into shallow pan and chill in freezer or refrigerator until set.
In food processor, whip chilled mixture until very smooth and creamy. Halfway through processing, stop machine and scrape sides of work bowl with spatula.
Fill tart crusts with Almond Pastry Cream. Arrange fruit on top of cream. Serve with pistachios, flowers, freeze-dried raspberries and raspberry sauce (recipe follows).

title: Rich Almond Milk (for Almond Fruit Tart)
instructions: Combine almonds and water in blender or VitaMix. Process for few minutes until well-homogenized.
Strain almond milk through fine sieve or nut milk bag. Add vanilla. Set aside 2 cups for almond cream recipe (recipe following).

title: Raspberry Sauce (for Almond Fruit Tart)
instructions: Combine maple crystals and water in a pot and stir until dissolved.
Combine frozen raspberries and hot syrup in a food processor. Pulse a few times till pureed.

Pour puree through a fine mesh strainer set over a medium-size bowl. Stir and push on the solids with the back of a rubber spatula until all of the liquid has been extracted. This will take several minutes as the mixture will be thick.
Discard the seeds. Add the liqueur, if using, and stir to combine.

title: Golden Turmeric 'Panna Cotta' with Ginger-Pepper Lace Cookies
ingredients: 1/2 cup pecans, toasted and coarsely chopped
instructions: Grease inside of small silicone molds with oil.

Combine agar flakes and milk in medium saucepan and simmer over medium heat until agar flakes have dissolved. Add cardamom, turmeric, ginger, maple syrup, vanilla bean, and salt. Simmer an additional 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Strain mixture through chinois and pour into silicone molds. Cool until firm.
In blender, puree blackberries and maple syrup. Strain coulis and transfer to squeeze bottle.
Preheat convection oven to 300°F. Line 5 half-sheet trays with Silpats.
Transfer pecans to medium bowl. Add flour and arrowroot. Whisk to combine.
In separate bowl, whisk oil, rice syrup, maple syrup, vanilla, ginger, black pepper, and salt. Add to pecan/flour mixture, mixing until combined. Let batter sit for 15 minutes.
Use teaspoon to drop batter onto Silpat-lined baking sheets. Place only 5 cookies per sheet – one in each corner and one in middle. Bake one sheet at a time for 10 minutes until cookies are bubbly.

Cool cookies for approximately 30 seconds before removing from Silpats. Working quickly (because cookies cool and set hard very fast), remove tuiles from sheet tray using small offset spatula and wrap around cannoli molds. Repeat procedure with remaining sheet trays until all batter is used.
Invert panna cotta onto plate. Garnish with tuile, blackberries, gold dust, blackberry coulis and mint sprigs.

title: Hemp Coconut 'Milk' (for Golden Turmeric 'Panna Cotta')
instructions: Add hemp seeds, coconut milk, water, and salt to a high-speed blender.
Blend mixture until smooth, and strain through fine mesh strainer (or nut milk bag), if finer texture is desired.
Serve warm or cold, or use in your favorite recipes. Store in refrigerator in an airtight container for 3 to 5 days.

title: Red Cabbage-Caraway Sauerkraut
ingredients: 1 head red cabbage (about 2 pounds), quartered, core removed and thinly sliced
instructions: Place cabbage in large bowl and sprinkle with salt. Squeeze and massage cabbage to release water. Continue to massage and squeeze until enough liquid has been released to create salty brine that covers cabbage by 1/4 inch. Add caraway seeds.

Pack tightly into 2 quart wide-mouthed glass jar or ceramic crock (liquid brine must be above cabbage for healthy successful fermentation). If cabbage has not released enough juices to cover, add salted water to jar/crock in a ratio of 1 teaspoon sea salt per 1 cup water.

Place plate or discover cabbage and press down to squeeze more brine to surface. Cover mouth of vessel with cheesecloth and secure to rim of lid with kitchen twine, rubber band, OR if using airlock lid, fill airlock and secure lid.

Store sauerkraut in dry place for 2 - 4 weeks at room temperature. Be sure to check water level, adding more salt water as needed to ensure the mixture is covered throughout fermentation process. Taste every few days and eat or refrigerate when desired tangy flavor is achieved.

title: Green Cabbage Sauerkraut with Ginger and Turmeric
ingredients: 1 head green cabbage (about 2 pounds), quartered, core removed and thinly sliced
instructions: Place cabbage in large bowl and sprinkle with salt. Squeeze and massage cabbage to release water. Continue to massage and squeeze until enough liquid has been released to create salty brine that covers cabbage by 1/4 inch. Add ginger and turmeric.

Pack tightly into 2 quart wide-mouthed glass jar or ceramic crock (liquid brine must be above cabbage for healthy successful fermentation). If cabbage has not released enough juices to cover, add salted water to jar/crock in a ratio of 1 teaspoon sea salt per 1 cup water.

Place plate or discover cabbage and press down to squeeze more brine to the surface. Cover mouth of vessel with cheesecloth and secure to rim of lid with kitchen twine, rubber band, OR if using airlock lid, fill airlock and secure lid.

Store sauerkraut in dry place for 2 - 4 weeks at room temperature. Be sure to check water level, adding more salt water as needed to ensure the mixture is covered throughout fermentation process. Taste every few days and eat or refrigerate when desired tangy flavor is achieved.

title: BEET KVASS
ingredients: 2-3 medium organic beets (¾ pound), scrubbed (not peeled), chopped in 1-inch pieces
¼ cup whey or sauerkraut juice (optional, as starter)
1 tablespoon sea salt (or 1 ½ tablespoons if not using whey/juice)
instructions: Place beets in bottom of jar, add whey/juice (if using), and salt as needed.
Cover beets with water, place cheesecloth over mouth of jar and secure with string.
Set in cool dark spot for 2 days to ferment if using whey/juice or 3 days without.
Strain beets from liquid, and discard beets. Return liquid to jar and refrigerate.

title: DILL PICKLES
ingredients: ¼ cup sea salt, not iodized
1 ½ pounds Kirby or pickling cucumbers, 3-4 inches long
instructions: Combine salt and water in pitcher and stir until the salt has dissolved.
Rinse cucumbers thoroughly and snip off the blossom end stem. Set aside.

Place half peppercorns, garlic, mustard seed, and fresh dill into clean half-gallon fermentation jar or crock. Add cucumbers to crock on top of aromatics. Add the remainder of peppercorns, seeds, garlic, and dill. Pour brine mixture over cucumbers to cover them completely.

If using crock or jar without airlock lid, pour remaining water into 1-gallon zip-top plastic bag and seal. Place bag on top of pickles ensuring all of them are completely submerged in brine. Store in cool, dry place, OR if using a fermentation jar, fill airlock and secure lid.
Check ferment every day. Fermentation has begun if you see bubbles rising to top of crock. Skim off any scum that forms. If scum forms on plastic bag, rinse it off and return to top of crock.

Fermentation is complete when pickles taste sour and bubbles have stopped rising; this should take approximately 6 to 7 days. Once this happens, cover the crock loosely and place in refrigerator for 3 days, skimming daily or as needed.
Store for up to 2 months in refrigerator.

title: HORSERADISH-CRUSTED SALMON
ingredients: 1 ½ pounds salmon, skinned and divided into six 4-ounce portions
Salt and pepper, to season fish
1 ounce parsley, stemmed and finely chopped
3 ounces fresh horseradish, peeled and finely grated
Juice of 2 lemons, approximately ¼ cup
instructions: Heat convection oven to 375°F.
Season filet with salt and pepper.
Combine mayonnaise, parsley, horseradish, lemon zest, lemon, bread crumbs, salt, and pepper in large bowl. Stir to combine.
Spread mixture in even layer over flesh side of fish.
Transfer to half sheet tray lined with lightly oiled parchment paper.
Bake for 5-10 minutes, depending on thickness of fish filet.

title: MISO-GLAZED WHITE FISH
ingredients: 8 white fish filets, skin on, divided into eight 4-ounce portions
instructions: Combine sake, mirin, miso, rice syrup, and shoyu in small pot. Whisk to emulsify. Transfer to ice bath to quickly cool.
Place filets in shallow hotel pan and pour marinade over them. Refrigerate for 1 hour.
Heat broiler. Lightly oil half sheet tray. Arrange filets skin side down on tray. Broil 5-7 minutes.

title: POTATO-CRUSTED BASS FILETS
ingredients: 1 pound russet potatoes, peeled, grated, soaked for 15 minutes in water
1/4 ounce thyme, picked and finely chopped
8 bass filets, skin removed, seasoned with salt and pepper
instructions: Drain potatoes, squeeze as much excess water from potatoes as possible with cheesecloth. Set aside.
Combine flour, salt, pepper, and thyme in bowl. Set aside.
Combine and whisk eggs in large bowl.
Liberally season filets with salt and pepper.
Dredge fish filet with flour, shaking off excess.
Transfer filets to egg mixture, making sure to coat entire filet.
Press potatoes onto filet, completely coating them.
Heat non-stick sauté pan over medium heat with 1/4-inch canola oil.
Transfer filets to pan and brown on both sides. If filets are thick, transfer browned filets to 375°F oven to finish cooking.

title: TUNA CEVICHE
ingredients: 6 ounces red onion, thin salad slice
1 ounce serrano chili, seeded and minced
2 ounces cilantro, stemmed and finely chopped
instructions: Combine all ingredients in large bowl.
Cover with plastic wrap and transfer to refrigerator for 30-45 minutes before serving.

title: MONKFISH WITH LEMON-CAPER BUTTER
ingredients: 4 monkfish filets, seasoned with salt and pepper
Salt and pepper to season fish
instructions: Heat conventional oven to 375°F.
Dredge filets in flour. Shake off excess.
Heat olive oil in 10-inch sauté pan over medium heat.
Brown filets on both sides.
Transfer fish to sizzle platter, and complete cooking in oven, approximately 10 minutes. Set sauté pan for next step.
Add butter to pan over low heat, melt, and cook until butter achieves light brown color.
Add capers and lemon juice to browned butter.
Transfer fish from oven to plate and garnish with browned butter. Serve immediately.

title: HAZELNUT-CRUSTED FLOUNDER
ingredients: 8 flounder filets, seasoned with salt and pepper
instructions: Preheat conventional oven to 375°F.
Combine hazelnuts and breadcrumbs in food processor. Pulse mixture until uniformly crumb-sized. Transfer mixture to half-hotel pan. Transfer flour and eggs, each to separate half hotel pans.
Dredge fish filet in flour, shaking off excess. Transfer filets to egg mixture, making sure to coat entire filet. Press breading onto filet, completely to coat.

Heat canola oil in large sauté pan over medium-high heat. Add filets to pan, careful not to crowd them. Cook each side until golden brown, approximately 1-2 minutes per side. If fish is not cooked through, can pace onto parchment-lined sheet tray and place into oven for 5 minutes.

title: MANGO SALSA FOR CRUSTED FLOUNDER
ingredients: 1 ripe mango, skinned, pitted and medium dice
4 ounces cucumber, seeded and small dice
instructions: Combine all ingredients and serve atop crusted flounder.

title: SALT-CRUSTED BAKED BASS
ingredients: 1 (3-4 pound) whole bass, gills, fins, and viscera removed
Extra virgin olive oil for drizzling
instructions: Preheat oven to 450° F.
Wash fish in cold water and pat dry.
Insert thyme into cavity of fish.
In mixing bowl, whip egg whites until they form soft peaks.
Fold salt into egg whites to combine.
On large, ovenproof platter suitable for serving and presentation, place 1/4 of egg white mixture.
Place fish atop egg white mixture, then cover fish with remaining mixture.
Transfer platter in oven and bake approximately 35 to 40 minutes.
Crack crust with sharp knife and carefully remove.

title: Sweet and Sour Sauce
instructions: In bowl, whisk all ingredients together. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1/2 hour.

title: Scallion-Ginger Sauce
instructions: In bowl mix all ingredients together. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1/2 hour.

title: WHOLE STEAMED FISH
ingredients: 2 1/2-3 pound whole dressed fish, head and tail intact, gills removed
1 ounce cilantro, leaves picked and washed
instructions: Rinse fish, removing any remaining scales or blood. Score fish by making two, 3-inch long/1/4-inch deep cuts into each side of fish.
Season outside and cavity of fish with salt and pepper.
In bowl, toss cilantro, scallion, garlic, and ginger together. Stuff half of mixture into cavity of fish.

Combine remaining herb/spice mixture with water, tamari, and sesame oil. Transfer mixture to deep hotel pan. Place lightly oiled perforated pan over mixture. Bring mixture to boil over high flame and reduce heat to simmer. Transfer fish to perforated pan. Cover pan with lid or aluminum foil. Steam fish for 15-20 minutes.
When fish is fully cooked and tender, gently transfer to platter. Ladle steaming liquid, herbs, and toasted sesame oil over fish.

title: SESAME-CRUSTED TUNA
ingredients: 2 tablespoons wasabi powder, mixed with water to form smooth paste
instructions: Season tuna with salt and pepper.
Combine sesame seeds and completely crust tuna.
Heat canola oil in sauté pan over medium heat.
Sear tuna on all sides, approximately 1-2 minutes each side.
Let tuna rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve with wasabi and dipping sauce.

title: DIPPING SAUCE
instructions: Combine all ingredients in small bowl.

title: Flounder Roulades
ingredients: 8-10 flounder filets, seasoned with salt and pepper
4 tablespoons butter, cut into small pieces
instructions: Preheat conventional oven to 375°F.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in 12-inch sauté pan. Add onions and sweat until tender. Add zucchini and cook for 1-2 more minutes.
Transfer onion/zucchini mixture to bowl and stir in breadcrumbs, capers, and tomatoes.
Place fish filets on cutting board presentation side down. Divide filling mixture into even portions among filets.
Roll filets and transfer to baking dish drizzled with remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil (seam side down).
Sprinkle butter and wine in pan along with sprigs of thyme.
Bake for 20 minutes.

title: Flounder en Papillote
ingredients: 4 ounces carrot, peeled and matchstick cut
2 tablespoons (total) stemmed and chopped fresh dill and tarragon
instructions: Preheat convection oven to 425°F.
In large bowl mix carrot, leeks, zucchini, peppers, shallots, garlic, salt, pepper, and fresh herbs.
Fold each sheet of parchment paper in half. Starting at folded edge, cut into half of heart shape (when fold is open it should be full heart shape).

Place 1 filet on one half of each of 4 parchment “hearts.” Divide vegetable mixture into 4 even portions, placing one portion atop each fish filet. Add 1 tablespoon wine or stock and 1 tablespoon olive atop each vegetable stack.

Seal papillotes by folding over edges of packet and crimping edges tight. Place each papillote on sheet pan and bake in oven for 10 minutes (depending how cold ingredients are) or until parchment is puffed and turning golden brown on top.

title: Gravad Lax
ingredients: 1-1 1/2 pound boneless salmon or Arctic Char filet
instructions: Score salmon by making two, 2-3-inch-long and 1/4 inch deep cuts into each side of fish.
Transfer salmon to shallow pan skin side down.
In bowl, mix all remaining ingredients.
Place mixture directly over salmon, packing ingredients down.

Wrap salmon tightly in plastic and refrigerate for 3 days to cure. After end of each day, drain any liquid that has been extracted from salmon. If necessary, add another cup of maple crystals and sea salt to salmon.
On last day, rinse off salmon. Color of flesh should have darkened, and texture should be firmer.

title: Brandade
ingredients: 1 pound russet potatoes, peeled and sliced 1/4-inch thick
1 pound salt cod, soaked and rinsed
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Combine potatoes, milk, and cream in large sautoir. Bring mixture to scald, and simmer until potatoes are almost tender. Add rinsed salt cod and continue to cook until potatoes and cod are completely tender. Remove from heat.
In small sauté pan, heat oil and garlic together. As soon as garlic begins to sizzle, remove from heat and set aside.
Carefully strain potatoes and fish from milk/cream mixture and transfer to stand mixer set up with paddle attachment. Retain milk/cream mixture for next step.
Add in 1 cup of milk/cream mixture, garlic/oil mixture, and lemon juice. Mix until thoroughly combined.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve on toasted baguette.

title: Stuffed Squid, Sicilian Style
ingredients: 10 medium squid, cleaned (about 1 3/4 pounds), save tentacles
Splash of white wine for deglazing
sea salt and black pepper to taste
lemon slices from 2 lemons for garnish
instructions: Remove tentacles from squid, chop finely and set aside.

In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium-high flame. When oil is hot, add peppers. Sauté until just tender, lower heat slightly, and add garlic, shrimp, and squid tentacles. Cook until shrimp is opaque. Deglaze pan with wine.
Place shrimp mixture into medium bowl. Let cool.
Add breadcrumbs, mint, olives, currants, capers and lemon juice to bowl with shrimp mixture.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.

Stuff squid bodies with mixture. Make sure not to overstuff as mixture expands during cooking. Use about 3 tablespoons per squid body. Secure squid body opening with one or two toothpicks. Place in pan large enough to accommodate squid in one even layer.
Add wine, stock, tomato puree, bay leaves and salt. Cover pan and turn heat to low.
Simmer until squid is tender, about 40-50 minutes. At 20-minute mark, turn squid over.
Remove from pan. Garnish with mint and lemon slices.

title: Fried Calamari
ingredients: Canola oil OR coconut oil for deep-frying
1 1/2 pounds clean squid, cut into 1/4 inch ringlets
instructions: In small mixing bowl, combine salt, pepper and flour.
In wok, heat oil to 350°F-375°F.
Dredge squid in flour mixture, coating well. Shake off excess flour.
Deep-fry squid in small batches until golden (this helps to maintain constant oil temperature to produce crispy squid).
Drain fried calamari on paper towels.
Sprinkle with salt; garnish with mint and lemon wedges.

title: Blackened Shrimp
ingredients: 12 large OR jumbo shrimp, shelled and deveined
instructions: In shallow pan or plate, distribute Blackening Spice Mix evenly.
Skewer shrimp, 3 to skewer.
Roll skewered shrimp in spice mix. Heat griddle grill.
Grill shrimp about 1 1/2-2 minutes on each side until shrimp is opaque.
Combine all ingredients for Blackening Spice Mix.

title: Poached Shrimp
ingredients: 25-30 pieces peeled and deveined shrimp, tails on
instructions: Bring the water to a boil and add shrimp. Poach for 4-5 minutes and remove with spider.
Cool shrimp in ice bath. Remove and place on serving platter with Cocktail Sauce.

title: Cocktail Sauce
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl, and serve with poached shrimp.

title: Mussels Simmered in White Wine
ingredients: 2 tablespoons unsalted butter OR 1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil
black pepper and sea salt to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot or 12-inch sauté pan, combine wine, onion, garlic, parsley, bay leaf, and butter OR oil. Cover and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 2-3 minutes. Add mussels, cover and cook for 3-5 minutes over high heat until mussels open. Season with salt and pepper.
Using slotted spoon, remove mussels from pot or pan. Allow cooking liquid to settle in case of sand, and then ladle liquid over mussels. Serve immediately.

title: Ginger Marinade
instructions: Combine ingredients in blender and puree.

title: Orange-Miso Barbecue Sauce
ingredients: 1/2 teaspoon Dijon mustard or more to taste
instructions: In small pot combine rice syrup, tomato paste, mustard, miso, vinegar, zest and juice. Cook for approximately 5 minutes.
In small bowl, dissolve kuzu in 2 tablespoons of water; then add enough water to make 1 cup.
Pour kuzu mixture into saucepan and heat slowly, stirring with whisk to prevent lumping. Add salt.
Simmer 5 minutes and adjust seasonings.

title: THREE-CITRUS MARINADE
instructions: Combine lime juice, lemon juice, orange juice, olive oil, garlic, black pepper, bay leaves and cilantro in blender.
Let stand 30 minutes to allow flavors to develop before using.

title: BALINESE CHILE MARINADE
instructions: Combine olive oil, water, garlic, red onion, tomato puree, ginger juice, macadamia nuts, jalapeno pepper, turmeric, red pepper flakes, salt and lime juice in blender. Process to make thin paste. Add water to thin if necessary.

title: HERB MARINADE
instructions: In small saucepan, warm all ingredients in oil and remove from heat. Cool marinade before use.

title: RED WINE MARINADE
instructions: In blender, combine wine, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, salt, pepper and thyme.

title: Curry Marinade
instructions: In 8-inch sauté pan, heat oil and fry garlic and spices until fragrant (30 to 60 seconds), taking care not to burn them.
Cool for approximately 10 minutes. Puree mixture in blender.

title: Barbecue-Style Rub
instructions: Whisk together all ingredients.
Toss food being grilled in rub (tempeh, tofu, chicken, or fish) in spice mixture. Let food marinate for 30 minutes to 1 hour before grilling.

title: Fresh Herb Marinade
instructions: In small bowl, combine all ingredients.
Let vegetables soak in marinade for 20 to 30 minutes before grilling.

title: Basic Marinade
instructions: Whisk together all ingredients.
Marinate vegetables and grill.

title: WHITE WINE MARINADE
ingredients: 1 fresh lemon sliced OR zest of 1 orange
1 heaping tablespoon chopped fresh herbs OR 1 teaspoon dried herbs
instructions: Combine ingredients. This marinade can be used for most vegetables (use half amount of oil if using for fruit).

title: ASIAN FLAVORS MARINADE
instructions: In blender, combine all ingredients.

title: BOILED NOODLES – SHOCK METHOD
yield_amount: 4 to 5 cups per 8 ounces of noodles
instructions: Bring water to boil over medium heat. Add salt if needed.
Add noodles and stir to separate them.
Cover pot. Bring to rolling boil. Add 1/2 cup cold water and stir noodles.

Bring to rolling boil again. Add 1/2 cup cold water and stir noodles. Bring to rolling boil a third time. Add 1/2 cup cold water and stir noodles. Bring to rolling boil again. Remove from heat and let stand 2 to 3 minutes. Noodles are done when they are the same color inside and out.
Drain and rinse in cold water until noodles are cooled.

title: KASHA (for loaf)
instructions: In small pot, combine kasha, salt and water.
Bring to boil, immediately turn off heat, and let sit (with lid on) until all water is absorbed (approximately 10 minutes).

title: Kasha with Egg
ingredients: 1 cup kasha, medium grain (Wolf's brand is best)
instructions: In saucepot, bring stock to boil.
In medium bowl combine kasha with egg, stirring well to combine.
Heat 10-inch sauté pan over medium-high flame and add kasha.
Using wooden spoon, stir constantly, toasting until buckwheat is dry.
Turn off heat under pan and add stock. Quickly cover to avoid sputtering.
Bring back to boil and reduce to simmer for 10 to 15 minutes until all water is absorbed.

title: Brown Basmati Rice Variation #1
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F.
In small baking dish, dry roast rice in oven until dry. Stir oil into rice and bake few minutes more, stirring occasionally.
Add water and spices to rice; cover baking dish tightly with tin foil.
Cook covered, about 50 to 60 minutes, or until all water is absorbed.

title: Brown Basmati Rice Variation #2
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F.
In small baking dish, dry roast rice in oven until dry. Stir oil into rice and bake few minutes more, stirring occasionally.
Add water, salt, and kaffir lime leaves; cover dish tightly with tin foil.
Cook covered, about 50 to 60 minutes, or until all water is absorbed.

title: Brown Basmati Rice Variation #3
ingredients: 1 stalk lemongrass, cut into 3 inch pieces and bruised
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F.
In small baking dish, dry roast rice in oven until dry. Stir oil into rice and bake few minutes more, stirring occasionally.
Add water, salt, galangal, and lemongrass to rice; cover tightly with tin foil.
Cook covered, about 50 to 60 minutes, or until all water is absorbed.

title: Wild Rice
ingredients: 1 cup wild rice (soaked in 2 cups water for 8 hours and drained)
instructions: Wash and drain rice.
In small saucepot, add rice to water. Add salt. Bring to a boil, covered, lower heat and simmer about 45 to 50 minutes or until rice is tender (rice should butterfly slightly).
Place rice in colander and drain excess water.

title: Long Grain Brown Rice
ingredients: 1 cup long grain brown rice, washed
instructions: Dry roast until rice in small pot until dry and slightly aromatic.
Add boiling water and salt to rice. Cover. Bring back to a boil. Reduce heat, and simmer for 30 minutes or until all water is absorbed. Remove pot from heat and let steam 10 to 15 minutes covered.

title: Short Grain Brown Rice
ingredients: 2 cups short grain brown rice, washed
instructions: Combine rice, water and salt in small pressure cooker.
Place top on pressure cooker and bring up to pressure.
Lower heat and cook 20 minutes. Remove pressure cooker from stove. Let pressure come down completely before attempting to open it.

title: Bulgur
instructions: Dry roast bulgur in small sauté pan.
Bring water and salt to boil in small saucepot, add bulgur, cover and bring back to a rolling boil for 1 minute.
Remove pot from heat. Let steam, covered, 45 minutes.

title: Bulgur
instructions: Dry roast bulgur in small sauté pan.
Bring water and salt to boil in small saucepot, add bulgur, cover and bring back to a rolling boil for 1 minute.
Remove pot from heat. Let steam, covered, 45 minutes.

title: Wild and Long Grain Rice Salad
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups cooked long grain rice, cooled
Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
instructions: In medium bowl, combine rice, celery, radishes, currants and walnuts.
In blender, combine dressing ingredients.
Add dressing to rice-vegetable mixture.
Add parsley and season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: Refried Soba with Vegetables
ingredients: 4 large cloves garlic, peeled and minced
4 leaves bok choy (leaves and stems), cut into chiffonade
1 bunch scallions, sliced into 1/4 inch diagonal slices
1/2 pound Soba noodles, cooked, drained, and rinsed
instructions: Heat large wok; add sesame oil, ginger and garlic. Cook 1 minute.
Add bok choy stems, then add leaves, red pepper and scallions. Cook until pepper is just tender. Add sprouts and jalapeno.
Add shoyu and toasted sesame oil. Season with salt to taste. Add noodles. Mix well and serve.

title: Kasha-Potato Loaf
ingredients: 3 medium potatoes, peeled, cut into medium dice
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
In small pot, combine kasha with boiling water with salt; cover pot. Bring back to a rolling boil; turn off heat and let sit (with lid on) for 15 minutes.

In medium pot, cover potatoes with enough water to cover by 2 inches. Bring to boil, covered. Lower heat and simmer until tender, but not mushy, about 15 minutes. Drain and mash potatoes coarsely; set aside.
In 10-inch sauté pan, caramelize onions in olive oil until dark brown, adding celery in last few minutes of cooking along with salt, thyme and sage.
Roast caraway seeds over medium heat in small sauté pan, until fragrant, about 1 minute, stirring constantly.
In medium bowl, mix kasha with potatoes, onion mixture, caraway seeds and shoyu. Season to taste with pepper and more salt (or shoyu) if desired.
Line a loaf pan with parchment paper and fill with kasha-potato mixture, packing tightly.
Bake until warmed through, about 20 minutes.
Let cool to room temperature before removing from pan. Slice and serve.

title: TABOULI
ingredients: 2 plum tomatoes, seeded and cut into small dice
2 ribs celery, cut into small dice
instructions: In large bowl, toss warm bulgur with lemon juice, olive oil, and salt. Let sit until it reaches room temperature.
Combine bulgur with tomatoes, parsley, scallions, celery, and mint, mixing thoroughly. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

title: ROASTED RED PEPPER CHICKPEA PURÉE
ingredients: 1 cup chickpeas, soaked overnight, drained, and rinsed
1 medium red bell pepper, roasted, peeled, and seeded
Sea salt and pepper to taste
1 bag pita (6 pieces), cut into triangles and oven toasted
instructions: Combine chickpeas in pressure cooker with salt, cumin, and bay leaf. Add enough water to cover by 1 inch. Cover and bring to full pressure. Lower heat and cook for 15 minutes. Release pressure by running cold water over lid and drain beans, reserving cooking liquid.
Discard bay leaf, and transfer beans to food processor.
Add garlic, olive oil, lemon juice, bell pepper, and tahini. Process until smooth, adding bean liquid as necessary to form smooth, thick puree. Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with paprika and serve with pita.

title: SPICY KIDNEY BEAN PURÉE
ingredients: 1 cup kidney beans, soaked overnight, drained, and rinsed
instructions: Combine beans in pressure cooker with salt, chili powder, and bay leaf. Add enough water to cover by 1 inch. Cover and bring to full pressure. Lower heat and cook for 15 minutes. Release pressure by running cold water over lid and drain beans, reserving cooking liquid.
Discard bay leaf, and transfer beans to food processor.
Add garlic, oil, lime juice, and cayenne. Process until smooth, adding bean liquid as necessary to form smooth, thick purée. Season with salt and pepper.
Transfer purée to bowl and fold in cilantro. Serve with corn chips.

title: GREAT NORTHERN BEAN AND ROASTED GARLIC PURÉE
ingredients: 1 cup Great Northern beans, soaked overnight, drained, and rinsed
2 heads garlic, roasted and paste extracted
Sea salt and pepper to taste
1 whole wheat baguette, sliced and oven toasted
instructions: Combine beans in pressure cooker with salt, thyme, and bay leaf. Add enough water to cover by 1 inch. Cover and bring to full pressure. Lower heat and simmer for 15 minutes. Release pressure by running cold water over lid and drain beans, reserving cooking liquid.
Discard thyme stems and bay leaf and transfer beans to food processor.
Add lemon, garlic, and olive oil. Puree until smooth, adding bean liquid as necessary to form smooth, thick purée.
Transfer to bowl season to taste and fold in parsley, dill, salt, and pepper. Serve on toasted baguette.

title: ADZUKI BEAN STEW
ingredients: 1 cup aduki beans, soaked overnight, drained and rinsed
1 pound butternut squash, peeled and cut into large dice
2 tablespoons ginger juice (3 ounces fresh ginger)
2 tablespoons tamari or more to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot on medium-low flame, heat oil. Add onion, salt, and garlic. Sweat until onions become translucent. Add aduki beans, kombu, and stock. Cover and bring to boil over high heat, reduce heat and simmer, partially covered, for 30 minutes or until beans are almost tender.
Add butternut squash, cover, and continue simmering 20 minutes or until beans, onions, and squash are tender.
Remove kombu. Add ginger juice and tamari. Season to taste.
Garnish with scallions and serve.

title: PINTO BEAN STEW
ingredients: 1 cup pinto beans, soaked overnight, drained, and rinsed
2 medium red peppers, roasted, seeded, and cut into medium dice
Sea salt and pepper to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil on medium-low flame. Add onion, salt, garlic, and cumin. Sweat until onion becomes translucent. Add beans, salt, bay leaf, chipotle, and water. Cover pot and bring to boil over high heat. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 35-40 minutes, or until beans are tender. Remove chipotle from pot, mince, and set aside.
Add peppers and tomatoes; continue simmering for 5 minutes more.
Add minced chipotle and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with cilantro and serve.

title: SMOKY GREEN SPLIT PEA SOUP
ingredients: 1 cup green split peas, soaked overnight, drained, and rinsed
6 ounces collard greens (6 leaves), stemmed, and cut into bite-sized pieces
4 ounces cauliflower (1/4 small head), cut into bite-sized florets
Sea salt and pepper, to taste
instructions: Combine water and tea in 2-quart pot. Bring to boil, cover, and remove from heat. Steep 5 minutes, strain leaves from tea, and set aside.

Heat oil in separate 1-gallon pot over medium-low flame, and add onion, carrots, parsnip, celery, garlic, and salt. Sweat vegetables, covered, stirring occasionally until they begin to soften, about 7 minutes.
Add bay leaf, split peas, and stock. Bring to boil over high heat. Reduce heat and simmer with pot partially covered until split peas are tender, about 45-60 minutes.
Remove bay leaf. Add collard leaves and cauliflower. Continue to simmer until greens and cauliflower are tender.
Remove 2 cups of soup and purée in blender until creamy. Add blended ingredients back to pot.
Add vinegar and tea, bring to simmer again, remove from heat, and season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: CURRIED RED LENTIL WITH COCONUT MILK (DAIRY)
ingredients: 4 ounces (1/4 small head) cauliflower, cut into bit-sized florets
3 tablespoons ginger juice (5 ounces fresh ginger)
Sea salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee in 3-quart pot over medium-low flame; add onion, salt, garlic, carrot, and curry powder. Sweat vegetables covered, stirring occasionally, until they begin to soften, about 10 minutes.

Add lentils, stock, and bay leaf. Cover and bring to boil over high heat. Reduce heat to simmer, and cook partially covered until lentils are broken down, approximately 30 minutes. Remove bay leaf. Add cauliflower florets and simmer until florets are tender. Remove 2 cups of soup and purée in blender until creamy. Add blended ingredients back to pot.
Add coconut milk, ginger juice, and lime juice. Stir to combine.
Season to taste with salt and pepper. Garnish with cilantro and shredded coconut.

title: CURRIED RED LENTIL SOUP WITH COCONUT MILK (VEGAN)
ingredients: 4 ounces (1/4 small head) cauliflower, cut into bit-sized florets
3 tablespoons ginger juice (5 ounces fresh ginger)
Sea salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Heat coconut oil in 3-quart pot over medium-low flame. Add onion, salt, garlic, carrot, and curry powder. Sweat vegetables covered, stirring occasionally, until they begin to soften, about 10 minutes.
Add lentils, stock, and bay leaf. Cover and bring to boil over high heat. Reduce heat to simmer, and cook partially covered until lentils are broken down, approximately 30 minutes. Remove bay leaf.
Add cauliflower florets, and simmer until tender. Remove 2 cups of soup and purée in blender until creamy. Add blended ingredients back to pot.
Add coconut milk, ginger juice, and lime juice.
Season to taste with salt and pepper. Garnish with cilantro and shredded coconut.

title: CANNELLINI BEAN SALAD
ingredients: 1 cup cannellini beans, soaked overnight, drained and rinsed
1 roasted red pepper, peeled, seeded, and cut into small dice (1/4 inch)
1/4 cup Kalamata olives, pitted and chopped
Sea salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Combine beans, water, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and bay leaf in 1-gallon pot and bring to boil over high heat.
