Lower heat and simmer, uncovered, for about 25-30 minutes or until beans are tender. Remove bay leaf and drain beans in colander.
Whisk together remaining 1/2 teaspoon of salt, black pepper, mustard, lemon juice, and olive oil. Pour over warm beans.
When beans have cooled, toss together with red peppers, red onion, olives, and basil.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.

title: BLACK-EYED PEA SALAD
ingredients: 1 cup black-eyed peas, soaked overnight, drained and rinsed
Sea salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Combine beans, water, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and kombu in 1-gallon pot and bring to boil over high heat.
Lower heat and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes or until beans are tender. Remove kombu and drain beans in colander.
Whisk together remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt, pepper, vinegar, red pepper flakes, and olive oil. Pour over warm beans.
When beans have cooled, toss together with celery, tomatoes, olives, scallions, and parsley.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: PAN-FRIED POLENTA CAKES
instructions: Pour hot polenta onto parchment-lined half-sheet tray, and spread with lightly oiled spatula to 1/2 inch thickness. Let set until firm, 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Cut into desired shapes using cookie cutters.
Spread cornmeal on plate and dredge polenta. Prepare another plate with paper towel for draining.
Heat 1/4 inch oil in sauté pan, and fry polenta cakes over medium high heat until golden and crisp. Flip once and fry other side.
Drain on paper and serve hot.

title: BROILED AND/OR BAKED POLENTA
instructions: Preheat broiler or preheat oven to 350° F.
Pour hot polenta onto parchment-lined half-sheet tray, and spread with lightly oiled spatula to 1/2 inch thickness. Let set until firm, 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Combine garlic, olive oil, and salt. Set aside.
Cut polenta into desired shapes using cookie cutters.
Transfer cut polenta to parchment-lined sheet tray for baking or lightly oiled sheet tray (no parchment) for broiling. Brush top side with garlic oil.
Broil or bake polenta until golden and crisp (approximately 2 minutes in broiler or 10-15 minutes in the oven). Flip over with spatula, brush other side with oil, and broil.
Serve hot.

title: BAKED MILLET CROQUETTES
ingredients: 1/2 cup sunflower seeds, toasted and ground in food processor
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F.
In sauce pot, dry roast millet over medium heat, stirring constantly for about 10 minutes or until it smells like popcorn.
Add water and salt to millet and bring to boil. Simmer covered 30 minutes or until water is absorbed. Transfer millet to large bowl and cool.
Add sunflower seeds, scallions, parsley and carrots to millet. Add shoyu to taste, and squeeze mixture together with hands until soft and sticky.
Using 1/4 cup measure, form croquettes and bake on parchment-lined sheet tray until golden brown, about 30 minutes.

title: PAN-FRIED MILLET CROQUETTES
instructions: In sauce pot, dry roast millet over medium heat for 5 to 10 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add water and salt, cover, and bring to boil. Simmer 40 minutes or until water is absorbed.
Transfer millet to large bowl with breadcrumbs and let cool. Squeeze grain until sticky.
Add scallions, parsley, and shoyu to millet.
Using 1/4 cup measure, form croquettes. Mold croquettes with wet hands. Dredge in cornmeal.
Heat 1/2 inch of oil in large sauté pan. Prepare plate lined with paper towel for draining.
Fry croquettes until golden brown on each side. Flip once during cooking.

title: BAKED QUINOA WITH PEAS AND HERBS
ingredients: 1 tablespoon + 1 teaspoon extra virgin olive oil
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F.
Transfer quinoa to 8 x 8 baking casserole. Roast about 20 minutes or until quinoa is dry. Add 1 tablespoon of oil and stir into quinoa. Bake 5 minutes more, stirring once or twice.

Add water and 1/2 teaspoon salt to quinoa. Tightly cover with tin foil and bake 20 minutes or until all water is absorbed. Remove from oven and let sit covered 5 minutes. Remove foil and fluff with fork. Place quinoa into bowl and let cool.
Add peas, parsley, cilantro, chives, remaining 1 teaspoon oil, and lemon juice to cooked quinoa. Season with remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt.

title: LOCRO (ARGENTINE VEGETABLE STEW)
ingredients: 1/3 pound butternut squash, peeled and large diced
1/4 pound potato, peeled and large diced
1 small carrot, peeled and large diced
1 yam OR sweet potato, peeled and large diced
1 ear corn, kernels cut from cob
instructions: Drain beans, wheat berries, and barley. In 4-quart pot, combine beans, wheat berries, and barley with 3 cups water. Bring to boil, reduce heat, and simmer, covered, for 30 to 40 minutes.

Add vegetables to simmering beans and grains. Add remaining 1 cup water, bay leaves and salt. Continue simmering for 1 hour, covered, over low heat. Stir occasionally, and add more water if needed. Vegetables should be soft; squash will disintegrate, making thick stew. Add corn and cook another 10 minutes.
Serve hot, topped with Sofrito Topping (recipe below) and chopped scallions.

title: SOFRITO TOPPING
ingredients: 1 red pepper, seeded and finely chopped
2 ripe tomatoes, seeded and finely chopped
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil. Add red pepper, tomatoes, salt, and paprika. Stir well and cook, uncovered, over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring often. Serve hot.

title: BROWN VEGETABLE STOCK
ingredients: 1 cup white beans, soaked, drained, and rinsed
1 1/2 cup lentils, soaked, drained and rinsed
6 onions (5 pounds), peeled and large dice
4 shallots (3/4 pound), peeled and sauté sliced
Bouquet garni: 6 parsley stems, 3 bay leaves, 10 peppercorns, 2 sprigs fresh thyme, 2 cloves garlic
instructions: Preheat oven to 400-425°F.
In large stockpot, combine white beans, lentils, and ten quarts water. Bring to boil, covered, skimming foam as needed. Reduce heat to simmer and continue to cook partially covered.

Mix carrots, onions, shallots, celery, and parsnips with oil. Roast on large oiled baking sheet, stirring occasionally, until browned and aromatic. Add tomatoes and garlic to vegetables in oven near end of roasting time. Add roasted vegetables to bean stockpot.
Deglaze baking sheet with white wine. Add deglaze liquid, walnuts, bouquet garni, and shoyu to bean stockpot. Simmer, partially covered, for 60-90 minutes. Strain immediately.

title: CARROT ONION STOCK
instructions: In 3-gallon stockpot, heat oil. Add vegetables and sweat.
Add water to cover (7-8 quarts) and bouquet garni. Bring to boil and skim off foam. Simmer, partially covered, for 1 - 1 1/2 hours. Strain immediately through chinois.

title: BROWN VEGETABLE STOCK
ingredients: 1/2 cup white beans, soaked, drained, and rinsed
3/4 cup lentils, soaked, drained and rinsed
2 onions (2 pounds), peeled and large dice
2 shallots (4 ounces), peeled and sauté sliced
4 plum tomatoes (1 pound), seeded and chopped
Sachet d'épices: 3 parsley stems, 2 bay leaves, 5 peppercorns, 1 sprig fresh thyme
instructions: Preheat oven to 400-425°F.
In large stockpot, combine white beans, lentils, and water. Bring to boil, covered, skimming foam as needed. Reduce heat to simmer, and continue to cook partially covered.

Mix carrots, onions, shallots, celery, and parsnips with oil. Roast on large oiled baking sheet, stirring occasionally, until browned and aromatic. Add tomatoes and garlic to vegetables in oven near end of roasting time. Add roasted vegetables to bean stockpot.
Deglaze baking sheet with red wine. Add deglaze liquid, walnuts, sachet d'épices, and shoyu to bean stockpot. Simmer, partially covered for 60-90 minutes. Strain immediately.

title: CARROT ONION STOCK
ingredients: 6 onions (5 pounds), peeled, coarsely chopped (approximately 1/2-inch pieces)
instructions: In 3-gallon stockpot, heat oil. Add vegetables and sweat.
Add water to cover (7-8 quarts) and bouquet garni. Bring to boil and skim off foam. Simmer, partially covered, for 1 - 1 1/2 hours. Strain immediately through chinois.

title: PORCINI MUSHROOM STOCK
ingredients: 1/2 pound button mushrooms, washed, coarsely chopped
1 pound onion, cut into 1/2 inch medium dice
1/2 ounce dried porcini mushrooms, soaked 20 minutes in water (reserve soaking water)
instructions: Heat oil in 1-gallon stockpot. Add fresh mushrooms and onion to cook over medium heat stirring occasionally until well browned, about 15 minutes.
Add water, garlic, porcini mushrooms, reserved soaking water, celery, carrots, bay leaves, peppercorns, parsley stems, and thyme and bring to boil, covered.
Lower heat and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Remove dried mushrooms and discard or save for another use.
Strain stock through double layer of cheesecloth. Press vegetables firmly to get out as much liquid as possible.
Measure volume of stock. Return to stove and reduce to 3 cups.

title: FISH FUMET
ingredients: 1-2 pounds bones from flat fish (no gills or skin)
6 ounces 'white mirepoix' (2 ounces each: white portion of leeks, celery, and parsnips)
1 tablespoon brown rice OR apple cider vinegar
instructions: In medium stockpot, heat oil. Add bones and mirepoix and sweat for 10 to 15 minutes.
Add 2 1/2 quarts water, bouquet garni, vinegar and wine, if using.
Bring to boil, lower heat, and simmer for 30-40 minutes.
Strain stock, pressing firmly to extract as much liquid as possible, though fine mesh strainer or cheesecloth-lined chinois.

title: QUICK SHIITAKE MUSHROOM STOCK
instructions: In small stockpot, soak mushrooms in 2 quarts water for 30 minutes.
Add kombu and bring to boil. Lower flame and simmer 5 minutes.
Strain stock through fine chinois, pressing firmly to extract as much liquid as possible.

title: CURRY STOCK
instructions: In 1-gallon stockpot, heat oil OR ghee. Add vegetables, herbs, spices, and salt.
Sweat for 15-20 minutes. Add 4 quarts filtered water.
Bring to boil, covered, lower heat and simmer 30 minutes, uncovered. Strain stock through chinois.

title: ICHIBAN DASHI
instructions: Wipe kombu with towel, if it has salty residue. Place it in pot and cover with cold water. Let stand 30 minutes.
Bring to boil, add bonito flakes and remove from heat.
Wait till bonito flakes sink to bottom of pot and strain through cheesecloth.

title: SWEET ROOT VEGETABLE STOCK
ingredients: Bouquet garni: 2 bay leaves, 8 parsley stems, 2 sprigs thyme, 8 black peppercorns
instructions: In medium stockpot, simmer ingredients for 45 minutes. Strain.

title: WHITE VEAL STOCK
ingredients: 6 quarts water or enough to cover by 2 inches
2 tablespoons lemon juice or cider vinegar
instructions: Simmer 4 to 5 hours.

title: BROWN BEEF STOCK
ingredients: 6 quarts water or enough to cover by 2 inches
1 cup white or red wine or 2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar
instructions: Brown bones and vegetables by roasting first in 450° F oven. Simmer 6 to 8 hours.

title: CHICKEN STOCK
ingredients: 2 tablespoons brown rice vinegar or cider vinegar
instructions: Simmer 1 1/2 to 4 hours.

title: SAUCE ESPAGNOLE (BROWN SAUCE)
ingredients: 4 ounces brown roux (2 ounces flour + 1.5 ounces olive oil), cooled
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil over medium-high flame. Sauté onions, carrots, and celery until golden.
Add tomatoes and sauté for few minutes longer or until slightly caramelized.
Add 7 cups stock and bring to boil. Lower heat to simmer.
Slowly whisk remaining cup of warmed stock with cooled roux.
Slowly add roux-stock mixture back into sauce.
Simmer uncovered about 30 minutes or until reduced by half and nappé is achieved.
Strain through chinois. Adjust seasoning to taste.

title: VEGETARIAN BORDELAISE
ingredients: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart saucepan, heat oil over medium flame. Sweat shallots, carrots, celery, and onions until vegetables are softened.
Add peppercorns, thyme sprig, and red wine. Increase heat to medium-high and reduce wine until almost dry (au sec).
Add parsley sprigs and Sauce Espagnole.
Bring sauce to boil and skim. Reduce heat, and simmer, uncovered, for 20 to 25 minutes, or until flavor has rounded and nappé consistency is achieved.
Strain sauce through chinois. Adjust seasoning to taste.

title: MUSHROOM SAUCE
ingredients: 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided
6 ounces brown roux (3 ounces flour + 2 1/4 ounces olive oil), cooled
Sea salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium-high flame. Sauté onions, carrots and celery until golden.
Add tomatoes and sauté for few minutes or until slightly caramelized.
Add 6 cups stock and bring to boil. Lower heat to simmer.
Slowly whisk remaining 1 cup of stock to cooled roux.
Slowly add roux-stock mixture back into sauce.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat remaining 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat, add mushrooms and sweat until tender.
Add mushrooms to pot and simmer uncovered, about 30 minutes, or until reduced by half and nappé consistency is achieved.
Strain through chinois and return to pot.
Adjust seasoning to taste.

title: APPLE-MUSTARD SAUCE
ingredients: 1 pound apples (2 medium), peeled, cored, thinly sliced
Sachet d'Epices (3 bay leaves, 6 parsley stems, 5 sprigs thyme, 6 black peppercorns tied in cheesecloth)
4 ounces blond roux (3 ounces flour + 2 ounces oil), cooled
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium-high flame. Sauté shallots, onion, carrots, and apples until lightly caramelized.
Deglaze pan with apple juice and vinegar. Reduce liquid until almost dry (au sec). Do not remove vegetables.
Transfer vegetables to 1-gallon pot. Add mustard, 7 cups of warm brown stock, and sachet to pot. Bring to simmer.
In medium bowl, add 1 cup of remaining stock to roux. Whisk to combine.
Return stock and roux mixture to pot. Simmer 15 to 20 minutes until sauce thickens and nappé consistency is achieved.
Strain sauce through chinois. Adjust seasonings to taste.

title: ROASTED GARLIC SAUCE
ingredients: 2 heads garlic, feathery outer layers removed
2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided
Sachet d'Epices (3 bay leaves, 6 parsley stems, 5 sprigs thyme, 6 black peppercorns tied in cheesecloth)
2 ounces blond roux (1 ounce flour + 1 ounce oil), cooled
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
instructions: Roast garlic (wrapped in foil) in 400°F oven for approximately 35 minutes. When cool, squeeze out garlic paste from skin. Blend with small amount of stock until consistency is creamy.
In 1-gallon pot, over medium-high flame, heat remaining tablespoon oil. Sauté onions, carrots, and celery until lightly caramelized.
Deglaze over high heat with red wine and brown rice vinegar. Reduce liquid until almost dry (au sec).
Add 3 1/2 cups stock, sachet d'epices, and mushrooms. Cover sauce and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered 15 to 20 minutes. Skim occasionally. Discard sachet.
In small bowl, whisk remaining 1/2 cup stock to roux. Add to stock pot and simmer 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain sauce through chinois and return sauce to pot.
Add garlic paste mixture to pot to and simmer another 5 minutes or until nappé consistency is achieved. Adjust seasoning to taste.

title: Tomato Sauce
ingredients: 1 tablespoon agave syrup or more to taste
Sea salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
instructions: Strain whole tomatoes from juice. Reserve juice and set aside. Blend whole tomatoes in food processor.
In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil over low flame. Add garlic and salt. Sweat until garlic softens (do not burn garlic).
Raise heat to medium-high.
Add pureed whole tomatoes to pan. Sauté tomatoes for 12-15 minutes.
Lower heat, add agave, red pepper flakes, and reserved tomato juice. Simmer until sauce thickens, about 10 minutes.
Add basil leaves. Simmer 2-3 minutes more. Adjust seasoning to taste with salt and pepper.

title: Creole Sauce
ingredients: 4 ounces scallions (1/2 bunch), trimmed and minced
5 ounces green pepper (1 small), seeded and minced
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Strain whole tomatoes from juice. Reserve juice and set aside. Blend whole tomatoes in food processor.

In 12-inch sauté pan, heat oil over low heat. Add garlic and salt. Sweat until garlic softens (do not burn garlic). Add scallions, celery, red pepper flakes, and pepper. Sweat until vegetables are tender.
Raise heat to medium-high and add blended tomatoes to pan. Sauté until tomatoes begin to caramelize.
Lower heat and add agave and reserved tomato juice. Simmer until sauce thickens, about 10 minutes.
Adjust seasoning to taste.

title: Bolognese Sauce with Chicken
ingredients: 1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil, divided
Sea salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat 3 tablespoons of oil over medium flame. Add ground chicken and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring often to prevent any scorching. Remove chicken from pan.
Add remaining tablespoon of oil over medium-high flame.
Add onion and carrots. Sauté until golden.
Lower flame to medium, add garlic, salt, fennel, oregano, and tomato paste. Sweat until all vegetables are tender.
Add ground chicken back to pot and mix to incorporate. Continue to sweat few minutes longer.
In Vitamix, blend whole tomatoes and juices.
Add puree to pot and simmer until thickened to desired consistency.
Add rice syrup and basil. Simmer few minutes more. Adjust seasoning to taste.

title: Classic Béchamel
ingredients: Sea salt and ground white pepper to taste
instructions: Add onion piqué to milk in 2 1/2 quart saucepan and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes, stirring often to avoid scorching.
Remove piqué onion from pot. Set milk aside to cool.
In 10-inch sauté pan, combine flour and butter. Stir with wooden spoon for 2-3 minutes. Let roux cool.
Slowly and gradually whisk 4 cups cooled milk into warm roux.
Simmer sauce for approximately 20-25 minutes until thickened and reduced to nappé consistency.
Strain sauce through fine chinois. Add additional 1 cup milk on low flame, if needed.

title: Mornay Sauce
ingredients: Sea salt and white pepper to taste
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart sauce pan, over low flame, add onion piqué to milk. Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring often to avoid scorching.
Remove piqué onion from pot. Set milk aside to cool.
In 10-inch sauté pan, combine flour and butter. Stir with wooden spoon for 2-3 minutes. Let roux cool.
Slowly and gradually whisk cooled milk into warm roux.
Simmer sauce for approximately 20-30 minutes until thickened and reduced to nappé consistency.
Strain sauce through fine chinois.
Add cheese and whisk until cheese is totally melted and incorporated into sauce.

title: Velouté
ingredients: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 12-inch sauté pan, melt butter over medium-low flame. Sprinkle in flour and stir for about 3-4 minutes.
Slowly whisk in stock 1/2 cup at a time, waiting for sauce to thicken between each addition, until sauce is smooth.
Lower heat and simmer about 35-40 minutes or until sauce achieves nappé consistency.
Adjust seasonings to taste.

title: Sauce Supreme
ingredients: 2 cups Velouté Sauce (reserved from recipe above)
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat Velouté Sauce over low flame and simmer 2-3 minutes or until warmed through.
Slowly whisk in heavy cream, and mix until incorporated.
Adjust seasoning to taste.

title: Vegan Béchamel
ingredients: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart sauce pan, heat oil over medium flame. Sweat shallots until translucent.
Add flour and cook over low heat, stirring constantly for about 5 minutes.
Slowly whisk in almond milk with thyme, bay leaf, and nutmeg.
Increase heat to simmer and continue cooking for about 20-25 minutes, stirring often until sauce thickens.
Strain sauce through chinois. Add lemon juice. Season to taste.

title: FRESH ALMOND MILK
instructions: In 2-gallon pot, bring water to boil, pour over blanched almonds and let sit covered for 20-30 minutes.
Transfer almonds and water to Vitamix. Blend until well homogenized.
Pour almond mixture in batches through sieve or chinois lined with double layer of rinsed cheesecloth, wringing and squeezing cheesecloth to extract as much liquid from pulp as possible.

title: PESTO (CILANTRO-PUMPKIN SEED)
instructions: Prepare an ice bath. In 2 1/2 quart pot, bring 2 cups of water to boil. Add cilantro and pinch of salt. Blanch cilantro 2-3 seconds. Shock cilantro in ice bath, drain, and squeeze out excess moisture. Set aside.
Combine and puree pumpkin seeds, garlic, cilantro, umeboshi paste, and miso in food processor.
Through feed tube, add oil slowly and puree until well combined (20-30 seconds).

title: SWEET CORN SAUCE WITH BUTTER
instructions: Remove corn kernels from cob.
Puree corn kernels in VitaMix until smooth.
Transfer puree to strainer lined with moistened double layer of cheesecloth. Squeeze 'corn milk' through cheesecloth.
Transfer 'corn milk' to 2 1/2 quart pot, and simmer for 5-10 minutes, stirring constantly, until mixture begins to thicken. Whisk in butter. Sauce should have nappé consistency.
Whisk in lemon juice. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: SWEET CORN SAUCE
instructions: Remove corn kernels from cob.
Puree corn kernels in VitaMix until smooth.
Transfer puree to strainer lined with moistened double layer of cheesecloth. Squeeze 'corn milk' through cheesecloth.
Transfer 'corn milk' to 2 1/2 quart pot, and simmer for 5-10 minutes, stirring constantly, until desired nappé consistency is achieved.
Whisk in lemon juice, and season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: MUSHROOM SAUCE
ingredients: 8 ounces cremini mushrooms, stemmed and thinly sliced
Sea salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
2 scallions (1 ounce), trimmed and thinly sliced on bias
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil on medium-high flame. Sauté onion until caramelized. Add mushrooms with salt and continue to sauté a few minutes more, until mushrooms are caramelized.
In small bowl, dissolve kuzu in stock and tamari. Whisk kuzu slurry into onion-mushroom mixture.
Bring sauce to boil, and reduce flame to medium. Simmer partially covered 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens, or until desired nappé consistency is achieved. Whisk in vinegar.
Season to taste with salt and pepper. Garnish with scallions.

title: YELLOW PEPPER COULIS WITH BUTTER
ingredients: 10 ounces yellow pepper (2 medium), medium dice
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat butter over medium-low flame. Sweat shallots until translucent.
Add peppers and sweat until softened.
Add vinegar and reduce until almost dry (au sec).
Add stock and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
Puree in blender until creamy and return to pan. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: YELLOW PEPPER COULIS WITH OLIVE OIL
ingredients: 10 ounces yellow pepper (2 medium), medium dice
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium flame. Sweat shallots until translucent.
Add peppers and sweat until softened.
Add vinegar and reduce until almost dry (au sec).
Add stock and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
Puree in blender until creamy and return to pan. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: Yellow Pepper Coulis with Butter
ingredients: 10 ounces yellow pepper (2 medium), medium dice
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat butter over medium-low flame. Sweat shallots until translucent.
Add peppers and sweat until softened.
Add vinegar and reduce until almost dry (au sec).
Add stock and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
Puree in blender until creamy and return to pan. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: Yellow Pepper Coulis with Olive Oil
ingredients: 10 ounces yellow pepper (2 medium), medium dice
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium flame. Sweat shallots until translucent.
Add peppers and sweat until softened.
Add vinegar and reduce until almost dry (au sec).
Add stock and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
Puree in blender until creamy and return to pan. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: Roasted Red Pepper Coulis
ingredients: 1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil, divided
2-3 tablespoons vegetable stock, or more as needed
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
Dash hot sauce, or more to taste
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F.

Arrange peppers on half sheet pan, and roast in oven until blistered, about 20-30 minutes. Transfer peppers to covered, small bowl to steam for 10 minutes. When peppers are cool, remove skins and seeds.
In 8-inch sauté pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over a medium flame and sweat onions with garlic until onions are translucent.
Combine peppers with onion-garlic mixture in VitaMix and blend until creamy. Add remaining 2 tablespoons oil through feed tube, slowly while machine is running.
Add warm vegetable stock as needed for desired consistency. Add lemon juice to taste. Adjust seasonings with salt, pepper, and hot sauce.

title: Beurre Blanc
ingredients: 1/2 pound unsalted butter (2 sticks), chilled, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
instructions: In 8-inch sauté pan over medium flame, reduce vinegar with shallots until almost dry (au sec).
Add heavy cream to pan and reduce until mixture thickens slightly. Reduce heat to low.
Add butter, whisking in a few pieces at a time.
When all butter is incorporated, season to taste.
Strain sauce through chinois. Re-heat carefully over very low flame.

title: Fresh Curry Sauce
ingredients: 2 teaspoons whole cumin seed, lightly toasted
1 teaspoon whole coriander seeds, lightly toasted
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine coconut milk with cumin seeds, coriander seeds, cloves, cardamom pods, shallots, jalapeno, turmeric, and salt. Simmer on low flame 15-20 minutes or until sauce is thickened to nappé consistency. Do not allow sauce to boil at any time.
Strain vegetables and spices from sauce, and return sauce to pot.
Add ginger and lime juices. Season to taste.

title: Almond Sauce
ingredients: 5 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided
1/3 cup blanched almonds, soaked 10 minutes in warm water and strained
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F.

Peel off papery outer layers from garlic head, cut off top third of head, rub cut surface with 1 tablespoon olive oil, and wrap garlic in aluminum foil. Roast garlic for 45-60 minutes or until cloves are soft. When cool enough to handle, extract paste from garlic head and set aside.
In 8-inch sauté pan, over medium-high flame, reduce white wine with shallots until almost dry (au sec).
In blender combine shallots, roasted garlic paste, almonds and vegetable stock. Through feed tube, slowly add remaining 1/4 cup of olive oil. Blend until creamy. Season to taste.

title: Carrot-Beet Sauce
ingredients: 2 pounds carrots (8 medium), peeled and cut into large chunks
2 ounces beets, scrubbed, peeled, and cut into chunks
1/4 teaspoon fennel seed, toasted and ground
1 tablespoon plus 1 teaspoon red miso, dissolved in 1/4 cup liquid
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Combine carrots and beets with water to cover in a medium pressure cooker and cook until vegetables are tender, about 15-20 minutes. Drain vegetables and let cool. Reserve cooking liquid.
In 1-gallon sauce pan, heat oil over medium flame, sweat onion with salt and garlic until onions are translucent. Add oregano, basil, and fennel. Continue to sweat a few minutes longer.
Puree carrots and beets in blender with half of onion-garlic mixture, 2 cups reserved cooking liquid, miso, umeboshi paste, and balsamic vinegar.
Return sauce to sauce pan and add remaining onions and garlic. Add remaining 1/2 cup cooking liquid to achieve proper consistency, if needed.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.

title: Grilled Seitan
ingredients: 12-ounce package seitan, cut into six 2-ounce slabs (reserve liquid from package)
instructions: Combine seitan in medium bowl with liquid from package, cumin, paprika, garlic powder, olive oil, and salt. Marinate for 30 minutes.
Heat cast iron griddle. Grill seitan on each side until grill marks form. Return seitan to bowl with marinade until ready to serve. Serve with Chimichurri Sauce.

title: Chimichurri Sauce
ingredients: 3 ounces parsley, stemmed, washed, and spun dry
instructions: Roughly chop parsley and transfer to food processor.
Add garlic, onion, vinegar, lemon juice, and oregano to food processor with parsley and pulse to combine.
Add olive oil through feed tube and continue to process for 30 seconds or until sauce is blended, but still retains some texture. Season to taste.

title: Baked Tofu
ingredients: 14 ounces firm tofu (1 block), cut into 4 slabs, pressed for 30 minutes
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F.
Slice each slab of tofu into 16 cubes.
In a small baking vessel, combine tofu with cumin, paprika, garlic powder, apple juice, tamari, and oil.
Transfer to oven and bake 15 - 20 minutes. Remove from oven and serve with Romesco Sauce.

title: ROMESCO SAUCE
ingredients: 6 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided
1/4 cup hazelnuts, toasted, skins rubbed off
2 teaspoons sherry vinegar or to taste
1/2 teaspoon sea salt or to taste
instructions: Combine tomatoes in small bowl and set aside.

Arrange peppers on stove top, over direct flame, turning often as peppers become blackened and blistered. Transfer peppers to small bowl and cover. Allow peppers steam for 10 minutes. When peppers are cool, remove skins and seeds.
Heat cast iron skillet over medium flame. Toast chili each side until flexible. Do not scorch.
Remove seeds and transfer chili to 2 1/2 quart pot with water to cover. Simmer until chili softens, about 5 minutes. Drain chilis and set them aside, reserving soaking water.

In 10-inch sauté pan, heat 2 tablespoons oil over low flame, add garlic, and sweat until garlic has softened. Add tomatoes and peppers. Increase heat to medium-high and sauté until vegetables begin to caramelize.
In food processor, combine tomatoes, chili, hazelnuts, almonds, vinegar, and remaining 1/4 cup of oil. Blend until smooth. If sauce is too thick, add chili soaking water to processor to thin sauce.

title: PAN-FRIED TEMPEH
instructions: Heat oven to 350° F.
In a small baking vessel, combine tempeh with fennel, cumin, apple juice, and tamari.
Place in oven and bake 15 – 20 minutes.
Remove tempeh from oven and cool to room temperature.
Prepare a half sheet pan with paper towels and set aside.
In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil. Pan-fry tempeh slices until golden on each side. Transfer to paper-lined half sheet pan to drain.
Serve with Chermoula Sauce.

title: CHERMOULA SAUCE
ingredients: 1 cup loosely packed, stemmed cilantro (1 ounce)
2 cups loosely packed, stemmed parsley (1 3/4 ounces)
instructions: In VitaMix, combine all ingredients until well blended.

title: SWEET AND SOUR MANGO BARBEQUE SAUCE
ingredients: 1 small jalapeno, seeded, ribs removed, minced
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil on medium flame. Add onions, salt, chili powder, paprika, and cumin. Sweat until onions become translucent.
Add garlic and jalapeno and continue sweating until all vegetables are tender.
Reduce flame to low and add coffee and coconut sugar to pot and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a blender, combine the Worcestershire sauce, mangos, water and orange juice. Blend until creamy.
Add blended mixture to pot along with ketchup and maple syrup.
Simmer sauce 10-15 minutes or to desired consistency. Season to taste.

title: CLASSIC BARBEQUE SAUCE
ingredients: 1/2 cup water or more as needed
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine all ingredients and simmer on medium-low heat, stirring often, until nappé consistency is achieved.

title: TERIYAKI SAUCE
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, simmer all ingredients until desired consistency (nappé) is achieved.

title: TOFU SOUR CREAM
ingredients: 1/4 teaspoon sea salt or to taste
2 scallions, white part only, roughly chopped
instructions: Process all ingredients in food processor until smooth and creamy. Season to taste.

title: SHIITAKE BROTH WITH SHRIMP, SOBA, AND BABY BOK CHOY
ingredients: 1/2 pound baby bok choy, stems and leaves separated, both thinly sliced
6 ounces shrimp, tails removed and cleaned
3 ounces soba noodles, boiled, rinsed in cold water, and drained in a colander
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil over medium flame. Sweat onions, carrots, garlic, and peppercorns with salt until onions are translucent.
Add stock, star anise, chilies, lemongrass, kombu, dried shiitakes, and tamari. Cover pot and simmer for 25 minutes.
Remove and discard star anise, chilies, and lemongrass. Remove kombu and shiitakes from pot. Slice kombu and shiitakes (removing shiitake stems first) into very thin strips and add back to soup pot.
Add bok choy stems. Simmer until tender. Add bok choy leaves, shrimp, and ginger juice. Simmer until bok choy leaves wilt and shrimp just turns opaque.
Add lime juice and season to taste.
Place small portion of noodles in bowl. Pour soup over noodles and serve.

title: 3-BEAN CHILI WITH CASHEW SOUR CREAM
ingredients: 6 ounces carrot (1 medium carrot), large dice
2/3 cup pinto beans, soaked overnight, drained and rinsed
2/3 cup black beans, soaked overnight, drained and rinsed
2/3 cup kidney beans, soaked overnight, drained and rinsed
14 ounce can whole tomatoes, chopped (reserve juice from can)
1/3 cup frozen corn or 1-2 ears fresh corn in season, kernels removed
instructions: In tall, 2-gallon pressure cooker, heat oil over medium flame. Add onion, salt, chili powder, cumin, oregano, tomato paste, and garlic. When ingredients start to sizzle, immediately add carrots and avocado leaves. Sweat vegetables for 10-15 minutes, with pressure cooker lid slightly ajar, stirring occasionally.
Add pinto beans, black beans, kidney beans, and vegetable stock. Secure lid of pressure cooker, bring cooker to pressure, reduce heat, and cook for about 30 minutes.

Bring pressure down by running cold water on lid of cooker. Open pressure cooker and add tomatoes, reserved tomato juice, and corn. Simmer uncovered until chili thickens to desired consistency. Remove avocado leaves.
Add lime juice to taste. Garnish with lime rounds, cilantro, scallions, and Cashew Sour Cream.

title: CASHEW SOUR CREAM
ingredients: 1 cup cashews, soaked 4 hours to overnight (discard soaking water)
1/2 teaspoon or more sea salt to taste
instructions: Combine all ingredients in food processor until smooth and creamy. Season to taste.

title: DARK ONION SOUP WITH WHOLE GRAIN CROUTONS
ingredients: 6 cups Brown Stock (saved from Stock Practicum)
1 tablespoon rice vinegar or to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil over medium flame and sweat onions with salt until translucent. Lower heat, and continue to sweat until onions begin to soften and color, stirring often to prevent burning. Continue until onions are caramelized (brown and soft), about 30-40 minutes. Add black pepper, sherry, and garlic and continue cooking until sherry reduces and garlic softens.
Add thyme sprigs, bay leaf, and brown stock. Simmer 20-25 minutes.
Season to taste with tamari and vinegar.

title: WHOLE GRAIN CROUTONS
ingredients: 4 ounces (about 1/4 loaf) good quality whole grain bread, crust removed, medium dice
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F. Line half-sheet with parchment paper.
Toss bread in bowl with oil, thyme, salt, and pepper. Transfer croutons to sheet tray.
Bake until bread is golden in color and crisp in texture. Serve over soup.

title: BUTTERNUT MINESTRONE
ingredients: 8 ounces leeks (2 stalks), 1/2 inch half-moons, rinsed well
1 cup white beans, soaked overnight, drained and rinsed
1 pound butternut squash, peeled, medium dice
1 teaspoon red wine vinegar, or more to taste
Sea salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil over medium flame. Sweat onions with salt and red pepper flakes until onions are translucent. Add leeks, carrots, celery, and garlic. Continue sweating until vegetables begin to soften, about 5-7 minutes.
Add white beans, tomatoes, stock, rosemary, and sage to pot with vegetables. Simmer with lid ajar, until beans become just about tender, about 40 minutes.
Add butternut squash to pot and continue to simmer until squash begins to break down and soup thickens.
Add green beans and cook until tender, 4-5 minutes.
When beans are tender, season soup with vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Garnish soup with parsley and parmesan (if using).

title: CREAM OF MUSHROOM SOUP WITH ROUX
ingredients: Salt and pinch freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil on medium flame. Sweat shallots and mushrooms with salt until tender, about 10-12 minutes. Add sherry, reducing until almost dry. Add flour, and stir 5 to 7 minutes.
Add stock, 1 cup at a time, whisking to blend. Raise heat and bring soup to boil. Reduce heat, and simmer, with lid ajar, for 30 minutes.
Puree soup in blender until creamy.
Return soup to pot. Re-heat and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Garnish with parsley.

title: CREAM OF CURRIED SWEET POTATO SOUP
ingredients: 2 pounds sweet potatoes (2 large), medium dice
2 limes, sliced into 7 thin rounds, for garnish
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat coconut oil over medium flame. Add onion, salt, and curry powder. Sweat until onions are translucent.
Add sweet potatoes and stock to pot. Raise heat bring and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer with lid ajar until potatoes are tender.
Puree soup in blender until creamy.
Return soup to pot. Re-heat and whisk in coconut milk. Season to taste with salt and lime juice.
Garnish with lime rounds.

title: CREAM OF BROCCOLI SOUP
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
2 tablespoons chopped dill (1/2 ounce), for garnish
instructions: Peel and slice broccoli stems. Separate broccoli florets into bite-size pieces. Set aside.
In 1-gallon pot, heat oil over medium flame. Add onion and 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Sweat until onions are translucent, about 10 minutes. Stir often to prevent browning.
Add stock, rice, and cashews to pot. Raise heat and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer until rice is tender, about 15 minutes. Set aside.

In separate 1-gallon pot, bring 2 quarts water to boil with remaining salt. Prepare ice bath. Blanch broccoli until tender, about 2 minutes. Drain broccoli and transfer immediately to ice bath. Drain broccoli again.
Combine onion-rice and broccoli in Vitamix. Blend until creamy.
Return soup to pot. Re-heat and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Garnish with dill.

title: CREAM OF CARROT SOUP WITH POTATO
ingredients: 4 ounces potato (1/2 medium), peeled, medium dice
2 tablespoons chopped dill (1/2 ounce), for garnish
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil over medium flame. Add onions and salt. Sweat until onions are softened (about 5-8 minutes). Stir often to prevent browning.
Add carrots, cover pot, and sweat over low heat for 5-6 minutes. Stir to prevent browning.
Add stock and potatoes to pot. Raise heat and bring soup to boil. Reduce heat and simmer with lid ajar, 25 minutes, or until carrots and potatoes are very tender.
Blend soup until creamy, adding additional stock to achieve desired consistency.
Return soup to pot. Re-heat and season to taste with lemon and ginger juices.
Garnish with dill.

title: CREAM OF CARROT SOUP WITH OATS
ingredients: 2 tablespoons chopped dill (1/2 ounce) for garnish
instructions: In 1-gallon pot, heat oil over medium flame. Add onions and salt. Sweat until onions are softened, about 5-8 minutes. Stir often to prevent browning.
Add carrots, cover pot, and sweat over low heat for 5-6 minutes. Stir to prevent browning.
Add stock and oats to pot. Raise heat and bring soup to boil. Reduce heat and simmer with lid ajar 25 minutes, or until carrots are very tender.
Blend soup until creamy, adding additional stock to achieve desired consistency.
Return soup to pot. Re-heat and season to taste with lemon and ginger juices.
Garnish with dill.

title: SCRAMBLED EGGS
instructions: Collect equipment and food items: stainless steel bowl, whisk, spatula, sauté pan, eggs, salt, pepper, milk or cream, and butter.
Break eggs into stainless steel bowl and beat until well blended. Season with salt and white pepper. Do not use aluminum, which may discolor eggs.

If desired, add small amount of milk or cream, about 1 to 1 1/2 tablespoons for 2 eggs, or 8 to 12 ounces per quart of eggs. Too much liquid may make cooked eggs watery and dilutes flavor. Heavy cream adds richness.
Heat butter in small sauté pan.
When fat is just hot enough to make drop of water sizzle, pour in eggs.

Cook over low heat, stirring gently with spatula from time to time as eggs coagulate. Lift portions of coagulated egg so that uncooked egg can run underneath. Too much stirring breaks up eggs into very small particles. Do not let eggs brown. Keep heat low.
When eggs are set but still soft and moist, remove from heat. Turn out onto plate or into steam table pan.

title: HANDMADE GARLIC MAYONNAISE
ingredients: 1/3-1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil (Troy brand Turkish olive oil OR Spectrum brand Californian olive oil)
instructions: Smash garlic with salt to paste on cutting board.
Put garlic paste in bowl together with egg yolks and mustard; whisk until smooth.
Slowly add oil (no more than one teaspoon at a time) to egg yolk mixture, whisking until well incorporated before adding more oil.
As mixture begins to thicken, oil can be added somewhat more rapidly (no more than one tablespoon at a time).
When all oil has been incorporated, whisk in lemon juice.

title: BLENDER MAYONNAISE
instructions: Combine all ingredients except oil in blender and process until completely smooth.
With motor running, add oil very slowly, in steady stream. As mixture starts to thicken, oil can be added a bit more quickly.

title: PROCEDURE FOR EGG-THICKENED SAUCES - HOLLANDAISE
ingredients: 1/2 pound (8 ounces) clarified butter, melted and warm
white or cayenne pepper to taste
instructions: Using round-bottomed, straight-sided bowl over bain marie (double boiler), whisk yolks with lemon juice, salt and pepper until thick and creamy – until whisk leaves tracks in yolks.
Have butter warm but not hot. Add butter slowly, drop by drop first, whisking continuously.
Do not add more than egg yolks can hold. Add lemon juice, salt, and pepper to taste.
If sauce breaks, rescue by adding 1 teaspoon of cold water and beating vigorously. If this does not work, whisk 2 egg yolks into broken sauce as you would butter.
If necessary, thin with few drops of warm water.

title: Hollandaise
ingredients: 1/2 pound (8 ounces) clarified butter, melted and warm
white or cayenne pepper to taste
instructions: Using round-bottomed, straight-sided bowl over bain marie (double boiler), whisk yolks with lemon juice, salt and pepper until thick and creamy – until whisk leaves tracks in yolks.
Have butter warm but not hot. Add butter slowly, drop by drop first, whisking continuously.
Do not add more than egg yolks can hold. Add lemon juice, salt, and pepper to taste.
If sauce breaks, rescue by adding 1 teaspoon of cold water and beating vigorously. If this does not work, whisk 2 egg yolks into broken sauce as you would butter.
If necessary, thin with few drops of warm water.

title: Bernaise
ingredients: 1/2 teaspoon white peppercorn crushed in saucepan
instructions: Reduce all ingredients at simmer to 1-2 tablespoons.
Cool reduction and strain before adding to yolks in Hollandaise recipe (above).

title: Baked Meringue Shells
instructions: Preheat oven to 225°F and line sheet tray with parchment paper.
Beat egg whites to soft peaks, then add sweetener one tablespoon at a time until incorporated and meringue is stiff.
Using pastry bag or spoon, pipe or spoon meringue on sheet tray. Bake until crisp (minimum 2 hours).
Cool shells completely and remove from parchment.

title: Baked Custard (Crème Caramel)
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F.
In large bowl, combine eggs, maple crystals, salt and vanilla.
In small saucepan, scald milk (heat to just below simmering), then slowly whisk into egg mixture.
Strain mixture through chinois and skim foam from surface.
Set 6 ramekins in water bath reaching halfway up their sides. Add enough caramel* to cover bottom of ramekin. Carefully pour in custard.
Bake approximately 60-90 minutes. Water should be almost simmering as custard cooks. Do not let it boil as this makes the custard grainy.

To test for doneness, insert thin-bladed knife about an inch or two from the center. Custard is done if knife comes out clean. Center may not be completely set, but it will continue to cook in its own heat after removal from the oven.
Chill completely and un-mold right before service.

title: Crème Anglaise (Stirred Custard)
instructions: Heat cream or milk and vanilla to scalding (just below simmering) in double boiler.

In medium bowl, beat together egg yolks and maple crystals. Slowly pour hot cream/milk into egg mixture while whisking constantly (this raises the temperature of the eggs gradually and helps prevent curdling).

Heat mixture slowly in double boiler, stirring constantly to prevent curdling. Sauce is cooked when it lightly coats the back of a spoon instead of running off like milk, or when it reaches 185°F. Never let the temperatures go above 190°F.
When done, remove sauce from heat. Cool sauce by setting pan or bowl in ice water. Stir occasionally to cool evenly.

title: CHOCOLATE SOUFFLÉ
ingredients: 5 tablespoons unsalted butter (1 tablespoon softened, remaining 4 tablespoons cut into 1/2-inch chunks)
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F. Butter inside of soufflé dish (or dishes) with the one tablespoon of softened butter.
Dust inside of each dish with cocoa powder.
Refrigerate dishes until ready to fill.
Melt chocolate and remaining 4 tablespoons butter in medium bowl set over pot of simmering water. Turn off heat, stir in salt and vanilla and set aside.
In separate medium bowl, beat egg yolks and maple crystals with whisk or hand mixer until thick and yellow (about 3 minutes). Pour into chocolate mixture.
In separate medium bowl or electric stand mixer, combine egg whites and cream of tartar. By hand or mixer, whip egg whites until stiff, moist peaks are formed.
Vigorously stir one-quarter of whites into chocolate-egg yolk mixture.
Gently fold in remaining egg whites in 4 equal increments.
Transfer to ramekins and bake as follows: 1/2 cup dishes: 12 to 14 minutes, 2 cup dishes: 20 to 25 minutes, 1 quart dishes: 30 - 35 minutes, 2 quart dishes: 50 to 55 minutes.
Serve immediately.

title: ROASTED DULSE CONDIMENT
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Place dulse on half sheet pan with parchment and bake for 10 minutes.
Combine toasted sesame seeds with roasted dulse in suribachi and grind until most of seeds are broken.
Serve as a condiment over cooked millet.
Store excess in air-tight glass container.

title: NORI CONDIMENT
ingredients: 7 sheets Toasted Nori, torn into tiny pieces
instructions: In 2-quart saucepan, add nori, water, shoyu, and rice syrup.
Bring to boil, reduce to simmer, and stir often. Let simmer until all the liquid is absorbed.
Serve as condiment on grains or beans.

title: TOASTED NORI
instructions: With tongs, wave nori back and forth over flame several times, until nori turns brighter shade of green. Pass nori through flame carefully and quickly.

title: WAKAME CUCUMBER SALAD WITH ORANGE
ingredients: 1/4 cup wakame (loosely packed), soaked 10 minutes in water to cover and drained
2 juice oranges, supremed (plus juice extracted from squeezing membrane after supreme)
instructions: In strainer set over bowl, mix cucumber with sea salt. Press with weighted plate until liquid is expressed.
Chop wakame coarsely and combine with cucumbers, oranges, and cilantro in medium bowl.
In separate small bowl, combine dressing: rice vinegar, mirin, shoyu, and maple syrup.
Toss salad with dressing. Chill 20 minutes.

title: HIZIKI CAVIAR
ingredients: 1/2 cup hiziki (loosely packed), soaked 10 minutes in water to cover and drained
2 tablespoons grated ginger, squeezed to make juice
toasted baguette (1/4-inch slices), pita, toast points, or crackers
instructions: Sauté hiziki in medium pan with 2 tablespoons sesame oil for 3 minutes. Add water to cover, bring to boil, add shoyu and cook uncovered until all liquid is evaporated, about 15 minutes.
Sauté shallots and garlic in separate medium sauté pan with remaining 1 tablespoon sesame oil for 3 minutes.
Finely chop the hiziki while shallots and garlic are sautéing. Combine hiziki with cooked shallot-garlic mixture in small bowl.
Season with additional sea salt, if needed. Add lemon juice and ginger juice to taste. Serve on bread, pita, toast points, or crackers. Garnish with tofu sour cream.

title: TOFU SOUR CREAM
ingredients: 14 ounces (1 block) soft tofu, blanched for 1-2 minutes
instructions: Puree tofu, lemon juice, salt, olive oil, and water in food processor until smooth and creamy.
Add dill or chives and process for 30 seconds more. Chill for 20 minutes.

title: KANTEN
instructions: Soak agar flakes in juice for 10 minutes in 2 1/2 quart saucepan.
Bring mixture to boil. Watch carefully to prevent liquid from boiling over. Reduce heat to simmer and cook until agar is completely dissolved, whisking frequently.
Place sliced strawberries in an 8-inch square baking dish.
Slowly pour agar and juice mixture over berries. Set aside until no steam is rising from mixture.
Place in refrigerator until firmed up, about 45 minutes. Can either be served cut into cubes or blended in food processor for 'pudding.'

title: SEA PALM, WAKAME AND DULSE SEAWEED SALAD WITH CREAMY CARROT DRESSING
ingredients: 1 cup loosely packed sea palm (1 ounce), soaked in cold water for 10 minutes, drained, chopped, set aside
1/4 cup loosely packed wakame (.03 ounces), soaked in cold water to cover for 10 minutes, drained, chopped, set aside
1/2 cup loosely packed dulse (.04 ounces), soaked in cold water to cover for 1 minute, drained, chopped, set aside
1 pound cauliflower (1/2 head), florets, blanched, shocked
1 head romaine lettuce, washed, spun, chopped
1/4 pound roasted carrots, reserved from salad
3 tablespoons ginger juice (3 ounce piece of ginger)
1/3 cup + 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
instructions: Heat oven to 400°F. Toss carrots with olive oil, garlic, and rosemary. Transfer carrots to parchment-lined half sheet tray. Roast carrots until tender (about 20-30 minutes). Set aside to cool, reserving 1/4 pound carrots for dressing.
In 2 1/2 quart pot, bring sea palm, with enough water to cover, and shoyu to boil. Lower flame and simmer 20 minutes uncovered. Drain sea palm and set aside.
Transfer reserved 1/4 pound roasted carrots to Vitamix. Add rice vinegar, ginger juice, salt, olive oil, and water. Blend until very smooth, adding more water as necessary.
Arrange lettuce on plates. Arrange cauliflower, remaining roasted carrots, sea palm, wakame, and dulse on top of lettuce. Drizzle carrot dressing over salad. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds.

title: WAKAME, SEITAN, AND TOASTED WALNUT CASSEROLE
ingredients: 1 cup loosely packed wakame (1 ounce), rinsed, soaked in 1/4 cup water for 10 minutes, drained, coarsely chopped
1 1/2 cups walnuts, lightly toasted, coarsely chopped
6 ounces whole wheat elbow pasta, cooked al dente
Freshly ground cracked black pepper taste
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F.

In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oils over medium flame and sweat onions with salt until translucent. Lower heat and continue to sweat until onions begin to soften and color, stirring often to prevent burning. Continue until onions are caramelized (brown and soft), about 20 minutes. Add mushrooms and cook until tender.
Add wakame and seitan. Stir well. Add water and shoyu. Cover pan and bring to boil. Lower flame and remove lid. Simmer until mixture is almost dry, about 15-20 minutes.
In small bowl, whisk tahini, shoyu, and water. Set aside.
Transfer cooked pasta to large bowl and combine with sauté mixture, walnuts, and tahini sauce. Transfer mixture to lightly oiled Dutch oven.
Combine breadcrumbs, olive oil, fresh thyme, and black pepper in small bowl. Top casserole with mixture. Bake 20 minutes.

title: NORI ROLLS
ingredients: 2 cups short grain brown rice, soaked overnight, drained, rinsed
1 tablespoon wasabi powder, mixed with water to make spreadable paste
8 ounces carrot (1 medium), julienne, blanched, shocked
1 tablespoon ginger juice (1 ounce piece of ginger)
instructions: Combine rice, water, and pinch of salt in small pressure cooker. Bring pot to full pressure, reduce heat, and cook at low pressure for 35 minutes. Turn off heat, remove pressure cooker from stove, and steam rice, covered, for 15 minutes.
Transfer rice from pot to large bowl. While rice is still hot, add seasoning mixture and stir with spatula. Cover bowl with damp towel and allow rice to rest for 10 minutes.

Place nori sheet on near edge of sushi mat, shiny side down. Have small bowl of water nearby to moisten fingers. Transfer 3/4 cup of rice to nori sheet and spread evenly with wet fingertips, leaving two-inch strip at far edge of nori bare.

Using chopstick or spoon, spread separate, thin, horizontal lines of wasabi and umeboshi paste across the rice at near end of nori sheet. Arrange slices of avocado, carrot, and scallions lengthwise on top of paste.

Bring near edge of nori and mat up and snugly over arranged vegetables, in rolling motion. Continue rolling forward, withdrawing mat as you go. Moisten strip of nori at far end of roll with wet fingertips. Continue to roll forward to seal roll.

title: HIZIKI WITH CARROTS, ONIONS, AND AGÉ TOFU
ingredients: 2 cups canola or coconut oil for frying
7 ounces (1/2 block) firm tofu, pressed, cut into 1/2 inch slabs, then 1/2 inch strips
1/3 cup loosely packed hiziki (.05 ounces), soaked 30 minutes in water to cover, drained, set aside
1 tablespoon white sesame seeds, lightly toasted
instructions: Heat oil to 375°F in 2 1/2 quart pot. Line plate with paper towel. Fry tofu in batches until golden. Drain on paper towel.
Combine hiziki in separate 2 1/2 quart pot with apple juice and water. Bring to boil. Add carrots, onion, fried tofu, toasted sesame oil, and shoyu. Simmer 30 minutes or until all liquid is absorbed.
Garnish with sesame seeds before serving.

title: ARAME WITH VEGETABLES
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups arame, loosely packed (1.5 ounces), soaked in water to cover for 10 minutes, drained
8 ounces bok choy stems and leaves, chiffonade
instructions: In 10-inch sauté pan, heat oil over medium flame. Sweat onion for 5 minutes or until translucent. Add carrot and sweat another 5 minutes.
Add arame to onions and carrots. Stir well. Sweat for another minute until arame is heated through.
Add water to cover arame halfway. Bring water to boil. Add shoyu, brown rice syrup, and mirin. Simmer for 20-30 minutes or until all liquid has evaporated.
Add bok choy stems and sweat until stems just become tender. Add leaves and sweat until leaves wilt.

title: CUCUMBER-DULSE SALAD
instructions: Score skin of cucumbers along length with peeler or zester. Slice thinly into rounds and sprinkle with salt. Set aside for 30 minutes. Rinse, if too salty.
Quickly rinse dulse in bowl of cold water, until softened. Squeeze excess liquid out. Finely chop dulse.
Combine dulse and cucumbers in medium bowl. Mix well.
In small bowl, whisk, rice vinegar, garlic, and mustard. Slowly drizzle in olive oil and whisk to combine. Pour dressing over vegetables. Let marinate 15-30 minutes.

title: STIR-FRIED ARAME AND GINGER WITH SOBA NOODLES
ingredients: 2 ounces soba noodles, broken in half
1/2 cup arame, loosely packed (.05 ounces), soaked 10 minutes in water to cover, drained
4 scallions (1/2 bunch), trimmed, thinly sliced on the diagonal
instructions: Boil soba until tender, about 8-10 minutes. Drain, rinse in cold water, and set aside.
Coarsely chop arame. Set aside.
Heat wok with both sesame oils. Add ginger and stir well. Add garlic. Cook until ginger and garlic are lightly golden, but avoid burning.
Stir in arame, peppers, and Chinese cabbage. Once cabbage wilts, add soba.
Mix water, tamari, umeboshi vinegar, and maple syrup in small bowl. Pour over vegetables and noodles. Stir until liquid has evaporated.
Add scallions and stir to combine.

title: ARAME STRUDEL
ingredients: 4 ounces arame, soaked 15 minutes and drained
1 medium size onion, sliced into salad slice
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. In large sauté pan, sauté arame in 2-3 tablespoons sesame oil for 3 to 5 minutes. Add water to pan (arame should be three-quarters covered), and bring to boil. Reduce heat to simmer and cook until liquid is evaporated. Stir in shoyu, brown rice syrup, and mirin. Reduce until arame is dry. Add ginger juice. Set aside.
In another medium pan, sauté carrots, onion, and sea salt in 1 tablespoon sesame oil until carrots and onions are tender, approximately 10-12 minutes. Set aside.

On a sheet of parchment paper, stack 3 sheets of phyllo, brushing each individual layer lightly with remaining sesame oil. Spoon 1-inch wide strip of arame mixture and carrot mixture horizontally onto near end of phyllo. Using the parchment paper, firmly roll strudel forward into a log. Score rolled log diagonally at 2-inch intervals across its length. Repeat this process using remaining phyllo, arame, and carrot/onion mixture until all mixture and phyllo is used up.
Transfer strudels to sheet pan; brush their tops with sesame oil, and bake approximately 20 minutes, until deeply golden. When cool, slice strudel into individual servings.

title: COFFEE CUSTARD
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine milk and agar. Soak agar 5 minutes.
Bring mixture to boil, lower heat, and simmer uncovered for about 5 minutes or more, until agar is completely dissolved.
Add maple syrup and stir until combined.
Dissolve kuzu and coffee in water until there are no lumps. Add to milk mixture and simmer until slightly thickened, about 5 minutes.
Lightly oil ramekins and sprinkle 2 teaspoons maple crystals in bottom of each ramekin.
Pour custard into ramekins. Refrigerate to set.
When custard is set, run paring knife around side of each ramekin to separate custard. Invert custard onto plate. Garnish with pecans.

title: COCONUT-LIME FLAN
instructions: In 2 1/2 quart pot, combine coconut milk and agar. Soak agar for 5 minutes.
Bring mixture to boil. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for about 5 minutes or more, until agar is completely dissolved.
Add maple syrup and stir until combined.
Dissolve kuzu in lime juice and water until there are no lumps. Add to milk mixture and simmer until slightly thickened, about 5 minutes.
Lightly oil ramekins and sprinkle 2 teaspoons maple crystals in bottom of each ramekin.
Pour custard into ramekins. Refrigerate to set.
When custard is set, run paring knife around the side of each ramekin to separate custard. Invert custard onto plate. Garnish with coconut.
